1. Create priority scoring function combining multiple metrics
2. Generate recommendations comparing current vs suggested priority
3. CLI: --robot-priority outputs priority-ranked JSON with explanations
4. TUI: 'Suggested' badge/indicator on items

## Scoring Function
impact_score = (
    0.3 * normalized_pagerank +
    0.3 * normalized_betweenness +
    0.2 * blocker_count / max_blocker_count +
    0.1 * freshness_penalty +
    0.1 * explicit_priority_boost
)

title: Context Packs & Recipes System
description:
Implement named workflow presets ('recipes') that bundle filters, layouts, and exports into one-command operations. This dramatically simplifies common workflows for both humans and AI agents.

## Background & Motivation
Users repeatedly perform the same sequences: 'Show me blocked high-priority items' or 'Export a release changelog'. Currently this requires remembering multiple flags. Recipes encode these patterns.

## Value Proposition
- For Humans: 'bv --recipe triage' replaces 'bv --status open,blocked --priority 0,1 --sort priority'
- For AI Agents: Recipes provide semantic entry points - 'triage mode' vs 'release mode' vs 'dependency analysis mode'

## Technical Approach
1. Define recipe schema (YAML)
2. Embed sensible default recipes
3. Allow user-defined recipes in .bv/recipes.yaml
4. CLI: bv --recipe <name> or bv -r <name>
5. TUI: Recipe picker with descriptions

## Built-in Recipes
- triage: Open/blocked issues, sorted by priority
- release-cut: Recently closed issues for changelog
- blocked-review: All blocked items with blocker info
- dependency-risk: High betweenness/PageRank items
- quick-wins: Low-priority, no-dependency items
- stale: Items not updated in 14+ days

title: audit: classify bt JSONL persistence state (v1.0.1 opt-in migration)
description:
## Problem

beads v1.0.1 made JSONL export opt-in (was mandatory in v0.x). bt's JSONL persistence state has not been audited since upgrade. Needs classification per the `dotfiles-jlp` decision tree.

## Context

Child of cross-project epic **`dotfiles-jlp`** вҖ” audit JSONL persistence across all beads-tracked projects after the v1.0.1 opt-in shift.

Per dotfiles-5xa audit findings (2026-04-21): bt's JSONL tracking state is UNTESTED. Need per-project verification.

## Proposed Fix

1. `cd ~/System/tools/bt`
2. Run verification:
   ```bash
   bd config get export.auto
   bd config get export.path
   git ls-files | grep -F '.beads/issues.jsonl' || echo "not tracked"
   git ls-remote origin 'refs/dolt/data' 2>/dev/null && echo "Dolt synced via git origin" || echo "no refs/dolt/data"
   bd --version
   bd doctor
   ```
3. Classify per `dotfiles-jlp` decision tree (Case A / B / C / D)
4. Execute migration per classification:
   - **Case A (redundant)**: `bd config set export.auto false` + `git rm --cached .beads/issues.jsonl` + commit
   - **Case B (JSONL-only)**: `bd dolt push` to seed `refs/dolt/data`, then Case A treatment
   - **Case C (neither)**: no action or enable Dolt sync if cross-machine work needed
   - **Case D (multi-clone)**: coordinate via `bd dolt push`/`pull`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Case classified and recorded as a comment on this bead
- [ ] Migration executed per decision tree
- [ ] `bd doctor` clean post-migration
- [ ] Parent `dotfiles-jlp` checklist updated with completion status for bt

## Scope Guards

- Do NOT modify `.beads/issues.jsonl` outside the migration action (git-rm or disable export)
- Do NOT conflate with dotfiles-5xa remote plumbing вҖ” that's a prerequisite, not scope

## Relationships

- Parent: `dotfiles-jlp` (cross-project epic)
- Depends on: `dotfiles-5xa` Phase 4 for bt (only if bt lacks `refs/dolt/data`)

title: feature: hydrate created_by_session / claimed_by_session from Dolt (bd-34v)
description:
## Problem

bd-34v Phase 1a (shipping today, upstream PR Fixes #3400) adds `created_by_session` column to beads schema. Phase 1b (follow-up PR pending signature changes) adds `claimed_by_session`. These are first-class columns. bt's Dolt reader must hydrate them for display in bt-2cvx and filtering in bt-ssk7.

## Context

Research 2026-04-21 + bd-34v upstream PR:

- `created_by_session`: immutable on create, set from `--session` flag or `CLAUDE_SESSION_ID` env
- `claimed_by_session`: overwrites on every re-claim (last-writer-wins)
- `closed_by_session`: existing column, unchanged
- All three stored as `VARCHAR(255) DEFAULT ''` (nullable via default)

bt's compact output surface (bt-2cvx) and global federation (bt-ssk7) already plan to display session provenance. This bead ensures the data pipeline delivers session_id from Dolt to the rendering layer.

## Proposed Fix

**Phase 1 (after bd-34v Phase 1a merges upstream):**
- Update Dolt reader (`internal/dolt/`) to hydrate `created_by_session` into issue model struct
- Add to robot JSON output (--robot)
- Wire into bt-2cvx detail pane rendering (separate from `created_by` which is actor dim)

**Phase 2 (after bd-34v Phase 1b merges):**
- Hydrate `claimed_by_session`
- Extend render logic

**Both phases:**
- Null-safe handling for beads created before session columns existed (empty string is fine)
- Cross-project queries (global mode) preserve session context

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Reader tests verify session_id fields populate from Dolt
- [ ] bt detail pane shows session columns when populated (null/empty handled gracefully)
- [ ] Robot JSON output includes all three session fields
- [ ] Cross-project queries preserve session context (ties into bt-ssk7 federation)
- [ ] Regression test: fresh install + reading pre-migration beads without session columns doesn't error

## Scope Guards

- DO NOT conflate session_id with BD_ACTOR вҖ” orthogonal dimensions per Gas Town identity model
- DO NOT assume both columns land together вҖ” Phase 1a is `created_by_session` only; Phase 1b comes later
- DO NOT implement display logic here вҖ” that's bt-2cvx's scope; this bead is data-pipeline only

## Relationships

- Epic parent: bt-ushd
- Depends on: bd-34v Phase 1a (for `created_by_session`), bd-34v Phase 1b (for `claimed_by_session`)
- Related: bt-2cvx (display layer), bt-ssk7 (federation layer)
- Paired: bd-34v вҶ” bt-5hl9 (schema landing вҶ” bt hydration)

title: decision: map molecule lifecycle (Formula/Mol/Digest) to bt status + views
description:
## Problem

Gas Town molecule lifecycle (Formula вҶ’ cook вҶ’ Proto вҶ’ pour вҶ’ Mol/wisp вҶ’ squash/burn вҶ’ Digest) is a core beads abstraction upstream, but bt's status enum doesn't reflect it. IN_PROGRESS beads may be in any of several lifecycle sub-states (cooking, poured, active, stalled, squashing) with different meanings вҖ” agents using molecules via `bd mol current` don't see their lifecycle position in bt.

## Context

Research 2026-04-21 from `gastown-docs-concepts-molecules.md` + `gastown-docs-reference.md`:

- Molecules have checklist-driven step progression
- `bd mol current` shows "where am I" with step-by-step progress
- `bd close <step> --continue` auto-advances and marks next step in_progress
- Wisps are ephemeral molecules (patrol cycles) вҖ” don't accumulate history
- Molecules are persistent deliverables вҖ” audit trail matters

Best practice from upstream docs (quoted verbatim):
> CRITICAL: Close steps in real-time. Mark in_progress BEFORE starting, closed IMMEDIATELY after completing. Never batch-close steps at the end. Molecules ARE the ledger вҖ” each step closure is a timestamped CV entry.

bt currently treats all IN_PROGRESS beads identically. Molecule-step beads and standalone beads render the same.

## Proposed Decision Points

**1. Should bt surface IN_PROGRESS sub-states?**
- Option A: No вҖ” keep status enum flat; rely on bt-8zgy staleness for differentiation
- Option B: Yes вҖ” extend renderer to show lifecycle icon when bead is part of a molecule
- Option C: Hybrid вҖ” show sub-state only in detail pane of a molecule-step bead

**2. Should bt render molecule progress (like `bd mol current`) in-TUI?**
- Option A: No вҖ” let bd CLI handle it
- Option B: Progress widget in detail pane when viewing a molecule-step bead
- Option C: Dedicated "active molecules" dashboard view

**3. How do we distinguish molecule-step vs wisp-step vs standalone beads visually?**

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Decision documented with rationale for each of the 3 points above
- [ ] Impacts on bt-oiaj (TUI read/write) captured
- [ ] Impacts on bt-8zgy (IN_PROGRESS freshness) captured вҖ” lifecycle sub-states may refine staleness model
- [ ] If opting for visual treatment: mockup in Charm v2

## Scope Guards

- This is a DECISION bead, not implementation. Document + decide before building.
- DO NOT implement molecule rendering ad-hoc across views вҖ” needs consistent treatment

## Relationships

- Related: bt-8zgy (freshness model interacts), bt-oiaj (TUI create/edit), bt-ushd (cross-project OS)
- Upstream source: `gastown-docs-concepts-molecules.md` (vault)

title: feature: integrate bd audit --actor and bd stats --group-by=actor into TUI + robot CLI
description:
## Problem

Upstream beads v1.0.1 ships `bd audit --actor=<path>` and `bd stats --group-by=actor` as first-class audit primitives. bt's TUI insights view + robot CLI don't surface these queries, so agents inspecting cross-project actor workload must shell out to bd directly per project.

## Context

Research corpus (2026-04-21, vault at `.obs/sms/02.projects/02.11_dev/beads/resources/`) confirms these as upstream-stable primitives. The BD_ACTOR format `<rig>/<role>/<name>` enables hierarchical scoping: individual actors, all polecats, all of a rig, cross-rig wildcards.

## Proposed Fix

Wrap upstream commands in bt surfaces (don't reimplement):

**TUI additions**:
- Actor-scoped metric panel in insights view (stale by actor, issues owned, cycle time, revision count)
- Actor picker with hierarchy awareness (rig вҶ’ role вҶ’ name), matching BD_ACTOR hierarchy for filter UI

**Robot CLI additions**:
- `bt --robot-stats --group-by=actor` (optionally `--global`)
- `bt --robot-audit --actor=<path>` (optionally `--global`, `--since=`, `--until=`)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] TUI insights view has at least one actor-scoped metric panel
- [ ] Robot CLI `--group-by=actor` stats work per-project
- [ ] `--global` mode aggregates across projects using routes.jsonl-style routing (see bt-ghbl)
- [ ] Output shape matches upstream `bd audit` / `bd stats` JSON for consistency
- [ ] Help text documents the BD_ACTOR format

## Scope Guards

- DO NOT reimplement audit logic in bt вҖ” wrap upstream commands, handle aggregation at bt layer
- DO NOT invent a new actor format вҖ” use BD_ACTOR `<rig>/<role>/<name>` as-is

## Relationships

- Epic parent: bt-ushd
- Related: bt-lt2h (human CLI), bt-4jyd (global audit), bt-ghbl (cross-project routing)

## Research source

Session brief: `C:\Users\sms\.obs\sms\02.projects\02.11_dev\beads\sessions\04212026\research-corpus-clip-and-beads-propagation.md`

title: bt doctor: health-check subcommand for bt's integration points (Dolt, bd, blurb, env, project state)
description:
## Why

bt has many integration points that can silently go wrong: Dolt server reachability, schema compatibility, data dir layout, `bd` on PATH, `BT_*` env vars, search index consistency, agent-blurb version, `.beads/` presence. When something is off, users see obscure errors or bt simply appearing to do nothing вҖ” no single command to ask "am I set up correctly?"

Adjacent tools already have this:
- `bd doctor` вҖ” upstream beads health + schema checks
- `cass doctor` вҖ” cass installation + index rebuild

bt is the odd one out. Filing as a feature request (not an audit вҖ” the command literally doesn't exist today).

## What

New subcommand `bt doctor` that checks bt's own integration points and emits a diagnostic report. Separate from `bd doctor` (which owns Dolt/schema/database side); `bt doctor` layers on top and checks **bt's integration with bd + environment + project state**.

### Checks to run (v1)

**Environment**
- `bd` binary on PATH + version compatible with bt (what's the minimum bd version bt needs?)
- `BT_*` env vars set and sensible (`BT_OUTPUT_FORMAT`, `BT_OUTPUT_SHAPE`, `BT_SEARCH_MODE`, `BT_SEARCH_PRESET`, `BT_NO_BROWSER`, `BT_TEST_MODE`)
- Dolt server running + reachable (via bd's dolt status, not reimplementing)
- Dolt schema version matches what bt can read (hooks into bt-uc6k audit territory вҖ” if new upstream columns land and bt hasn't caught up, doctor should warn)

**Project state**
- `.beads/` directory present and not empty
- `.bt/` data dir exists (or confirm bt doesn't use one locally in this project)
- `.beads/conventions/reference.md` and `.beads/conventions/labels.md` present (fail soft вҖ” warn if missing, they're for agents not runtime)
- `AGENTS.md` or `CLAUDE.md` present in project root; if present, report bt-agent-blurb status: none / v1 (stale) / v2 (current) вҖ” ties to bt-yqgn

**bt internals**
- Search index (`.bt/search/` or wherever) consistent with source data; mtime sanity
- Workspace config `.bt/workspace.yaml` parseable if present
- Any known-stale caches

### Output modes

- Default: human-readable, grouped by severity (pass / warn / fail)
- `--robot` / `--json`: machine-readable envelope matching the existing `RobotEnvelope` convention (`schema: "doctor.v1"`)
- `-v` / `--verbose`: include passed checks (noisy but complete picture)

### Fix mode (`--fix`)

Opt-in auto-repair of **safe** conditions only:
- Rebuild search index
- Regenerate agent blurb (v1 вҶ’ v2 upgrade; depends on bt-yqgn landing first)
- Clear stale caches
- Never modifies beads data, git state, or Dolt contents

Explicitly NOT fixed by `--fix`:
- Missing `bd` binary (user action)
- Dolt server not running (bd owns this вҖ” defer to `bd dolt start`)
- Schema mismatches (user decides whether to upgrade)
- Project-state issues (user decides)

## Scope

- In: new `bt doctor` subcommand, JSON envelope, `--fix` for safe repairs, tests
- Out: replacing `bd doctor` вҖ” bt's doctor delegates Dolt/schema/database checks to bd
- Out: auto-upgrading bd or Dolt (user-driven)
- Out: a TUI view for doctor output (command-line only in v1; could feed into a TUI panel later)
- Out: cross-project doctor checks (global-mode doctor is a future concern)

## Acceptance Criteria

- `bt doctor` runs without arguments and exits 0 if all checks pass, non-zero otherwise
- Each check produces a structured result: name, severity (pass/warn/fail), message, suggested fix (if any)
- `bt doctor --robot` emits a `RobotEnvelope` with `schema: "doctor.v1"` and a `checks` array
- `bt doctor --fix` only runs safe repairs and reports what it did; prints a dry-run-style summary first
- Unit tests cover each check's pass and fail paths; integration tests against a real project layout
- Documented in `AGENTS.md` and referenced from `bd prime` output where appropriate

## Steps to Reproduce

Not a bug вҖ” feature request. Motivation is the discovery (2026-04-20) that bt has no health-check surface despite having many silent-failure modes.

## Relates

- `bt-yqgn` вҖ” pkg/agents blurb v1 вҶ’ v2 rewrite. `bt doctor` consumes the blurb-version detector from that bead.
- `bt-uc6k` (closed) вҖ” schema drift audit; doctor should detect drift and warn.
- `bt-wjzk` вҖ” periodic schema drift detection; doctor is the user-initiated surface for that.
- `bd doctor` (upstream) вҖ” bt doctor delegates Dolt/schema questions to it rather than reimplementing. Investigate whether `bd doctor --json` can be parsed and embedded in `bt doctor`'s output.

## Notes

- Consider whether doctor should also surface a quick "session health" panel вҖ” what's `$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID`, what beads has this session opened/closed (ties to bt-ldq4). Probably out of v1 scope but worth thinking about as a future extension.
- `bt doctor` is a natural agent surface вҖ” agents running in a project should run `bt doctor --robot` early to catch misconfig before burning time on confused commands.
- Exit code convention: 0 = all pass, 1 = any warn, 2 = any fail (so scripts can gate on severity).

title: Rewrite pkg/agents blurb: v1 -> v2 (post-rename, post-robot-subcommand-surface, drop deprecated commands)
description:
## Why

`pkg/agents/blurb.go` contains the canonical instruction text that bt injects into a project's `AGENTS.md` / `CLAUDE.md` on first-run (the self-install mechanism). It was last written for beads_viewer (`bv`) and has drifted significantly since the fork takeover and rename to bt. Any fresh project that runs bt today and accepts the blurb install gets outdated instructions вҖ” agents working in that project see wrong commands, stale protocol, missing surfaces.

Audit done 2026-04-20 during the root-CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md consolidation.

## What's wrong (audit findings)

### Content that's outright incorrect

- `bd sync` referenced 4 times вҖ” deprecated; current commands are `bd dolt push` + `git push`
- Issue types list: `task, bug, feature, epic, question, docs` вҖ” actual enum is `bug, feature, task, epic, chore` (no `question`, no `docs`; missing Gastown types like `spike`, `gate`, `human`, `event` where relevant)
- `bd create --title="..."` вҖ” title is a positional arg, not a flag: `bd create "Title" -t task -p 2 ...`
- `bd update <id> --status=in_progress` вҖ” current atomic idiom is `bd update <id> --claim`
- `bd close <id> --reason="Completed"` вҖ” close template required per `.beads/conventions/reference.md`: Summary / Change / Files / Verify / Risk / Notes
- Session protocol promoted: `git add; bd sync; git commit; bd sync; git push` вҖ” actual current: `git pull --rebase && bd dolt push && git push`
- Priority meanings generic (`P0=critical, P1=high`) вҖ” project defines specific thresholds (P0: data loss or blocks all work, P1: core command/view broken, etc.)

### Missing surfaces that matter for agents today

- `bt robot` subcommand family вҖ” the primary agent surface (list, triage, next, portfolio, pairs, refs, diff, plan, impact, insights, blocker-chain, impact-network, causality, related, orphans, search, suggest, drift, alerts, priority). None of this is mentioned.
- `--global` mode for cross-project work
- `--compact` / `--full` / `--shape` persistent flags shipped in bt-mhwy.1
- Session provenance: `$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID` env var, `--session` flag on `bd update` / `bd close`, `metadata.created_by_session` bridge
- Mandatory reads: `.beads/conventions/reference.md`, `.beads/conventions/labels.md`, `AGENTS.md`
- `bd prime` as the onboarding entrypoint for full workflow context

### Stale naming in the blurb internals

- Start marker: `<!-- bv-agent-instructions-v1 -->`
- End marker: `<!-- end-bv-agent-instructions -->`
- `blurbVersionRegex = <!-- bv-agent-instructions-v(\d+) -->`

All three carry the `bv-` prefix from the pre-rename era. Legacy match patterns (`LegacyBlurbPatterns`, `legacyBlurbStartPattern`) correctly target the real old `--robot-insights` / `--robot-plan` single-dash blurb content вҖ” keep those as-is for the upgrade-path detection.

## What (implementation scope)

Bump the blurb from v1 to v2 with a clean naming migration:

1. Rewrite `AgentBlurb` content end-to-end for current bt command surface (robot subcommands primary, CLI secondary, session provenance, current close template, current session-close protocol)
2. Bump `BlurbVersion = 2`
3. New markers: `<!-- bt-agent-instructions-v2 -->` / `<!-- end-bt-agent-instructions -->`
4. Update `blurbVersionRegex` to match the new `bt-` marker
5. Extend `GetBlurbVersion` / `NeedsUpdate` / `RemoveBlurb` to recognize BOTH `bv-` (v1) and `bt-` (v2+) markers during the transition window вҖ” so existing v1 installs out in the wild upgrade cleanly
6. Keep `LegacyBlurbPatterns` and `legacyBlurbStartPattern` untouched вҖ” those target the pre-v1 format, not pre-rename
7. Update tests in `blurb_test.go` to exercise the v1 вҶ’ v2 upgrade path specifically
8. Add a regression guard test: assert `bd sync` never appears in `AgentBlurb` (or any other explicitly-deprecated command we decide to blacklist)

## Scope

- In: content rewrite, version bump, marker rename with backward-compat detection, tests
- Out: changing the install UX (prompt, TTY guard, prefs file) вҖ” separate concern
- Out: detecting and mass-updating remote projects that already have v1 installed вҖ” users upgrade locally when they run bt in those projects next

## Acceptance Criteria

- `AgentBlurb` reflects 2026-era command surface (robot subcommands, global mode, compact shape, session provenance, current close template + session protocol)
- `BlurbVersion == 2`; new markers use `bt-` prefix; old `bv-` markers still recognized for upgrade
- A project with v1 blurb installed upgrades to v2 on next bt run (integration test covers this path)
- A fresh project gets v2 directly
- Regression test asserts no `bd sync` or other deprecated commands leak back into the blurb
- All `pkg/agents/*_test.go` still pass

## Steps to Reproduce

Not a bug вҖ” audit task. Reproduction is reading the current `pkg/agents/blurb.go` and comparing against current `AGENTS.md`, `.beads/conventions/reference.md`, and the shipped robot subcommand surface.

## Relates

- `bt-mhwy.1` (shipped) вҖ” compact output mode; blurb should mention it as the agent-facing default
- `bt-mhwy.4` / `bt-mhwy.2` / `bt-mhwy.5` (shipped) вҖ” robot portfolio, pairs, external dep resolution; blurb should mention these as primary agent surfaces
- `bt-xavk` (help system redesign, P1) вҖ” user-facing help content audit; this bead is the programmatic equivalent for the self-install path
- `bd-34v` (upstream beads) вҖ” first-class `created_by_session` column; if it lands before this bead is implemented, blurb should mention the flag; otherwise document the metadata bridge

## Notes

- Blurb version bump is a breaking format change from the user's perspective вҖ” expected and fine, `NeedsUpdate` logic handles it.
- Consider whether the blurb should reference specific beads (the `bt-mhwy` epic, for example, as a live pointer to the cross-project intelligence layer work). Lean against: beads churn; the blurb should reference stable surfaces only.
- Pre-rename `bv-` prefix has been in the marker for months. If anyone has v1 installed, their blurb won't conflict with the new v2 install because `UpdateBlurb` already calls `RemoveBlurb` (which looks for `<!-- bv-agent-instructions-v` prefix) before appending the new one. For the transition, RemoveBlurb needs to match BOTH old and new prefixes вҖ” small fix to make sure the clean-slate path doesn't leave orphan v1 markers when the new `bt-` content lands.

title: TUI: surface IN_PROGRESS freshness (stalled vs active) in list + details pane
description:
## Why

The IN_PROGRESS status is binary in the TUI but "actively being worked on" and "claimed 3 weeks ago and forgotten" are very different states. `bt-if3w` is the dogfood example: status IN_PROGRESS, actual last update 1 week ago. The list row shows the "1w ago" update timestamp next to the title вҖ” easy to miss вҖ” and the details pane buries the activity signal entirely. Visually, `bt-if3w` is indistinguishable from something claimed this morning.

This matters because:
- Quick scan of the list should surface "which claimed things are actually moving?"
- Agents reading their own `bd list --status=in_progress` output should be able to tell which work is live vs abandoned without opening each
- Stale-IN_PROGRESS is a coordination risk (someone else might assume it's owned; it effectively isn't)

The data exists: the alerts panel already flags beads as "inactive for N days" (see the alert pane in the dogfood screenshot вҖ” `bt-ushd`, `bt-t82t`, `bt-s4b7`, `bt-53du`, `bt-dcby`, `bt-ssk7`, `bt-0cht`, `bt-xavk` all surfaced). Same computation needs to feed the status chip + detail pane.

## What

Composite freshness signal on IN_PROGRESS (and optionally OPEN) beads surfaced in:

### 1. List row status chip

Today the status column shows `OPEN`, `PROG`, `DEFER`, etc. Proposal: for `IN_PROGRESS`, append a freshness hint when stale:

```
PROG       bt-if3w  Refactor pkg/ui monolith + Charm v2 migration ...   1w ago
PROGВ·7d    bt-if3w  Refactor pkg/ui monolith + Charm v2 migration ...   1w ago
```

Or color-code: green = fresh (<24h), yellow = recent (<7d), red = stale (>=7d). Don't add a new column вҖ” reuse the existing status cell.

### 2. Details pane: promote last-activity

Today the Details pane header looks like:

```
ID       Status       Priority   Assignee   Created
bt-if3w  IN_PROGRESS  P2         @sms       2026-04-08 10:21
```

Updated date is not shown. Proposal: add a "Last activity" field (relative + absolute), and when status is IN_PROGRESS and last activity is stale, show a derived `[stalled 7d]` tag next to the Status value:

```
ID       Status                     Priority   Assignee   Last activity            Created
bt-if3w  IN_PROGRESS [stalled 7d]   P2         @sms       2026-04-13 (7d ago)      2026-04-08
```

### 3. Activity heartbeat (optional, later)

A small sub-section in the detail pane showing the last N events that touched this bead: updates, comments, claim events. The "Activity" timeline. Requires richer event history than just `updated_at` + comments вҖ” out of scope v1 unless the data is already trivially available.

## Scope

### In (v1)

- Freshness classification rule: `updated_at` vs now, tiered (fresh / recent / stale) with configurable thresholds
- Reuse the existing alerts staleness computation (don't redefine the rule)
- List-row status chip composition or color-coding
- Details-pane "Last activity" field + stalled-tag next to Status

### Out (v1)

- Changing the underlying status enum вҖ” IN_PROGRESS stays IN_PROGRESS; freshness is a *display* dimension layered on top
- Event-history timeline (activity heartbeat) вҖ” deferred to v2 unless trivial
- Automatic status transitions (e.g., auto-deferring stale IN_PROGRESS) вҖ” separate policy discussion
- Changing the list sort order вҖ” separate concern

## Acceptance Criteria

- An IN_PROGRESS bead with `updated_at > 7d ago` renders visibly differently in the list (color OR tag OR both) than a fresh IN_PROGRESS bead
- Details pane for any bead shows last-activity prominently near the Status field, not buried at the bottom
- When status is IN_PROGRESS and stale, a derived stalled indicator (tag or color) is visible next to the Status value
- Freshness threshold is a single source of truth shared with the alerts staleness computation (no two rules to drift)
- Behavior verified against `bt-if3w` in global mode вҖ” status chip reflects staleness

## Steps to Reproduce

1. `bt` in a terminal wide enough to show list + details
2. Select `bt-if3w` (or any IN_PROGRESS bead with `updated_at > 7d ago`)
3. Observe: Status column reads `PROG`, Details pane shows IN_PROGRESS, no visual hint that the work is dormant
4. Compare with Alerts panel вҖ” the same bead family (`bt-ushd`, `bt-t82t`, etc.) is correctly flagged stale there, but the information doesn't reach the primary list/detail display

## Relates

- `bt-46p6` (Alerts system redesign, P1 epic) вҖ” owns the staleness pipeline; this bead reuses its rule
- `bt-qzgl` (Graph view overhaul, P2) вҖ” similar critique pattern: data exists, display doesn't surface it
- `bt-if3w` вҖ” the concrete dogfood case

## Notes

- Threshold proposal: fresh <24h, recent <7d, stale >=7d. Match whatever the alerts pipeline uses.
- Agents-first concern: `bd list --json --status=in_progress` could carry a `stale` bool field in compact output so agents scanning their owned work can self-triage. Small extension; not strictly TUI. Flag as potential follow-up.

comments.author: sms
comments.text:
## Events-as-truth principle informs freshness computation (2026-04-21)

From gastown-docs-design-operational-state.md (clipped to vault):
> Events are the source of truth. Labels are the cache.

**Implication for freshness staleness classification**: last-activity timestamp should pull from the event log (audit stream) rather than just `updated_at` label. Pure label timestamps can lag or be overwritten in ways event streams can't.

**Molecule lifecycle angle** (gastown-docs-concepts-molecules.md): IN_PROGRESS may have sub-states worth distinguishing:
- "cooking" (Formula вҶ’ Proto) 
- "poured" (Proto вҶ’ Mol, steps created)
- "stalled" (Mol active but no step transitions recently)
- "squashing" (near completion, digest being written)

v1 scope can treat all as IN_PROGRESS with stale tag/color (as currently proposed). v1.1+ could distinguish via bt-5hkm (new molecule-lifecycle-mapping decision bead).

**Related**: bt-5hkm (NEW вҖ” molecule lifecycle display decision)

title: robot session-stats: net beads-opened vs closed delta (global + per-project + TUI)
description:
## Why

Agent sessions currently have no "did I actually move the needle?" signal. A session that closes 3 beads and opens 5 is *net negative* progress вҖ” more TODO debt than when it started. Today you only see raw counts (beads closed, beads created) without the composition.

Dogfood observation (2026-04-20): across bt-mhwy.1 вҶ’ .5 вҶ’ .4 execution, the natural question after each session is "how much did we chip out of the backlog vs add to it?" Nothing surfaces this. Portfolio just shipped (bt-mhwy.4) with per-project `velocity` but that's a lifetime rate, not a session delta.

## What

A **session delta** metric: `beads_closed_by_session - beads_opened_by_session` over a selectable window, exposed globally (across all projects) and per-project.

Three surfaces:

### 1. CLI / agent consumption (robot subcommand)

`bt robot session-stats` (or similar) returning:

```json
{
  "schema": "session_stats.v1",
  "session_id": "daa0ba67-вҖҰ",
  "window": "since:2026-04-20T12:56:00Z",
  "global": {
    "opened": 12,
    "closed": 7,
    "delta": -5,
    "verdict": "added debt"
  },
  "per_project": [
    {"source_repo": "bt", "opened": 5, "closed": 4, "delta": -1},
    {"source_repo": "beads", "opened": 2, "closed": 0, "delta": -2},
    {"source_repo": "marketplace", "opened": 0, "closed": 1, "delta": 1}
  ]
}
```

Flags: `--session <id>` (default: current `$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID`), `--since <time-or-ref>` for windowing beyond session start, `--global` for cross-project aggregation.

### 2. Portfolio record field (extend bt-mhwy.4)

Add `session_delta` to `PortfolioRecord` v2 so `bt robot portfolio --session <id>` shows current-session impact per-project alongside the other aggregates.

### 3. TUI visual

Footer or dedicated widget. Shapes to consider:
- Inline gauge: `в–“в–“в–“в–‘в–‘  +4 / -7   net -3`
- Color-coded single number (green = net close, red = net open, gray = zero)
- Per-project bar-chart widget (like the board-view health score but session-scoped)

## Data source

- **Closes**: `Issue.ClosedBySession` (first-class, via bt-mhwy.0 вҖ” already readable in both Dolt readers)
- **Opens**: `Issue.Metadata["created_by_session"]` (bridge via bt-mhwy.0 metadata blob). First-class column post-bd-34v, transparent swap later.
- **Window**: default "since session start" using `$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID` + session start timestamp from CASS or from `min(created_at WHERE created_by_session=X)` in the data itself.

## Scope

- In: counts + delta + per-project split, JSON robot output, TUI footer indicator
- Out: velocity curve over time (separate concern; trendlines live in portfolio)
- Out: filtering by actor or label (v1 is session-scoped only; extensions additive)
- Out: cross-session aggregation ("this week's delta across all my sessions") вҖ” defer to v2 once the v1 shape settles

## Acceptance Criteria

- `bt robot session-stats` emits the shape above with correct counts verified against hand-checked fixture
- `--session <id>` overrides the default (env var pickup)
- Single-project mode: `per_project` array has one entry; `global` and it match
- Global mode: aggregated `global` counts = sum of `per_project` counts
- Beads created pre-session (before session start) with `created_by_session == current` still count as opens (bridge metadata doesn't lie about intent)
- TUI footer shows delta gauge when a session is active (`$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID` set at launch or via config)
- Schema version `session_stats.v1`; envelope `schema` field populated

## Steps to Reproduce

N/A (feature, not bug)

## Relates

- `bt-mhwy.0` вҖ” provides the data columns (metadata + closed_by_session) this consumes
- `bt-mhwy.4` вҖ” portfolio record could embed session_delta per project
- `bt-mhwy.6` вҖ” provenance surfacing; complementary (source_repo filter + session_delta together give full "who / where / net" slice)
- `bd-34v` (upstream beads) вҖ” first-class `created_by_session` column; v1 uses metadata bridge, v2 swaps transparently
- `bt-qp1j` вҖ” test suite perf audit; unrelated but mentioned for cross-project test rigor on new projection

## Notes

- Default surface should be **one line in the TUI footer** so the signal is passive and ambient вҖ” agents don't have to ask "how'd I do?"; it's visible the whole session.
- Consider a "session digest" command for end-of-session: `bt robot session-digest` that lists each opened/closed bead by ID grouped by project. Keeps the conversational follow-through instead of a raw delta number.

comments.author: sms
comments.text:
Scope expansion (2026-04-20): time-window flexibility beyond just session.

Should support arbitrary bucket granularity:
- --window=session (default, CLAUDE_SESSION_ID scoped)
- --window=day (today / --date=2026-04-19)
- --window=week (this week / --week-of=2026-04-14)
- --window=month (this month / --month=2026-04)
- --window=custom --since=<time-or-ref> --until=<time-or-ref>

Reference implementation to learn from: cctui at ~/System/tools/cctui does exactly this time-bucket aggregation for Claude Code usage data. data/aggregate.go there takes raw entries and buckets into daily/weekly/monthly/session aggregates. Same pattern bt-ldq4 needs for bead open/close events.

Not a dependency, but worth reading before implementation вҖ” the shape of the aggregator API, the panel model (projects grouped with sessions drilling down), and the time-view panel design are all directly reusable mental models.

Followup to consider: extract the time-bucket aggregation into a shared Go pkg consumable by cctui/cass/bt. See separate exploration discussion in bt session daa0ba67-4124-4d1c-85fd-ab51a6a56bd6 (2026-04-20).

title: Project Filter modal border renders broken when Details panel visible
description:
## Bug

The Project Filter modal's border renders broken: the top and right edges don't close cleanly, and text from the underlying Details panel bleeds through where the modal border should be drawn.

## Observed

With a wide terminal and the Details panel visible on the right, opening the Project Filter modal shows:

- Top border starts as `в”Ңв”Җ Project Filter в”Җв”Җв”Җ` then stops вҖ” no right corner or right edge
- Project list rows render without a right border; text from the Details panel is visible to the right of each project name
- Bottom caption row (`toggle: space  all: a  apply: enter`) is partial or misaligned
- Result: the modal looks like a half-drawn overlay pasted on top of the detail pane rather than a bounded box

## Expected

The modal should render as a fully-bounded box that either masks or sits cleanly above the Details panel text, with closed top/right/bottom borders and the caption inside the frame.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. `bt` in a terminal wide enough to show both the issue list and the Details panel side-by-side
2. Select any issue so the Details panel is populated
3. Open the Project Filter modal (via the `projects` footer hint / hotkey)
4. Observe the modal's right edge and top-right corner

## Acceptance Criteria

- Project Filter modal renders with all four border edges closed regardless of Details-panel visibility
- Caption row (`toggle: space  all: a  apply: enter`) sits inside the frame
- No text from the Details panel bleeds through the modal area while the modal is open
- Fix holds at multiple terminal widths (narrow enough that Details panel collapses, wide enough that it renders)

## Likely cause (speculative)

Probably a lipgloss width or Place/PlaceOverlay issue where:
- The modal's computed width doesn't match its render width (border width accounting off by 1вҖ“2 cells), OR
- The modal is rendered on top of the detail pane without background fill, so unrendered border cells show the pane underneath, OR
- Terminal cell-width math is off when the modal renders across a pane boundary

This is a regression вҖ” the modal rendered correctly in a recent prior session.

## Scope

- In: fix the border so it closes cleanly regardless of whether the Details panel is visible
- Out: redesigning the modal layout itself; this is a rendering fix

## Files (probable)

- `pkg/ui/` вҖ” wherever the Project Filter modal is composed
- lipgloss Border / Place / PlaceOverlay calls
- Project/workspace filter handler

## Related

- Global mode polish work (bt-dcby, etc.)
- Recent model-decomp / charm v2 work may have introduced this

## Environment

- Windows 11, Windows Terminal
- bt `main` post-bt-mhwy.1 ship (commit `69b1f602` or later)

comments.author: sms
comments.text:
Additional dogfood data (2026-04-20): the border breakage varies by view state, not just Details-panel presence. In TRIAGE view the footer composition shifts (OPENвҶ’TRIAGE indicator) and the modal bottom row alignment changes with it вҖ” the breakage looks worse than in the default list view. In default/global list + sort views, the top/left/right edges render okay but the bottom border gap where the caption (toggle/all/apply) sits is still visible.

State-machine summary:
- default list + Details visible: top-right edge gap (original repro)
- TRIAGE view + Details visible: bottom row extra-broken вҖ” footer changes shift the modal's overlay coordinates
- sort-only views (no Details): renders cleanly вҖ” narrowest failure mode confirms the overlay math depends on adjacent-pane width

This reinforces the likely root cause: the modal's Place/PlaceOverlay coordinates are computed against a snapshot of the current view's chrome (footer text, right panel width) rather than being self-contained. Fix shape: ensure the modal carries its own background fill across all four edges and renders independent of the footer/rightpane composition.

title: Audit test-suite runtime performance + coverage gaps
description:
## Why

Observed during bt-mhwy.0 verification: `go test ./...` takes >60s wall time and runs the CPU hot. Worst offenders in the last run:

- `pkg/ui` 22.7 s
- `pkg/loader` 20.6 s
- `pkg/export` 17.9 s
- `pkg/hooks` 11.0 s

Everything else is sub-2 s. Four packages account for the bulk of the runtime. For a project this size that's slower than it should be вҖ” slow feedback loop during development, CI gets expensive, and adjacent beads (`bt-tjq0` progress visibility, `bt-5dvl` test-suite correctness audit) suggest the suite has been a source of friction for a while.

Existing beads in this space:

- `bt-5dvl` (P2) вҖ” fix correctness issues from the 2026-04-03 test-suite audit.
- `bt-tjq0` (P2) вҖ” test progress visibility.
- `bt-kvk0` (P3) вҖ” Dolt e2e infra gap.
- `bt-5e99` (P1) вҖ” three failing drift tests (just filed).

None address runtime performance or survey coverage gaps systematically. This bead does that.

## What

Produce `docs/audit/<date>-test-suite-perf-coverage.md` covering:

1. **Runtime profile** per package. Identify the long poles in `pkg/ui`, `pkg/loader`, `pkg/export`, `pkg/hooks`. For each:
   - Where is the time going? (sleeps, fixture setup, real I/O, serial subtests that should parallelize)
   - Can it be cut with `t.Parallel()`, shared fixtures, or scope reduction without losing coverage?
2. **Parallelism audit**. Which packages opt out of `-parallel` and why? Any `t.Setenv` / `t.Chdir` contamination stopping parallel runs?
3. **CPU / mem profile** of the suite. Use `go test -cpuprofile` on the top-4 offenders; flag any hot loops that look like accidental O(nВІ).
4. **Coverage gaps**. Run `go test -coverprofile`, summarize per-package. Flag packages with <50% coverage. Distinguish "fine вҖ” code is trivial" from "risky вҖ” core logic uncovered".
5. **Flaky / sensitive tests**. Any tests that depend on wall time, process env, filesystem ordering, or port availability. Catalog them as flakes or re-architect candidates.
6. **Recommendations**. Tiered output (Tier 1: fix flakes + parallelize obvious wins; Tier 2: rewrite top-1 offender; Tier 3: backfill coverage gaps). Each recommendation ends up as its own bead.

## Scope

- **In**: auditing the current suite, producing a report, filing follow-up beads for the recommendations.
- **Out**: implementing the fixes. Each recommendation is a separate bead with its own scope.
- **Out**: migrating off Go's default test runner.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Report at `docs/audit/<date>-test-suite-perf-coverage.md` with the six sections above.
- Each recommendation has a filed bead with a `depends_on` back to this one.
- Top-4 slow packages each have an explanation (why it's slow) and a class-of-fix proposed.
- Coverage gaps are quantified per package, not vibes-based.

## Relates

- `bt-5dvl` (scope sibling; this bead covers perf+coverage, that one covers correctness)
- `bt-tjq0` (progress visibility вҖ” complementary)
- `bt-kvk0` (Dolt e2e infra вҖ” likely called out in the coverage gap section)
- `bt-5e99` (drift test failures вҖ” will surface in the flaky-tests section)

title: Audit: bt Dolt column list vs upstream beads schema
description:
## Why

bt's Dolt readers (`internal/datasource/dolt.go`, `global_dolt.go`, `columns.go`) query a fixed column list from upstream beads' `issues` table. Upstream beads ships ~30 releases / 4 months and adds columns regularly. bt's column list drifts silently вҖ” new upstream columns exist but bt cannot read them.

Two already-known drift points (discovered 2026-04-20 during bt-mhwy.1 brainstorm):
- `metadata` вҖ” JSON blob on issues table (migration 0001, has always existed)
- `closed_by_session` вҖ” first-class session ID column (migration 034, Dec 2025)

Probability of exactly 2 missing columns being the full gap: near zero. Need a systematic pass.

## What

- Read upstream beads' current `issues` table schema from `~/System/tools/beads/internal/storage/schema/migrations/` (latest migration is authoritative)
- Compare against bt's `IssuesColumns` constant and scan logic in `dolt.go` / `global_dolt.go`
- Also compare related tables: `labels`, `dependencies`, `comments` (bt queries these separately)
- Produce a report: missing columns (bt cannot read), renamed/retyped columns (bt's scan would break), removed columns (bt reads something that no longer exists)

## Scope

- Out: implementing the fixes вҖ” that is bt-mhwy.0
- Out: changes beyond the issues table family (gates, molecules, wisps вҖ” separate scope if needed)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Report document at `docs/audit/YYYY-MM-DD-bt-beads-schema-drift.md` listing:
  - Missing columns on `issues`
  - Missing columns on `labels`, `dependencies`, `comments`
  - Any type/name mismatches
  - Recommended scope for bt-mhwy.0
- Report is the authoritative scope input for bt-mhwy.0

## Relates

- bt-mhwy.0 (implementation bead that consumes this audit)
- Future: periodic drift detection (see related bead вҖ” filed as follow-up)
close_reason:
## Summary

Audit of bts Dolt column list vs upstream beads schema identified 25 missing columns on the issues table - 23 more than the two known points (metadata, closed_by_session). Labels and comments tables are clean; dependencies has 4 missing fields (enrichment only).

## Change

Produced docs/audit/2026-04-20-bt-beads-schema-drift.md with a per-column table (upstream vs bt), grouped by purpose, plus a tiered recommendation for bt-mhwy.0 scope:
- Tier 1 (blocks bt-mhwy.1): metadata, closed_by_session
- Tier 2 (cheap wins): created_by, owner, spec_id, defer_until, started_at, no_history, pinned, content_hash
- Tier 3 (defer): 16 agent/event-wisp/gate-waiter columns

## Files

- docs/audit/2026-04-20-bt-beads-schema-drift.md (new)

## Verify

- Cross-checked upstream schema in ~/System/tools/beads/internal/storage/schema/migrations/ (0001 base + additive migrations 0023, 0027).
- Cross-checked bt columns in internal/datasource/columns.go, scan logic in internal/datasource/dolt.go and internal/datasource/global_dolt.go.

## Risk

Report only; no code change. Minimal risk of becoming stale between now and bt-mhwy.0 execution. If a new upstream migration lands in that window, the report flags enough about the scan pattern that the catchup still applies.

## Notes

Followup bead bt-wjzk already exists for periodic drift detection. Audit surfaced that bts pkg/model/types.go Issue.Clone() is the common omission point when adding new pointer/map/slice fields - noted in the report for bt-mhwy.0.

title: Provenance surfacing: source_repo in compact output + --source filter
description:
## Why

Every issue has a `source_repo` field populated from the Dolt database name. Today it's not exposed in robot output, so agents can't filter "show me beads from cass" or see at a glance that "this blocker lives in beads repo." When upstream beads adds richer session provenance (bt-2cvx, currently blocked on upstream support), bt should surface that too вҖ” but the floor is surfacing what's already there.

## What

- Include `source_repo` in compact output records for all robot subcommands (piggy-backs on bt-mhwy.1's compact shape)
- Add `--source <prefix>` filter to `robot list` and `robot triage` вҖ” e.g., `--source cass` or `--source cass,bt` for multi-project
- Document `source_repo` field in `robot schema` and `robot docs` output

## Acceptance Criteria

- `source_repo` appears in compact output records across robot subcommands
- `--source <prefix>` filters correctly, comma-sep supported for multi-project
- Unknown prefix in `--source` produces empty result + warning (not error)
- `robot schema` output includes the `source_repo` field definition

## Relates

- bt-2cvx (session author provenance вҖ” future richer data)
- dotfiles-jeq (cross-project authorship requirements)
close_reason:
Summary: Shipped --source <project>[,<project>...] persistent robot flag scoping output to one or more projects. Closes the 7th and final bt-mhwy child. Scope was smaller than originally planned because source_repo already surfaced in compact/portfolio/pair output from the earlier children вҖ” .6 reduced to the CLI ergonomics flag.

Change: Added filterBySource helper with comma-parse + case-insensitive prefix/SourceRepo matching. Wired persistent --source flag on robotCmd. Applied inside robotPreRun (covers triage/next/insights/plan/priority/alerts/suggest/etc.) AND inside robot list RunE (which bypasses robotPreRun). robot list also carries the filter through to compact reverse-map computation so children_count/unblocks_count reflect the scoped graph, and echoes query.source in the envelope.

Files: cmd/bt/helpers.go (filterBySource + splitSourceFilter + matchesSource), cmd/bt/cobra_robot.go (robotFlagSource var + PersistentFlag registration + robotPreRun hook), cmd/bt/robot_list.go (baseIssues from filterBySource + compact reverse-map uses baseIssues + listQuery.Source field + echo), cmd/bt/helpers_source_test.go (8 unit cases), cmd/bt/robot_source_test.go (3 binary contract cases), cmd/bt/robot_help.go (--source section documenting flag + source_repo surfacing in compact/pair/portfolio schemas), CHANGELOG.md (session entry), docs/adr/002-stabilize-and-ship.md (Stream 1 [x] line).

Verify: go build ./... PASS. go test ./cmd/bt/ -run TestFilterBySource|TestRobotList_SourceFilter PASS (11 cases). bt robot list --source=bt --limit=3 returns 3 bt-* records with query.source=bt. bt robot list --source=nonexistent returns count=0 exit 0. bt robot list --source=cass,bt --limit=5 --global mixes records. bt robot triage --source=cass --global scopes triage stats. source_repo already documented in pkg/view/schemas/{compact_issue,pair_record}.v1.json (no new schema work needed).

Risk: Low. filterBySource is a pure filter composed alongside existing filterByRepo. Applied in two separate places because robot list bypasses robotPreRun; both call the same helper so semantics stay consistent. Unknown prefixes produce empty results (not error) per plan. Pre-existing failures (bt-5e99 drift, bt-kt7x blocker-chain, bt-eqro e2e timeout) unchanged.

Notes: mhwy epic now 7 of 7 children closed вҖ” .0 (columns), .1 (compact), .5 (external deps), .4 (portfolio), .2 (pairs), .3 (refs), .6 (source filter). Open follow-ups: bt-gkyn (pairs v2 intent identity), bt-vxu9 (refs v2 sigil identity). Comma-separated syntax chosen over repeated --source flags for terseness; whitespace trimming + empty token dropping make hand-edited commands forgiving. Matching is case-insensitive on both sides to avoid agent-facing ambiguity between `bt` and `BT`.

comments.author: sms
comments.text: Upstream dependency: bd-34v (extend `--session` to bd create and bd update --claim, per-event columns created_by_session/claimed_by_session/closed_by_session). Filed in beads project 2026-04-20 per Gas Town decision 009-session-events-architecture.md. Until it ships, metadata-blob bridge (`--set-metadata session_id=$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID`) can populate provenance and compact output can surface via metadata lookup. Once bd-34v ships first-class columns, bt compact output should bump to compact.v2 and surface those columns directly. See bd-34v full body for per-event naming + abandon/re-claim semantics.

comments.author: sms
comments.text:
Partial credit status after bt-mhwy.2 shipped (2026-04-20):

**Already done** вҖ” `source_repo` already ships in the compact projection (`pkg/view/compact_issue.go` вҖ” field present with `omitempty`). Also surfaced in:
- `portfolio.v1` records (via `project` field, which keys on `SourceRepo` under `--global`)
- `pair.v1` records (`PairMember.SourceRepo` вҖ” used to distinguish mirrors)

**Still to do** here:
1. `--source <prefix>` filter flag on `robot list` / `robot triage` вҖ” the actual filter logic and multi-value comma parsing.
2. Document the field in `robot schema` / `robot docs`.

Scope is smaller than originally framed because surfacing landed incrementally as the other .5 / .4 / .2 children shipped. Consider this .6 primarily a CLI ergonomics feature (the `--source` filter) rather than a data exposure feature вҖ” the data is already exposed.

title: go test output has poor progress visibility - can't tell if stuck, running, or silently failed
description:
## Bug

Running `go test ./...` for bt takes ~30-60s with no intermediate progress output. Packages don't print anything until they're done, and the aggregate runner doesn't stream per-package progress either. During a run you cannot tell:

- Whether the suite is still progressing or hung
- Which package it's currently executing
- Which tests have passed so far vs. which are queued
- Whether a silent failure has been swallowed

Impact is worst for agent-driven workflows: when an AI session dispatches a full test run and the command output stays empty, it's impossible to distinguish between "still running" and "completed with no output captured" without manually tailing the output file. This session hit it twice today вҖ” both runs were backgrounded because they appeared to hang; the first had actually already finished successfully ~15s earlier.

## Repro

1. `go test ./...` from repo root
2. Observe: no output for ~30s, then one final block

## Expected

- Per-package streaming: each `ok` / `FAIL` line emitted as it completes, not batched at the end
- Optionally: a heartbeat ("running: pkg/ui ...") so a multi-minute package doesn't look hung
- Clear terminal indication when the run has completed, even if the last output line scrolled past

## Likely paths

- Check whether we can stream via `go test -v ./...` (though -v floods with per-test logs)
- `go test -json ./...` + a small formatter that shows per-package state transitions
- A Makefile / `scripts/test.sh` wrapper with prettier output
- Whether `go test -run=. -count=1 ./... | while read line; ...` style pipeline solves the batching

## Related

- Session today: bt-nzsy fix - first `go test ./...` finished silently while I waited for it; the agent never knew.

## Acceptance

- [ ] A standard "run all tests" command shows per-package progress as it happens, not batched
- [ ] Agent or human can tell at a glance whether the suite is running, completed, or stuck
- [ ] Silent failures (e.g. a panic in a package with no test output) are surfaced clearly
notes: dogfood verify 2026-04-18: triggering refresh to test bt-nzsy fix - filter should stay populated after this update.

title: E2e TestExportPages_CSSPresent crashes on Windows: copyFile syscall failure
description:
## Bug

TestExportPages_CSSPresent panics in stageViewerAssets -> copyDirRecursive -> copyFile at export_pages_test.go:169 (os.Create syscall). The test takes 601s (hits the go test 10-minute timeout for the e2e package) because the panic crashes the goroutine.

## Stack trace
syscall.createFile fails on the destination path. Path length is 0x66 (102 chars) which combined with Windows temp dir nesting could hit MAX_PATH limits. Or it could be a permissions issue with the test temp directory.

## Impact
The e2e test suite takes 601s instead of completing quickly, slowing the feedback loop for all bt development. Only this one test fails - all other packages pass.

## Fix options
1. Shorten the temp dir prefix in buildBtBinary/createSimpleRepo to reduce path depth
2. Use t.TempDir() closer to the root (shorter base path)
3. Skip on Windows if path length is the root cause
4. Enable long path support in the test setup

comments.author: sms
comments.text:
Confirmed hit 2026-04-20 during bt-mhwy.2 session close: `go test ./...` timed out at 601s with the exact stack trace on `TestExportPages_SQLiteDatabase` (copyFile вҶ’ createFile вҶ’ syscall.createFile). Panic is identical to the one recorded here. Workaround for the close-session flow: `go test $(go list ./... | grep -v tests/e2e)` completes in ~45s and still covers every other package.

Ask when fixing: skip under `-short` might be the smallest change, and it'd let CI keep full coverage while developers get fast local test runs.

title: Dolt poll change detection misses comment-only updates
description:
## Bug

The Dolt poll loop (doltPollOnce / globalDoltPollOnce) detects data changes by checking MAX(updated_at) FROM issues. When a comment is added via bd comment, beads does not bump the issue's updated_at field. This means comment-only updates are invisible to the change detection, and the TUI never refreshes to show new comments.

## Observed

cass-wv2 has Updated: 2026-04-15 but a comment was added at 2026-04-16 14:57. The TUI did not detect or display the new comment.

## Expected

New comments should trigger a data refresh so the TUI displays them.

## Fix

Include MAX(created_at) FROM comments in the change detection query using GREATEST().
close_reason:
## Summary
Fixed comments not loading in global mode TUI.

## Root Causes (3 bugs)
1. Comment.ID was int64 but Dolt uses char(36) UUIDs - every scan failed silently (326 errors, 0 loaded)
2. Change detection only checked issues.updated_at, missing comment-only updates
3. Global UNION ALL had no resilience for databases missing the comments table

## Changes
- pkg/model/types.go: Comment.ID int64->string + custom UnmarshalJSON for backward compat
- internal/datasource/dolt.go: GetLastModified uses GREATEST() across issues+comments
- internal/datasource/global_dolt.go: buildLastModifiedQuery includes comments, databasesWithTable helper, loadAllComments filters DBs
- Tests and references updated across 9 files

## Verify
Restart bt in global mode, select a bead with comments, tab to detail panel, scroll down - comments visible.

title: CASS session correlator finds no results for known issue IDs
description:
## Bug

Pressing V on an issue in bt shows 'No correlated sessions found' even when cass sessions exist that discuss that issue ID. Confirmed: cass search from CLI also returns 0 for 'bt-46p6' despite sessions containing that string.

## Likely Cause
Issue IDs with hyphens and dots (bt-46p6.1) may not tokenize properly in cass search. The correlator calls cass search under the hood - if the search doesn't find matches, the modal never opens.

## Reproduce
1. Open bt, select bt-46p6.1
2. Press V
3. Status bar: 'No correlated sessions found for bt-46p6.1'
4. But: cass search 'alert modal' finds sessions discussing these exact beads

## Cross-project
The fix is likely in cass (search tokenization) not bt. Paired bead needed in cass.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] V on an issue finds sessions that mention that issue ID
- [ ] IDs with hyphens and dots match correctly

comments.author: sms
comments.text: Paired with cass-4yn4 in cass project. The fix is likely on the cass search/tokenization side. bt-4yn4 tracks the consumer experience (V key correlator).

comments.author: sms
comments.text:
2026-04-15 analysis from cass project:

**The search does work for simple hyphenated IDs** - tested 'bt-46p6' against the live index, returns 1 result. The hyphen gets split by both the query sanitizer and content tokenizer, so the AND of fragments ('bt' AND '46p6') matches correctly.

**Where bt's correlator may be hitting issues:**
1. If searching for dotted sub-IDs like 'bt-46p6.1' - the dot splits into a third token '1' which is too common and dilutes results or returns false matches
2. If the session being searched hasn't been indexed yet (watcher lag) - check that cass watcher is running and the session file exists before searching
3. If the correlator adds extra flags (--days, time filters) that exclude the target session

**What bt should do for now:**
- For bead ID searches: use just the suffix portion ('46p6') which is unique enough to match without the hyphen problem
- Or: use '--mode lexical' explicitly to avoid hybrid mode latency on short queries
- Verify cass watcher is running before V correlator calls

**What cass will fix (tracked in cass-4yn4):**
- Preserve hyphens in query sanitization so 'bt-46p6' is treated as one token
- This is a cass-side fix, no bt changes needed once it ships

comments.author: sms
comments.text:
## cass side has research-informed fixes incoming (2026-04-21)

Research corpus identifies the CASS session correlator pattern this bug is hitting. Parallel session surveyed cass's open beads and proposed new integration work:

- **cass-yxjp** (new) вҖ” epic for beadsвҶ”cass integration, parent for session_id exposure + bead-to-transcript commands
- **cass-fjip** (existing) вҖ” post bd-fjip landing, verify path format is consumable by `cass transcript <path>`
- **cass-bmf1** (existing) вҖ” add session_id as first-class field on SearchHit
- **cass-uh3c** (new) вҖ” `cass trace <bead-id>` command

**Likely root cause of this bug**: correlator may be looking for beads in cass's index but the beads data lives in Dolt (not indexed by cass yet). cass-zzi discussion in cass's beads suggests indexing beads into cass SQLite as the cross-project aggregation path.

**Once bd-34v Phase 1a lands** (today), beads will have `created_by_session` column. cass can then join beads вҶ’ sessions by session_id. That should fix the correlator bug by providing a reliable join key.

**Recommend**: leave this bug open; revisit after cass-yxjp is executed. Likely becomes trivially fixable once the join key lands.

title: Repo picker opens with nothing selected in all-projects mode
description:
## Feature

When opening the project picker from the all-projects view, it currently shows all projects checked. Better UX: open with nothing selected so you can quickly pick one or two with minimal keystrokes.

## Current Behavior
- All-projects mode: picker opens with all checkmarks on
- Filtered mode (3 projects): picker opens with those 3 checked

## Proposed Behavior  
- All-projects mode: picker opens with nothing checked (quick-pick)
- Filtered mode (3 projects): picker opens with those 3 checked (add/remove)
- a key still toggles all on/off as escape hatch

## Edge Case
User has 3 selected, wants to add a 4th: works naturally because picker opens with the 3 checked.

## Files
- pkg/ui/repo_picker.go - SetActiveRepos() initialization
- pkg/ui/model_update_input.go - picker open logic

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Picker opens empty when current state is all-projects
- [ ] Picker opens with current selection when filtered
- [ ] a key toggles all/none
- [ ] Enter with nothing selected returns to all-projects mode
close_reason: Picker opens empty when activeRepos is nil or single-project. Multi-project groups (2+) preserve checkmarks. Quick-pick via enter-on-cursor works for fast project switching.

title: Background data refresh closes open modals
description:
## Bug

When beads data updates in the background (Dolt polling detects changes), the alerts modal closes unexpectedly. Likely affects all modals, not just alerts.

## Reproduce
1. Open bt in workspace mode
2. Open the alerts modal (!)
3. In another session, update a bead (bd update, bd close, etc.)
4. When bt detects the data change, the modal closes

## Expected
Modals should stay open across background refreshes. Data inside the modal should update, but the modal itself shouldn't dismiss.

## Files
- pkg/ui/model_update_data.go - lines near closeModal() calls during refresh

title: CLI alert system alignment with TUI changes
description:
## Task

Ensure CLI alert commands stay in sync with TUI alert system changes. The CLI (robot_alerts.go, cli_baseline.go) already has severity/type/label filtering and JSON output. Any taxonomy renames, new alert types, or severity recalibrations must be reflected in both surfaces.

## Scope

This is a coordination task, not a standalone implementation. It tracks the CLI side-effects of other beads in this epic:

- bt-46p6.4 (taxonomy rename): CLI --alert-type must accept new names, old names still work
- bt-46p6.6 (severity recalibration): CLI JSON output severity fields reflect new assignments
- bt-46p6.7 (abandoned_claim): CLI --alert-type=abandoned_claim must work
- bt-46p6.8 (scoping): CLI may need --scope=project|global flag

## Files
- cmd/bt/robot_alerts.go - runAlerts(), filter logic, JSON output
- cmd/bt/cli_baseline.go - runCheckDrift(), runSaveBaseline()

## Notes
This bead should be checked after each sibling is completed to verify CLI parity.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] CLI --alert-type accepts all new type names (plus backward compat for old)
- [ ] CLI JSON output reflects recalibrated severity assignments
- [ ] CLI --alert-type=abandoned_claim works after detector is implemented
- [ ] CLI drift check output consistent with TUI alert display
- [ ] No breaking changes to existing CLI scripts or agent integrations

comments.author: sms
comments.text: Post-ship check (2026-04-15 session): bt-46p6.3, .5, .6, .7 shipped. CLI is fine - severity recalibration and abandoned_claim flow through the shared drift calculator automatically. --alert-type=abandoned_claim confirmed working. No CLI-specific changes needed until .4 (taxonomy rename) lands, which will need backward-compat name mapping in robot_alerts.go.

title: Blocking cascade indicator icon in list view
description:
## Feature

Add a visual indicator to the list view for issues that are blocking cascade candidates (completing them would unblock 3+ downstream issues). This is high-signal at-a-glance information that shouldn't require opening the alerts modal.

## Context

The blocking cascade data is already computed by drift.Calculator.checkBlockingCascade(). Each alert includes the IssueID and the count of downstream unblocks. The list view already shows various icons (type icons, status icons, priority) - adding a cascade indicator follows the same pattern.

## Design

- Icon: small cascade/ripple symbol (e.g. вҡЎ or вҶҜ or similar) next to issues that would unblock 3+ others
- Position: after the existing status/type icons, before the title
- Color: use theme.Feature (yellow/gold) to indicate "opportunity" not "problem"  
- Tooltip/detail: show count in detail panel: "Unblocks 7 downstream issues"
- Data flow: during filter/list population, check if issue ID appears in blocking cascade alerts

## Files
- pkg/ui/model_filter.go - applyFilter() where IssueItems are constructed
- pkg/ui/item.go - IssueItem struct, may need a CascadeCount field
- pkg/ui/delegate.go - list item rendering
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Issues that would unblock 3+ downstream items show a cascade icon in list view
- [ ] Icon uses a distinct color (not red/severity - this is opportunity, not problem)
- [ ] Detail panel shows unblock count when viewing a cascade issue
- [ ] Cascade data sourced from existing drift computation (no extra analysis pass)
- [ ] Icon doesn't appear when there are 0 cascade alerts
close_reason: Already implemented by triage system. UnblocksCount populates in model_filter.go, renders as lock/arrow+count in delegate.go:273-285, detail panel shows full unblock info. No new code needed.

title: Scope alert computation for global vs project modes
description:
## Task

Alert computation currently runs on the full issue set regardless of view mode. Graph-structural metrics (density, node/edge counts, pagerank) mean different things at global vs project scope. Cross-project dependencies (external: prefix) affect graph density but aren't properly accounted for.

## Problems

1. **Density growth** in global mode measures density across 15+ projects - cross-project deps are sparse, so density is always low. A "density growth" alert at global scope means something fundamentally different than at project scope.
2. **PageRank** across all projects mixes unrelated dependency graphs. A highly-central issue in one project gets diluted by unrelated issues from other projects.
3. **Cross-project deps** (external:project:capability) create edges between projects that the graph engine doesn't resolve - they're treated as dangling references.
4. **Baseline comparison** is global-only. No per-project baseline snapshots.

## Design

### Option A: Scope-aware computation
- In project mode: compute alerts only on that project's issues
- In global mode: compute per-project + global aggregate
- Alert.SourceProject field to indicate scope

### Option B: Dual computation
- Always compute both global and per-project alerts
- Display appropriate set based on current view mode
- More expensive but provides complete picture

### Cross-project dep resolution
- Before running analysis: resolve external: deps against the global issue set
- This gives the graph engine real edges instead of dangling refs
- Affects density, pagerank, blocking cascade, cycle detection

## Files
- pkg/ui/model_alerts.go - computeAlerts() input selection
- pkg/drift/calculator.go - all check methods
- pkg/analysis/analyzer.go - graph construction (where external deps need resolving)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Project mode alerts computed only on that project's issues
- [ ] Global mode alerts include project attribution
- [ ] Cross-project external: deps resolved before graph analysis
- [ ] Density and pagerank metrics meaningful at both scopes
- [ ] Alert struct includes scope/project attribution field

title: Implement abandoned_claim alert detector
description:
## Feature

Implement the abandoned_claim alert type. Detects when someone claimed an issue (set assignee or moved to in_progress) but then went silent - no updates for a configurable threshold.

## Context

The AlertAbandonedClaim constant exists in pkg/drift/drift.go (line 39) but has zero implementation. The data needed is already available: issue.Owner (assignee), issue.Status, issue.UpdatedAt. This is a common real-world problem - work gets claimed and forgotten.

## Design

### Detection Logic (in calculator.go)
1. Find issues where: status == in_progress AND owner != "" AND (now - updated_at) >= threshold
2. Severity:
   - Warning: P0-P1 abandoned 7+ days (configurable)
   - Warning: P2 abandoned 14+ days
   - Info: P3-P4 abandoned 21+ days
3. Message format: "Issue {id} claimed by {owner} but inactive for {days} days"

### Config (in config.go)
- abandoned_warning_days: default 14 (overridable)
- abandoned_priority_multiplier: like in_progress_stale_multiplier, tighter for high-priority

### Distinction from stale_issue
- stale_issue: any issue inactive for N days regardless of assignment
- abandoned_claim: specifically assigned/in-progress issues that went silent
- An issue can trigger both if it's both stale AND claimed

## Files
- pkg/drift/calculator.go - new checkAbandonedClaims() method
- pkg/drift/config.go - new threshold fields
- pkg/drift/drift.go - constant already exists
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Issues in_progress with an owner and no updates for N days trigger abandoned_claim alert
- [ ] Severity scales with issue priority (P0-P1 stricter thresholds)
- [ ] Config supports abandoned_warning_days threshold
- [ ] Alert message includes issue ID, owner, and days inactive
- [ ] Distinct from stale_issue (both can fire on same issue)
- [ ] Respects disabled_alerts config
- [ ] CLI --alert-type=abandoned_claim filter works
close_reason: checkAbandonedClaims() implemented with priority-scaled thresholds. Config field AbandonedWarningDays (default 14). P0-P1 warn at 7d, P2 at 14d, P3-P4 at 21d. Zero alerts currently (all in_progress issues actively maintained).

title: Recalibrate alert severity with priority awareness
description:
## Task

Review and recalibrate severity assignments across all alert types. Current severity is assigned without considering issue priority, leading to noise: a stale P4 backlog item at 30 days gets "critical" alongside a genuine dependency deadlock.

## Current Problems

1. **Stale issues**: 30+ days = critical regardless of priority. A forgotten P4 shouldn't be critical. A stale P0 absolutely should be.
2. **Actionable count change**: 30% drop = warning. This is informational, not actionable from the alert. Better suited as a notification.
3. **Node/edge count changes**: always info. Low signal, catches normal growth. Consider demoting or removing.
4. **No priority weighting**: severity is purely threshold-based, ignoring issue priority entirely.

## Proposed Recalibration

### Priority-aware staleness
- P0 stale 7+ days вҶ’ critical, P0 stale 3+ days вҶ’ warning
- P1 stale 14+ days вҶ’ critical, P1 stale 7+ days вҶ’ warning  
- P2 stale 30+ days вҶ’ warning, P2 stale 14+ days вҶ’ info
- P3/P4 stale 30+ days вҶ’ info only (never critical/warning)

### Reclassifications
- actionable_change: demote to info-only (or move to notification center)
- node/edge count: consider disabling by default in drift config
- blocking_cascade: consider promoting info (3+) items to warning when downstream priorities are P0-P1

## Impact

- pkg/drift/calculator.go - checkStaleness(), checkActionable(), checkBlockingCascade()
- pkg/drift/config.go - new priority-aware threshold fields
- Existing drift configs may need migration
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Stale issue severity factors in issue priority (P0-P1 stricter than P3-P4)
- [ ] Actionable count change demoted or moved to notification tier
- [ ] Node/edge count changes disabled by default or demoted
- [ ] Blocking cascade severity considers downstream priorities
- [ ] Drift config supports priority-aware thresholds
- [ ] Existing configs continue to work (backward compat)
close_reason: Priority-aware staleness (P0-P1 halved, P3 doubled, P4+ info-only), actionable demoted to info, node/edge disabled by default, cascade promoted on high-pri downstream. 171->153 alerts, 105->78 critical.

comments.author: sms
comments.text: Session discussion (2026-04-15): blocked_increase may belong in a health/trends section rather than per-issue alert list - it's an aggregate signal, not per-item. node/edge count changes should be disabled by default (too noisy, catches normal growth). actionable_change feels more like a notification than an alert - consider demoting to notification center (bt-46p6.10).

title: Stackable alert filters: severity, type, project, sort
description:
## Feature

Add stackable filters to the alerts modal: severity, alert type, project, and sort order. Filters compose (AND logic) so users can narrow to e.g. "critical stale alerts in bt project, oldest first."

## Context

The CLI already supports severity and type filtering (robot_alerts.go lines 84-105). The TUI has no filter mechanism in the alerts modal. The label picker and repo picker modals demonstrate filter UI patterns.

## Design

### Filter Dimensions
1. **Severity**: cycle through all вҶ’ critical вҶ’ warning вҶ’ info (key: `s`)
2. **Alert type**: cycle through all вҶ’ stale вҶ’ cascade вҶ’ loop вҶ’ etc. (key: `t`)
3. **Project**: cycle through all вҶ’ per-project (key: `p`)
4. **Sort**: default вҶ’ oldest first вҶ’ newest first вҶ’ severity (key: `o` for order)

### UI
- Active filters shown in summary line below title: "65 warning В· bt В· oldest first"
- Each filter key cycles through its options
- Filters apply immediately (no "apply" step)
- Clear all filters: capital `R` (reset)

### State
- Add filter state fields to Model: alertFilterSeverity, alertFilterType, alertFilterProject, alertSortOrder
- visibleAlerts() applies all active filters before returning
- Filter state persists while modal is open, resets on close

## Dependencies

- bt-46p6.3 (surface alert types) should land first so users can see what they're filtering by
- bt-46p6.4 (taxonomy rename) for human-readable type names in the filter cycle
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Severity filter cycles through all/critical/warning/info
- [ ] Type filter cycles through all and each active alert type
- [ ] Project filter cycles through all and each project with alerts
- [ ] Sort toggles between default/oldest-first/newest-first
- [ ] Filters compose (AND logic) and update count in summary
- [ ] Active filters displayed in summary line
- [ ] Reset key clears all filters
- [ ] Filter state resets when modal is closed and reopened
close_reason: Stackable filters (s/t/p/o + shift:prev), pre-built sort keys, modal layout polish, standardized footer format. All acceptance criteria met.

comments.author: sms
comments.text: Session discussion (2026-04-15): search in alerts modal likely unnecessary - filtering by severity + type + project + sort covers the actual use cases. Alert messages are machine-generated and repetitive, not free text you'd search through. If filtering proves insufficient, revisit.

title: Surface alert types in TUI modal
description:
## Feature

Surface alert type information in the alerts modal. Currently each alert shows only a severity icon and message string - the type (stale_issue, blocking_cascade, new_cycle, etc.) is computed but invisible.

## Context

Alert types are stored in drift.Alert.Type but never rendered. The CLI robot mode (robot_alerts.go) already exposes types via --alert-type filter flag. The TUI needs to catch up.

## Design Options

1. **Inline label**: Show type as a tag/badge on each alert line: `в–І [stale] Issue portfolio-mavb inactive for 21 days`
2. **Group headers**: Group alerts by type with section headers
3. **Filter-only**: Don't show in list but expose as a filter dimension

Recommendation: Option 1 (inline label) as the minimal viable approach. Grouping can be a follow-up. The label should use the human-readable type name (after taxonomy rename, bt-46p6.4).

## Files

- pkg/ui/model_alerts.go - renderAlertsPanel(), alert line rendering (lines 172-235)
- pkg/drift/drift.go - AlertType constants (lines 27-41)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Each alert line displays its type as a readable label
- [ ] Type labels use human-friendly names (not raw constants)
- [ ] Type display doesn't break layout or truncation at narrow widths
- [ ] Type information available for filter integration (bt-46p6.5)
close_reason: Added inline [type] tags to alert modal lines. alertTypeLabel() maps all 13 types to human-readable names. Visual confirmation passed. Force-close: dep direction was inverted (.3 unblocks .5, not the reverse).

title: Alert modal pagination with left/right arrow keys
description:
## Feature

Add left/right arrow key pagination to the alerts modal, matching the pattern used by the label filter picker. When there are more alerts than fit on one page, show page indicator (e.g. "3/12 (169 alerts)") and navigate with left/right arrows.

## Context

The label picker (pkg/ui/label_picker.go) already implements this pattern with page indicators and left/right navigation. The alerts modal currently uses j/k scrolling with scroll offset tracking but no concept of discrete pages.

## Design

- Page size = alertsVisibleLines() (already computed)
- Left arrow: previous page, right arrow: next page
- Footer shows: "2/12 (169 alerts)" alongside existing keybindings
- j/k still works for fine-grained navigation within a page
- Page turns move cursor to first item on new page
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Left/right arrows navigate between pages of alerts
- [ ] Page indicator shows current page, total pages, and total alert count
- [ ] j/k navigation still works within a page
- [ ] Page size matches alertsVisibleLines() calculation
- [ ] First/last page boundaries handled (no wrap)
close_reason: Replaced scroll-offset rendering with page-aligned windows (cursor position / pageSize determines visible page). Added left/right/h/l keys for page navigation. Page indicator shows 'N/M (X alerts)' when content exceeds one page. Footer shows arrow key hint when paginated. Removed dead alertsScrollOffset field.

title: Fix: alert modal expands on line breaks in message text
description:
## Bug

Alert messages containing line breaks cause the modal panel to expand vertically beyond intended bounds. The panel should have a fixed max height and truncate/wrap long messages within the allocated space.

## Reproduction

1. Open bt in global mode with shared Dolt server
2. Press ! to open alerts modal
3. Scroll to an alert with a multi-line message (e.g. "Transitive dependency hook scanning: analyze new deps added by updates")
4. Observe: modal expands to accommodate the line break instead of wrapping within bounds

## Root Cause

renderAlertsPanel() in model_alerts.go doesn't sanitize newlines from alert messages before rendering. The message string is used directly, and lipgloss line counting treats each \n as a new row in the panel.

## Fix

Sanitize alert messages: replace newlines with spaces before rendering, or truncate at the panel's inner width with proper text wrapping.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Alert messages with newlines render on a single line (or wrap within panel width)
- [ ] Modal height stays within ~70% terminal height cap regardless of message content
- [ ] No truncation of useful information - full message readable via wrapping or tooltip
close_reason: Sanitized newlines in alert messages: strings.ReplaceAll(a.Message, "\n", " ") in renderAlertsPanel() before building the display line. Messages now render on a single line within the panel width.

title: Label picker redesign: overlay + UX parity with project filter
description:
## What was done

Design pass to bring label picker in line with the project filter picker:

- Converted from full-screen replacement to overlay compositing (OverlayCenter)
- Round borders with title in the border via RenderTitledPanel
- Removed j/k hints from modal body (cleaner, less noise)
- Wrapping: cursor wraps from last to first and first to last
- Opens to currently applied label filter (cursor positioned, checkmark indicator)
