title: Parallel research across angles: feature-catalog,tui-design,implementation
description: Dispatch parallel agents per angle. Each writes findings to a structured output (bead notes section or doc). Use /superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents.
notes:
# Research Findings Summary

## Angle 1: Feature Catalog Feasibility

### P0 Items
- **Gate fields** (await_type, await_id, timeout, waiters): NOT in model, NOT in SQL queries, NOT in global mode
- **Molecule fields** (ephemeral, template, mol_type): NOT in model, NOT in SQL queries
- **Human flag**: No dedicated indicator - just a label string, no special treatment
- **Rich markdown**: pkg/ui/markdown.go exists, glamour v2 integrated, description renders but design/acceptance_criteria/notes also render in detail panel already (model_filter.go:727-748)

### P1 Items - Already Partially Supported
- **BQL**: FULL implementation in pkg/bql/ (parser, lexer, executor, validator, AST)
- **Comments**: Loaded via batch queries, displayed in detail panel (model_filter.go:764-772)
- **Stale/overdue**: DueDate + UpdatedAt fields exist, analysis/label_health.go has freshness metrics
- **Epic progress**: DepParentChild dependency type, graph analysis tracks parent-child
- **Deferred status**: Full visual support (DEFR badge, custom colors, graph rendering)

### Global Mode
- UNION ALL query uses same IssuesColumns constant - no gate/molecule columns
- Labels, deps, comments loaded via separate UNION ALL queries per database
- Source repo tracked via _global_source column

### BD CLI Execution
- Pattern exists: exec.CommandContext() in cmd/bt/root.go:845-881 and internal/doltctl/doltctl.go
- LookPath("bd") + timeout + CombinedOutput pattern established
- No persistent background worker for bd commands yet

## Angle 2: TUI Design Patterns

### Detail Panel (model_filter.go:615-788)
- 11 sections: title, meta table, labels, triage, search scores, graph analysis, desc/design/acceptance/notes, resolution, dep tree, comments, history
- Labels section at line 649-652 is natural insertion point for gate/human badges
- New sections can be inserted between existing ones

### Badge System (styles.go:190-252)
- RenderPriorityBadge(): P0-P4 with fg/bg color pairs
- RenderStatusBadge(): OPEN, PROG, BLKD, DEFR, PIN, HOOK, REVW, DONE, TOMB
- Color palette: status-specific foreground + background pairs in styles.go:28-96
- Pattern: lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(fg).Background(bg).Bold(true).Render(label)

### List Item Rendering (delegate.go:36-298)
- Layout: [selector] [repo] [type_icon] [prio_badge] [hint] [triage] [status_badge] [score] [ID] [title]
- Second line: [age] [comment_count] [sparkline] [assignee] [labels]
- Triage indicator pattern (lines 228-240) shows how custom indicators integrate

### View Mode Architecture
- 10 view modes: List, Graph, Board, Tree, Insights, Attention, FlowMatrix, History, Sprint, LabelDashboard
- Adding new view: enum constant + struct + dispatch case in model_view.go:89-129
- Moderate effort - each view is self-contained component with SetSize() + View()

### Charm v2 Status
- ALREADY MIGRATED: bubbletea v2.0.2, lipgloss v2.0.2, bubbles v2.1.0, glamour v2.0.0, huh v2.0.3
- No migration work needed

## Angle 3: Implementation Feasibility

### SQL & Columns
- IssuesColumns constant in columns.go:9-14 - single extension point (22 columns)
- Comment on line 6: "One place to update when beads adds columns upstream"
- Scan order must match in dolt.go:84-91 AND global_dolt.go:428-436
- Adding 6 columns: ~10 lines each in dolt.go and global_dolt.go

### Issue Model (types.go:8-36)
- 28 fields, all with json tags
- Pointers for nullable fields (sql.NullString/NullTime/NullInt64 pattern)
- Clone() method at lines 38-97 needs updating for new pointer fields
- 6 new fields needed: AwaitType, AwaitID, Timeout, Ephemeral, Template, MolType

### Data Refresh
- 5-second Dolt polling via BT_DOLT_POLL_INTERVAL_S
- Change detection: MAX(updated_at) comparison
- Debounce: 200ms (BT_DEBOUNCE_MS)
- Temporal cache: hourly, 30 days of daily snapshots
- No changes needed to polling - new columns auto-included in refresh

### Total Implementation Estimate
- ~40-50 lines across 4 files for column extension
- Badge rendering: ~20 lines using existing patterns
- Detail panel section: ~15 lines
- List indicator: ~10 lines
- Total for P0 data plumbing + basic display: ~100 lines
close_reason: All 3 parallel research agents completed. Feature catalog, TUI design, and implementation angles fully investigated with file:line references. Findings written to notes.

title: Poll refresh clears active search results but preserves search text
description:
When bt has an active search/filter and the Dolt poll loop detects changes and triggers a refresh, the search results disappear (shows 'No items') but the search text remains in the input field. Pressing / reopens the search bar with the text still typed in, but results don't populate until the user presses any key (space, delete, etc.) which re-triggers the filter.

**Repro steps:**
1. Open bt in global mode
2. Type / to search, enter a query (e.g., 'bd-0xx')
3. Results appear correctly
4. Wait for a poll refresh to trigger (another session creates/updates a bead)
5. Search results disappear, list shows 'No items'
6. Press / - search bar opens with original text but no results
7. Press any key (space then delete) - results re-appear

**Root cause hypothesis:** The refresh path rebuilds the issues list and filtered view without checking if a search is active. The search query text is preserved in the text input model, but the filtered results slice gets replaced with the full unfiltered list (which then shows nothing because the view expects filtered results) or gets cleared entirely.

**Where to look:**
- background_worker.go TriggerRefresh path
- The message handler for refresh completion in model.go / model_update_data.go
- How search state (query text + filtered results) interacts with issue list replacement
notes:
## Reopened 2026-04-13

Fix in e1a27133 only covered handleSnapshotReady (poll/background refresh). Two additional paths still break search:

1. **Manual refresh (Ctrl+R)** - goes through a different handler (handleFileChanged or handleDataSourceReload), not handleSnapshotReady. Same fix needed there.
2. **FilterApplied state (after pressing Enter on search)** - the applied/locked filter state breaks on both poll and manual refresh. Need to verify the fix handles FilterApplied vs Filtering correctly.

The search preservation fix needs to be applied to ALL refresh paths, not just handleSnapshotReady. Check: handleFileChanged (line 438), handleDataSourceReload (line 388).

### Additional observation during dogfooding:
After manual Ctrl+R refresh, the stale indicator did NOT appear for 30+ seconds. This suggests manual refresh might reset the DoltVerified timestamp, masking bt-6l2c. The poll loop may actually be broken but manual refresh hides it by resetting the freshness clock.
close_reason: Applied filter save/restore pattern to handleFileChanged and handleDataSourceReload, completing coverage of all three refresh paths.

title: Poll loop goes stale after ~10 minutes even in proper global mode
description: After restarting bt with the shared server running (proper global mode, no startup fallback), the TUI still shows stale after ~10 minutes of no bd commands. Manual refresh (R) fixes it and picks up new data. This is different from bt-zsy8/bt-4ckk (startup sequencing). The poll loop at background_worker.go:1968 should send DoltVerifiedMsg every 5 seconds on successful poll, preventing staleness. Either the poll is silently failing (connection timeout after ConnMaxLifetime?) or the DoltVerifiedMsg isn't reaching the model. Need to check: (1) whether doltPollOnce/globalDoltPollOnce errors are being swallowed, (2) whether the 5min ConnMaxLifetime causes issues since each poll creates a fresh connection, (3) whether the poll ticker itself stops.
notes:
## Investigation status (2026-04-13)

### What we know:
- Startup fix (bt-zsy8) works: server starts, data loads, correct SourceTypeDoltGlobal
- Poll loop shows stale after 30 seconds (warn threshold) regardless of startup path
- Happens in both: (1) after startup fix starts server, (2) after clean restart with server already running
- Manual refresh (Ctrl+R) works and picks up new data - so the datasource/connection is fine
- This is independent of the startup sequencing bug - it's the poll loop itself

### Diagnostic next step:
Close bt TUI, restart with trace logging:
```
BT_WORKER_LOG_LEVEL=trace bt 2>/tmp/bt-trace.log
```
Wait 30 seconds for stale indicator, quit bt, then read the trace:
```
cat /tmp/bt-trace.log | grep -i 'poll\|dolt\|error\|fail' | head -30
```
This will show whether the poll loop starts, what errors globalDoltPollOnce returns, and how many consecutive failures occur.

### Key code paths:
- Poll loop: pkg/ui/background_worker.go:1968-2066
- globalDoltPollOnce: pkg/ui/background_worker.go:2100-2129
- DoltVerifiedMsg sent on success: pkg/ui/background_worker.go:2058-2063
- Freshness thresholds: pkg/ui/model.go:212-218 (30s warn, 2min stale)

### Related:
- bt-zsy8 (closed): startup fix shipped, server starts correctly
- bt-4kuh (closed): search preservation on refresh shipped
- bt-zdae (closed): status bar auto-dismiss shipped but has a gap (see below)

### bt-zdae gap discovered during dogfooding:
The auto-dismiss only triggers when renderFooter runs. If nothing causes a re-render after the 5s window, the message persists. 'Showing all projects' and 'Filtering to project...' messages exhibit this. The workerPollTick (bv-9nfy) should trigger re-renders, but if the poll loop itself is broken (this bug), there are no ticks to drive the dismiss. Fix the poll loop first - that may resolve the dismiss gap too since poll ticks drive footer re-renders.
## Trace log results (2026-04-13 13:43)

Ran with BT_WORKER_LOG_LEVEL=trace, redirected stderr to C:/tmp/bt-trace.log. Only one line appeared:
```
INFO global mode: discovered databases count=15 databases=[beads bt cass ...]
```

No poll events (dolt_poll_start, dolt_poll_failed, etc.) appeared in the trace. This means either:
1. The poll loop isn't starting at all (startDoltPollLoop never called)
2. The log events are going to a different output (slog vs log.Printf)
3. stderr redirect isn't capturing the worker's log output

The database discovery line comes from slog.Info in global_dolt.go:158. The poll loop uses w.logEvent() which goes through log.Printf (line 513-515 in background_worker.go). Both should hit stderr.

**Critical clue:** If the poll loop isn't starting, it means the background worker's Start() method at line 605 isn't entering the Dolt branch. This would happen if w.dataSource is nil or its Type isn't SourceTypeDolt/SourceTypeDoltGlobal.

Next step: Add a temporary fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, ...) at background_worker.go:621 right before go w.startDoltPollLoop() to confirm whether that line is reached. Or check if bgEnabled is false at model.go:905.
## Key insight from dogfooding:

Manual Ctrl+R refresh works perfectly - connects to Dolt, loads 2461 issues, shows live data. This proves the server and datasource are fine. The poll loop simply isn't starting at boot.

Combined with the trace showing zero poll events, the diagnosis is: the background worker either isn't getting the datasource, or bgEnabled is false for the global mode path.

Check model.go:905:
```go
bgEnabled := (beadsPath != "" || isDolt) && (backgroundModeRequested || isDolt)
```

In global mode: beadsPath is "" and isDolt depends on ds.Type. If ds is SourceTypeDoltGlobal, isDolt should be true (line 898 checks for both SourceTypeDolt and SourceTypeDoltGlobal). But verify this is actually the case - if isDolt is false, bgEnabled is false, and the poll loop never starts.

This is the ROOT CAUSE of:
- Staleness indicator (no DoltVerifiedMsg without polling)
- Status bar not auto-dismissing (no poll ticks to drive re-renders)
- Search not updating on data changes (no background refresh triggers)

Fix the poll loop start condition and all three resolve.
close_reason: TemporalCacheReadyMsg handler was not re-subscribing to WaitForBackgroundWorkerMsgCmd, breaking the subscription chain after the first few messages. Fixed by adding re-subscribe in the handler.

title: Staleness indicator possibly misleading after shared server fallback
description: When bt boots and falls back from shared server to local project server (bd dolt start), the TUI shows data as stale after ~2 minutes. This may be a false positive - the data isn't actually stale, but the poll loop may not be detecting changes correctly when the server was started via the fallback path rather than the normal shared server path. Possibly related to the DR implementation. Need to investigate whether the poll loop's change detection is wired up correctly in the fallback scenario.
close_reason: Fixed in e1a27133 - shared server startup, search preservation, status bar auto-dismiss

title: bt should start shared Dolt server when port file exists but server is down
description: When bt boots, it finds ~/.beads/shared-server/dolt-server.port (contains 3308) but the server isn't running. Currently prints a warning and falls back to starting a per-project server via doltctl.EnsureServer(). Instead, bt should start the shared server itself. This is also a prerequisite for CRUD-from-TUI - writes shell out to bd, which needs a running server. Need to understand how bd manages shared vs per-project server lifecycle before implementing. Key questions: does bd dolt start always start the shared server? Can shared and per-project coexist? What's the right invocation from bt?
design:
## Implementation Plan (covers bt-zsy8 + bt-4ckk)

### Step 1: Add startSharedDoltServer helper to cmd/bt/root.go

New function ~25 lines. Checks for bd CLI, runs bd dolt start with BEADS_DOLT_SHARED_SERVER=1 env var, waits for TCP readiness with 10s timeout.

### Step 2: Replace auto-global fallback in loadIssues()

At root.go:221-225, replace the warning-and-fallthrough with: call startSharedDoltServer(), retry LoadFromSource(globalSource), set appCtx fields on success. If start or retry fails, fall through to local as safety net.

### Step 3: Test manually

- Kill shared server, run bt -> should see 'Starting shared Dolt server...' then normal global mode
- No port file -> should skip auto-global, load local project normally
- bt --global with server down -> should hard-error (unchanged)
- bd not installed -> should fall through to local with warning

### Closes
bt-zsy8 and bt-4ckk (same root cause - startup sequencing). Spec at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-13-shared-server-startup-fix-design.md has full code snippets.
notes: Connected to CRUD-from-TUI design. The auto-global path in cmd/bt/root.go:211-226 is where the fallback happens. doltctl.EnsureServer() already handles per-project startup - may need a shared-server equivalent. User plans to consult beads session for upstream server lifecycle details.
close_reason: Fixed in e1a27133 - shared server startup, search preservation, status bar auto-dismiss

title: Temporal infrastructure: Dolt AS OF queries + TemporalCache
description: Shared plumbing for all temporal trending features (sparklines, diff mode, timeline view). Add LoadIssuesAsOf method to GlobalDoltReader using Dolt AS OF syntax. Build TemporalCache with background population, TTL-based invalidation, and snapshot metrics computation. Benchmark AS OF performance against production databases before committing to snapshot frequency. This is Phase 2 in the cross-project management plan.
close_reason: Implemented: LoadIssuesAsOf, TemporalCache, SnapshotMetrics, BackgroundWorker integration. 955 lines added, 13 new tests, all 1483 pkg tests passing.

title: Cross-project management gaps - user feedback from GH#3008
description:
## Why

Real user feedback from pewejekubam on gastownhall/beads#3008 identified three capabilities they built on top of the same shared-server architecture bt uses: cross-project aggregation, temporal trending, and DR replication. This validates bt's roadmap direction and highlights specific gaps.

## What

This epic tracks the gap between what that user built and what bt ships. Each child bead addresses a specific capability gap:

- **bt-ph1z.1**: Portfolio health scoreboard (cross-project aggregate metrics)
- **bt-ph1z.2**: Temporal trending sparklines (issue count over time)
- **bt-ph1z.3**: Temporal trending diff mode (what changed between two points)
- **bt-ph1z.4**: Temporal trending timeline view (full timeline visualization)
- **bt-ph1z.5**: Cross-project dependency graphs
- **bt-ph1z.6**: DR documentation and status indicator
- **bt-ph1z.7**: Temporal infrastructure (Dolt AS OF + TemporalCache) - DONE

## Success Criteria

- Gap analysis complete: each capability mapped to bt feature or bead (done)
- Temporal infrastructure landed (bt-ph1z.7 - done, 2026-04-12)
- At least one temporal trending view shipped (bt-ph1z.2 or bt-ph1z.4)
- Portfolio health scoreboard functional in global mode (bt-ph1z.1)

title: Extract sprint view as standalone component
description:
## Why

Sprint view (`pkg/ui/sprint_view.go`) is a full feature with its own rendering, key bindings, and tests but currently lives as methods on Model, reaching into model fields directly. This is one of the bigger remaining monolith decomposition targets from the bt-if3w refactor.

## What

Extract sprint view into a standalone component that receives data through a clean interface, same pattern as the footer extraction (bt-oim6).

Files: `pkg/ui/sprint_view.go`, `pkg/ui/sprint_view_keys_test.go`, references in `model_modes.go`, `model_view.go`, `context.go`, `tutorial.go`.

Not dead code, not a pattern violation - a feature that needs extraction. Discovered 2026-04-10 when Phase 4 (bt-t82t) incorrectly classified sprint view as "dead code" to delete.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Sprint view extracted into a standalone component with a clean data interface
- No direct Model field access from the sprint view component
- Existing tests (`sprint_view_keys_test.go`) updated to work with the extracted component
- All references in `model_modes.go`, `model_view.go`, `context.go`, `tutorial.go` updated
notes: Discovered 2026-04-10 when Phase 4 (bt-t82t) incorrectly classified sprint view as 'dead code' to delete. Corrected: it's decomposition work, not cleanup. Files: pkg/ui/sprint_view.go, pkg/ui/sprint_view_keys_test.go, references in model_modes.go, model_view.go, context.go, tutorial.go.

title: Phase 1.5: Extract footer as component (FooterData struct)
description:
## Why

`model_footer.go` (719 lines) reaches into 35+ Model fields directly. This tight coupling makes it hard to test, modify, or migrate independently. Deferred from Phase 1 (bt-98v9) because the worker ran out of context.

## What

Extract the footer into a proper component that receives a `FooterData` struct rather than accessing Model directly.

Don't convert style calls to renderer-scoped yet - Phase 2 removes the renderer entirely. See plan Phase 1.5 section.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Footer receives data through a `FooterData` struct instead of accessing Model fields
- No direct Model field access from the footer component
- Footer styling continues to use package-level color vars (no renderer-scoped conversion yet)
- `model_footer.go` decoupled from the 35+ Model fields it currently touches
notes: Footer extraction deferred to Phase 4 (bt-t82t). The AdaptiveColor kill in Phase 2 didn't need to touch footer styling patterns - they use package-level color vars which are now resolved. Footer still reaches into 35+ Model fields but this is a decomposition concern, not a color system issue.
close_reason: Extracted footer into FooterData value struct. Model.footerData() extracts ~35 fields into plain values, FooterData.Render() does pure rendering with no Model access. Added 12 tests covering status bar, normal footer, workspace badges, worker levels, alerts, time travel, search/sort badges, hint truncation, update badge, secondary instance. renderFooter() now delegates to footerData().Render(). Files: model_footer.go (rewritten), model_footer_test.go (new).

title: E2E tests bypass JSONL fixtures when shared Dolt server is running
description: E2E tests in tests/e2e/ create isolated JSONL fixtures, but when a shared Dolt server is running (the normal dev environment), auto-global mode detects it and bypasses the fixtures entirely. Tests end up running against 2,308 real issues across 14 databases instead of their curated test data. This causes: (1) timeouts - processing 2k+ issues takes longer than test timeouts, (2) wrong assertions - issue counts and content don't match expectations, (3) non-reproducible results - test behavior depends on what's in the shared server. Root cause: bt's auto-global detection (shared Dolt server on port 3308) overrides the JSONL data source the tests set up. Need a way to force tests into isolated mode - either an env var (BT_DATA_SOURCE=jsonl), a flag (--no-auto-global), or test harness changes to stop/mock the Dolt server detection.
notes: Related to bt-0cht (robot-mode bugs) - the JSON corruption fix exposed this: tests were failing fast on parse errors, now they hang because they hit the real Dolt server. Also related to bt-if3w (refactor) - Phase 1 model decomposition will touch test construction heavily.
close_reason: Fixed: DiscoverSharedServer() returns early when BT_TEST_MODE=1. Also fixed cmd/bt tests missing BT_TEST_MODE in subprocess env.

title: Phase 3: Cobra CLI migration
description: Replace 142-flag pflag monolith with cobra subcommands. bt (TUI), bt global (global TUI), bt robot triage/bql/etc (agent outputs). Flag taxonomy (persistent vs local). PersistentPreRunE for shared data loading. See plan Phase 3.
close_reason: Cobra CLI migration complete. main.go 1708->13 lines. 35+ robot subcommands, export/agents/baseline/version commands. All tests updated and passing.

title: Phase 1: Model decomposition (ViewMode enum, sub-state structs, Update split)
description: Break the 200-field Model struct and 2,396-line Update() into focused sub-structures. ViewMode enum replaces boolean view flags, focus enum split into ViewMode+PaneFocus, ModalState replaces 19 show* booleans, Update() becomes thin router delegating to handler files. See plan Phase 1.
close_reason: Steps 1.1-1.4 complete and merged to main. Step 1.5 (footer extraction) deferred - filed as separate bead.

title: Phase 2: Theme system redesign + AdaptiveColor kill
description: Replace 161 AdaptiveColor occurrences with Charm v2 LightDark pattern via tea.BackgroundColorMsg. Redesign Theme struct to hold resolved colors. Remove lipgloss Renderer (already done mechanically in Phase 0, this kills the AdaptiveColor shim). Pre-compute hot-path styles. See plan Phase 2.
close_reason: AdaptiveColor eliminated from pkg/ui. 174 occurrences across 25 files replaced with resolved color.Color. Theme struct redesigned, dark mode via tea.BackgroundColorMsg, adaptive_color.go deleted.

title: Parameterize BQL dateToSQL before DoltExecutor activation
description:
## Bug

`pkg/bql/sql.go:219-229` `dateToSQL` uses `fmt.Sprintf` to interpolate values into SQL INTERVAL clauses. This is a SQL injection vector.

Currently dead code (SQLBuilder unused) but must be parameterized before DoltExecutor is wired up.

## Root Cause

`fmt.Sprintf` string interpolation instead of parameterized queries in 3 branches of `dateToSQL`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Replace `fmt.Sprintf` with `?` placeholder + `b.params` append in all 3 branches
- No string interpolation of user-supplied values into SQL

See: `security/260409-2034-stride-owasp-full-audit/findings.md` Finding 4.

Blocked by: DoltExecutor work stream. No urgency until SQLBuilder is activated.
notes: Blocked by: DoltExecutor work stream. No urgency until SQLBuilder is activated.
close_reason: Replaced fmt.Sprintf interpolation with ? placeholders + b.params append in all 3 branches of dateToSQL (sql.go:220-228)

title: Add recursion depth limit to BQL parser and executor
description:
## Bug

`pkg/bql/parser.go` `parseFactor` and `pkg/bql/memory_executor.go` `evalExpr` recurse without depth limits. A deeply nested `--bql` query causes stack overflow.

## Root Cause

No recursion depth counter in the parser or executor. Unbounded nesting is accepted and executed.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Depth counter added to both `parseFactor` and `evalExpr` (max 100)
- Deeply nested queries return a clear error instead of crashing
- ~10 lines total change

See: `security/260409-2034-stride-owasp-full-audit/findings.md` Finding 3.
close_reason: Added maxParseDepth=100 counter to parseFactor (parser.go) and maxEvalDepth=100 to evalExpr (memory_executor.go). Parser returns error, executor fails closed.

title: Fix Windows cmd /c start URL injection in export
description:
## Bug

`pkg/export/github.go:610` and `pkg/export/cloudflare.go:347` use `exec.Command("cmd", "/c", "start", url)` which allows shell metacharacter injection (`&`, `|`) in URLs.

## Root Cause

Using `cmd /c start` passes the URL through the Windows shell, which interprets metacharacters. The safe `rundll32 url.dll,FileProtocolHandler` pattern is already used elsewhere in the codebase (`pkg/ui/model_export.go:79`).

## Acceptance Criteria

- Both `github.go:610` and `cloudflare.go:347` use `rundll32 url.dll,FileProtocolHandler` instead of `cmd /c start`
- No URL values passed through shell interpretation

See: `security/260409-2034-stride-owasp-full-audit/findings.md` Finding 1.
close_reason: Replaced cmd /c start with rundll32 url.dll,FileProtocolHandler in github.go and cloudflare.go, matching safe pattern already in model_export.go

title: Human-readable CLI output mode (non-TUI, non-robot)
description:
## Why

bt has two output modes: full TUI (interactive) and robot JSON (`--robot-*` flags for agents). There's no middle ground - no way to just print a human-readable table to the terminal like `bd list` does.

This is particularly important for the global layer since bd has no native global commands. bt is the only tool that can provide cross-project CLI output, and not every use case warrants launching the full TUI.

## What

Human-readable CLI output mode for both local and global contexts:

- `bt --list` (or future: `bt list`) - print issue table to stdout
- `bt --global --list` - cross-project issue table
- `bt --global --stats` - aggregate statistics
- `bt --global --health` - cross-project health summary
- `bt --global --databases` - list all beads projects on shared server (`EnumerateDatabases()` exists in Go but has no CLI surface)

### Design Considerations

- Subcommands (`bt global list`) vs flags (`bt --global --list`)? Subcommands are more discoverable but bt is currently flag-only.
- Output format: plain table by default, `--json` for structured output (distinct from `--robot-*` which has envelope/metadata)
- Should respect terminal width for column sizing
- Pipe-friendly: detect non-TTY and adjust output

See: `docs/brainstorms/2026-04-09-product-vision-brainstorm.md`

## Scope

Priority decision (2026-04-09): Agent (robot) outputs first, human-readable CLI later. Figure out what's actually useful for LLMs, build those, document the human CLI design for future adaptation. The subcommand migration (Cobra) happens as part of refactor (bt-if3w), which gives the architecture to add human CLI outputs later without more restructuring.

## Acceptance Criteria

- At least one human-readable list command works (`bt list` or `bt --list`)
- Global mode has human-readable output (`bt --global --list`)
- Output respects terminal width
- Pipe-friendly (detects non-TTY)
notes: PRIORITY DECISION (2026-04-09): Agent (robot) outputs first, human-readable CLI later. Figure out what's actually useful for LLMs, build those, document the human CLI design for future adaptation. Don't lose context but don't build human-readable tables yet. The subcommand migration (cobra) happens as part of refactor (bt-if3w), which gives us the architecture to add human CLI outputs later without more restructuring.

comments.author: sms
comments.text:
## Global CLI should wrap upstream commands (2026-04-21)

Upstream already provides structured CLI output for audit/stats:
- `bd audit --actor=<pattern>`
- `bd stats --group-by=actor`
- `bd list --json` (standard structured output)

bt's human CLI should wrap these (per-project, then aggregate) rather than reimplementing. For global mode: `bt list --global` structurally resembles `bd list` but adds a source-project column.

**Future extension**: `bt stats --global --group-by=actor` parallels `bd stats --group-by=actor` but aggregates across projects. Enables cross-project actor workload visibility вҖ” paired nicely with bt-4fxz (NEW вҖ” audit/stats integration).

**Related**: bt-4fxz (NEW)

title: Exiting label dashboard ([) leaves split view disabled
description:
## Bug

When entering label dashboard, `isSplitView` is cleared (needed so the dashboard renders full-width). But exiting with esc doesn't restore it, leaving the user in full-width list mode unexpectedly.

Introduced in commit 60f4223c.

## Root Cause

The exit handler for label dashboard doesn't restore `isSplitView = true`. First fix attempt (c8194351) didn't resolve it - the esc key is likely caught by the global handler before reaching the `focusLabelDashboard` dispatch. The key dispatch order needs investigation: the label dashboard exit needs to happen earlier in the chain.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Exiting label dashboard restores `isSplitView` to its previous state
- Other views that clear `isSplitView` checked for the same bug
notes: First fix attempt (c8194351) didn't resolve it. The esc key is likely caught by the global handler before reaching the focusLabelDashboard dispatch. Need to investigate the key dispatch order more carefully - the label dashboard exit needs to happen earlier in the chain.
close_reason: Fixed: global esc and q handlers for ViewLabelDashboard did not restore isSplitView=true. The esc handler at line 648 set mode=ViewList but forgot to restore split view. The q handler had no ViewLabelDashboard check at all, falling through to tea.Quit. Added isSplitView=true to both global handlers. The focus-switch handler already had the fix but was unreachable due to global handler priority.

title: Label dashboard: key dispatch broken - h/H/L fall through to global handlers
description:
## Bug

Multiple issues in the label dashboard (`[` key) view:

1. **Key dispatch broken** - `h`/`H`/`L` fall through to global handlers instead of dashboard-specific actions
2. **Scoping mismatch** - Enter on a label filters to that label but returns 0 results because the dashboard shows labels/counts from the GLOBAL dataset while the list filter runs against PROJECT-scoped issues. So you see "ui (60)" but drill in to updoots and get 0 items. Same bt-dcby scoping mismatch.
3. **Wrong footer** - Footer shows main list view hints, not label dashboard hints
4. The feature is potentially useful for label-based workflows but currently broken at multiple levels

## Root Cause

Key dispatch doesn't check for `focusLabelDashboard` state before falling through to global handlers. Label counts computed from unfiltered `m.issues` while drill-in filters against scoped issues (same root cause as bt-dcby).

## Acceptance Criteria

- `h`/`H`/`L` keys handled by label dashboard when it has focus
- Label counts match the active project filter scope
- Drill-in (enter) returns results consistent with displayed counts
- Footer shows label dashboard-specific key hints

title: Label dashboard ([) doesn't render - isSplitView takes precedence
description: Pressing [ sets focused=focusLabelDashboard but doesn't clear isSplitView. In model_view.go, the isSplitView check (line 103) comes before the focusLabelDashboard check (line 105), so split view always wins and the label dashboard never renders. Fix: either clear isSplitView when entering label dashboard, or reorder the checks. The handler at model.go:3098 sets focus but doesn't clear competing view flags.
notes: Label dashboard now renders (fixed isSplitView). But the view itself is a raw table dump - 318 labels, tiny bar charts, '!' suffix on every label name (rendering artifact?), no filtering/grouping/search, no scrollbar indication. Needs full visual redesign alongside other views.
close_reason: Render bug fixed (60f4223c). Visual redesign tracked separately.

title: Research TUI keybinding conventions (esc vs q, back vs quit, toggle patterns)
description:
## Why

bt needs consistent keybinding conventions before shipping CRUD (bt-oiaj). Read-only TUI can get away with inconsistency because nothing is destructive. Once you can close/edit/create from keyboard, wrong key = wrong action = data mutation. This research informs bt-xavk (help system) and the broader UX rethink.

## What

Research TUI keybinding conventions across established TUIs:

1. Is esc=back and q=quit the standard? (lazygit, htop, k9s do this)
2. Should filter keys toggle or require explicit clear?
3. What's the convention for modal dismiss vs app quit?
4. How do other TUIs handle nested navigation (view > overlay > detail)?
5. What's the convention for "confirm destructive action" in TUIs? (lazygit has the `d` then confirm pattern)

Research targets: lazygit, k9s, htop, charm examples.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Research document summarizing conventions from lazygit, k9s, htop, and charm examples
- Specific recommendations for bt's keybinding model
- Destructive action confirmation pattern proposed for CRUD
- Recommendations applied to bt-xavk (help system) and bt-xron (filter toggle) designs
notes: VISION CONTEXT (2026-04-09): Keybinding conventions become critical with CRUD (bt-oiaj). Read-only TUI can get away with inconsistency because nothing is destructive. Once you can close/edit/create from keyboard, wrong key = wrong action = data mutation. The research here should specifically address: what's the convention for 'confirm destructive action' in TUIs? lazygit has the 'd' then confirm pattern. bt needs this before shipping CRUD.

title: Filter keys (o/c/r) should toggle off when pressed again, not require esc
description:
## Bug

Pressing `o` filters to open issues but pressing `o` again doesn't clear the filter - you have to press esc. Same for `c` (closed) and `r` (ready). These should be toggles: press once to activate, press again to deactivate back to "all".

Esc feels like "I want to leave" not "clear filter".

Also: the "default" recipe shows as "DEFAULT" in the footer badge while the native unfiltered state shows "ALL" - unclear if these are actually different states or the same thing displayed differently.

## Root Cause

Filter key handlers only set the filter state - they don't check whether the filter is already active to toggle it off.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `o`, `c`, `r` toggle: pressing when active deactivates back to "all"
- Esc still works as an alternative way to clear filters
- "DEFAULT" vs "ALL" badge ambiguity resolved (either same behavior or clearly different)

title: Footer status bar: rethink layout, notifications, and information hierarchy
description:
## Why

The footer status bar is the primary information surface in bt, but it's overloaded - notifications, key hints, badges, and transient messages all compete for the same line. As bt evolves into a global command center, this one-line-fits-all approach doesn't scale.

## What

Rethink footer layout, notification handling, and information hierarchy. Current issues:
- Notifications/status messages appear in the same space as key hints and clobber them
- Information density is high but not well prioritized - recipe badge, project badge, issue counts, alerts count, key hints all competing
- Transient messages (reload, filter applied) disrupt the stable footer layout
- May need a separate notification area or toast system above the footer

Related: bt-y0k7 (poll refresh clobbers footer), bt-xavk (help system).

## Acceptance Criteria

- Key hints remain visible when transient messages appear
- Notification area is separate from stable footer content
- Information hierarchy is intentional - most important info gets most prominent placement
- Transient messages don't disrupt stable layout
notes: VISION CONTEXT (2026-04-09): The footer/status bar is becoming more important as bt evolves into a global command center. With smart reload messages (bt-spzz) showing cross-project activity ('bt-lt2h created in beadstui'), the status bar becomes a lightweight activity feed. This needs intentional design - notification area separate from key hints, transient messages that don't clobber stable UI, and clear information hierarchy. The current 'everything competes for one line' approach doesn't scale to global mode. Related bugs (bt-y0k7, bt-ihm0) should be fixed first, then this redesign shapes the foundation for bt-spzz.

title: Mouse click handlers needed throughout TUI
description: Mouse support is currently limited to scroll wheel. Need click handlers for: list items (select), board cards (select/open), graph nodes (select/focus), project picker items (toggle), alerts (open issue), tabs/views (switch). This would make bt much more accessible and match lazygit-style interaction patterns.

title: Responsive layout: adapt views for small terminal dimensions
description:
## Why

bt needs to work in small terminals (e.g. ~80x25 tpane splits). List view is readable at that size, but insights, board, graph, and history views would be unusable.

## What

Adapt views for small terminal dimensions:
- Minimum dimension detection with fallback layouts
- Different view arrangements per terminal size
- Graceful degradation (hide panels, stack instead of side-by-side)

Reference: lazygit handles this well with responsive panel sizing.

## Acceptance Criteria

- bt detects terminal dimensions and selects appropriate layout
- Views degrade gracefully at narrow widths rather than truncating to unreadable content
- At ~80x25, all views remain usable (even if simplified)

title: Poll refresh disrupts TUI views - flash + status bar clobber
description:
## Bug

When Dolt polls and finds changes (e.g. beads updated in another session), the entire view flashes/redraws and the status bar shows a "reload" message that overwrites the footer. Observed in insights (i) and board (b) views.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open bt in insights or board view
2. In another terminal, modify a bead (e.g. `bd update <id> --priority P1`)
3. Wait for Dolt poll interval
4. Observe full-screen flash and footer clobber

## Acceptance Criteria

- Poll refresh does not flash the whole screen
- Changes are surfaced non-disruptively (e.g. "3 issues updated")
- Footer key hints are not clobbered by reload messages
notes: VISION CONTEXT (2026-04-09): This bug is a blocker for bt-spzz (smarter reload messages). The reload message enhancement - especially the global mode extension showing 'bt-lt2h created in beadstui' style activity feed messages - is pointless if the status bar flashes and clobbers the view on every poll. Fix the disruptive refresh first, then make the messages smart. Part of the broader status bar rethink (bt-m9te, bt-it5r).

title: Alerts panel shows all projects even when filtered to one project
description:
## Bug

In auto-global mode, the issue list correctly auto-filters to the current project (e.g. updoots shows 631 issues). But the alerts panel (!) still shows all 199 alerts from all 13 projects. Alerts should respect the activeRepos filter.

Same class of bug as bt-gcuv (priority hints ignoring project filter).

## Acceptance Criteria

- Alerts panel only shows alerts for the currently filtered project(s)
- When activeRepos filter changes, alerts update accordingly
close_reason: Fixed: added visibleAlerts() method that filters by active repo filter + dismissed state. In workspace mode with a project filter, only alerts for issues in selected projects are shown. Footer counts also reflect filtered set. Enter on cross-project alert adds the repo to activeRepos before navigating.

title: Priority hints computed from global issues, not filtered project
description:
## Bug

When filtered to one project in global mode, pressing p shows priority arrows computed from all 2000+ issues across all projects. The recommendations should be scoped to the currently visible/filtered issue set.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Priority hints are computed from the filtered issue set, not the global set
- Changing project filter recalculates priority hints

title: Project picker: add 'deselect all' key (n or A)
description:
## Bug

The project picker has "a" for select all but no inverse. When all projects are selected and you want just one, you have to manually deselect 12 others.

## Acceptance Criteria

- A "deselect all" keybind exists (e.g. n or A)
- User can deselect all, then select the one project they want
close_reason: Resolved by project filter picker redesign (bt-s4b7). 'a' key now toggles all: deselects if any selected, selects if none. Enter-on-none jumps to cursor project.

title: Design auto-global default behavior and TUI mode toggle
description: Current: must explicitly pass --global flag. Proposed: bt auto-detects shared server and connects in global mode by default, but auto-filters to current project based on .beads/metadata.json in cwd. 'w' key widens to all projects. If not in a beads-init dir, defaults to global unfiltered. Also needs: a TUI toggle/keybind to switch between project mode and global mode without restarting. And a TUI settings panel (doesn't exist today) for persisting preferences like default mode.
close_reason: Implemented in 8569b003 and ec6ab47b

title: Alerts panel unusable at global scale (195 alerts, no pagination)
description:
## Bug

At global scale (2168 issues across 13 projects), the alerts panel shows 195 alerts (70 critical, 125 warning) with "149 more below". No way to filter by severity, group by project, paginate, or dismiss. The panel is a wall of "Issue X inactive for N days" lines.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Alerts panel supports severity filtering (critical vs warning)
- Alerts are grouped by project
- Pagination or virtual scroll so all alerts are reachable
- Bulk dismiss capability
notes: VISION CONTEXT (2026-04-09): At global scale (13 projects, 2168 issues), the alerts panel needs to become a first-class cross-project health dashboard, not just a flat list. This connects to the product vision's analytics pillar - bt surfaces insights beads doesn't. Project-grouped alerts with severity filtering would be a unique bt capability. Consider: is this just a bugfix (add pagination) or a redesign opportunity (alerts become the global health view)?
close_reason: Fixed in three changes: (1) Converted alerts from full-page view to popup overlay using OverlayCenter(), matching project/label picker pattern. (2) Added page-aligned pagination with left/right arrows, page indicator, fixed panel height. (3) Added visibleAlerts() filtering by project + dismissed state. Panel is now usable at any scale.

title: Rename 'repo/workspace' to 'project' throughout TUI and codebase
description: Three terms used interchangeably: repo, workspace, project. Decision from dogfooding session: 'project' is the canonical term. 'Repo' implies git. 'Workspace' is ambiguous (VS Code, tmux, etc). Rename: Repo Filter modal title -> Project Filter. workspaceMode -> projectMode (or globalMode). availableRepos -> availableProjects. Footer badge '13 repos' -> '13 projects'. repo_picker.go -> project_picker.go. All user-facing strings, variable names, and doc references.
close_reason: Implemented in 8569b003 and ec6ab47b

title: Refactor pkg/ui monolith + Charm v2 migration (combined stream)
description:
## Why

pkg/ui was an 8,366-line monolith with ~90 fields on the Model struct and a 2,300-line Update() switch. This inherited architecture from beads_viewer was the single biggest structural risk - every change touched the same file, merge conflicts were guaranteed, and no component could be tested in isolation.

## What

Decompose pkg/ui into focused packages and migrate from Charm v1 to v2 in the same pass (touching files once, not twice). Combined because Charm v2 migration forces touching 76 files - the same files the refactor targets.

### Completed
- Phase 0: Mechanical Charm v2 migration (import paths, API renames, compat bridge)
- Phase 0.5: Test foundation for refactor safety net
- Phase 1: Model decomposition (ViewMode enum, DataState/FilterState extraction, ModalType enum, Update() handler decomposition into model_keys.go, model_update_input.go, etc.)
- Phase 2: Kill AdaptiveColor (174 occurrences), resolve all colors to color.Color at load time, dynamic dark/light via tea.BackgroundColorMsg
- Phase 3: Cobra CLI migration (main.go from 1,708 to 13 lines, 35+ robot subcommands)

### Remaining (Phase 4 - bt-t82t)
- Stale refs and golden file validation
- Pre-compute hot-path styles (needs profiling first)
- Footer extraction as FooterData component (bt-oim6)
- Sprint view extraction as standalone component (bt-if3w.1)

## Acceptance Criteria

- All Phase 4 children closed
- No AdaptiveColor anywhere (done)
- All views use ViewMode enum (done)
- All modals use ModalType enum (done)
- Update() decomposed into handler files (done)
- Footer is a standalone component (bt-oim6)
- Sprint view is a standalone component (bt-if3w.1)
notes: Phase 2 (bt-k5zs) and Phase 3 (bt-zt9q) shipped 2026-04-10. Phase 1.5 footer extraction (bt-oim6) deferred to Phase 4. Pre-compute hot-path styles also deferred. Remaining: Phase 4 cleanup (bt-t82t).

title: TUI read/write: create and edit beads from bt
description:
## Why

bt is currently 100% read-only. Evolving it into a read/write interface lets users create, edit, close, and manage beads directly from the TUI without switching to the terminal.

## What

Add write capabilities to bt by shelling out to bd (beads CLI) for all mutations - bt never writes to Dolt directly. This is the final phase of the development arc (ADR-002): Audit -> Stabilize -> Polish -> Charm v2 -> Ship CRUD.

Architecture: shell out to bd, never write to Dolt directly. This keeps bt as a frontend/plugin, not a fork. In global mode, writing to an issue from another project needs directory context (cd to project dir before invoking bd) or bd needs to support --database flag.

All edits made through bt must carry dual attribution:
1. Tool: "bt" or "beadstui" (so you know the change came through the TUI)
2. Human: the user's identity (e.g. "sms") via BD_ACTOR

## Scope

**In scope**: create issues, edit fields (title, description, priority, status, type, labels, notes), close/reopen, add comments, manage dependencies. Full bd mutation surface.

**Not in initial scope**: bulk operations, drag-and-drop reordering, inline editing.

**Phased approach**:
- Phase 1: internal/bdcli/ package (~100 lines) - thin wrapper around bd command execution
- Phase 2: Status changes from TUI - single keystroke actions (Enter/Space to claim, X to close). Zero new UI components.
- Phase 3: Create modal - follows BQL modal pattern (textinput, parse, apply)
- Phase 4: Inline edit - textinput replacing card title, field-by-field in detail panel

The 30-line MVP: intercept X on board, run `bd close <selected-id>`, show "Closed <id>" in status bar, let poll loop refresh.

Depends on global mode (bt-ssk7) being stable first so the write path can handle both local and global contexts. Experimental BDExecutor exists in _tmp/perles/ but is not integrated.

See: docs/brainstorms/2026-04-09-product-vision-brainstorm.md

## Acceptance Criteria

- Users can perform basic CRUD operations on beads from within bt
- All mutations go through bd CLI, not direct Dolt writes
- Mutations carry dual attribution (tool + human)
- Poll refresh picks up changes made through bt
notes:
DESIGN CONTEXT (2026-04-08):
- bt has zero write infrastructure today. No exec.Command calls to bd for mutations.
- _tmp/perles/ has an experimental BDExecutor with full mutation API (UpdateStatus, CloseIssue, CreateEpic, etc.) but it's not integrated.
- bd supports BD_ACTOR env var for authoring attribution. bt edits should be distinguishable.
- Architecture decision: shell out to bd, never write to Dolt directly. This keeps bt as a frontend/plugin, not a fork.
- In global mode, writing to an issue from another project needs directory context (cd to project dir before invoking bd) or bd needs to support --database flag.
- Scope: create issues, edit fields (title, description, priority, status, type, labels, notes), close/reopen, add comments, manage dependencies. Full bd mutation surface.
- Not in initial scope: bulk operations, drag-and-drop reordering, inline editing.

ATTRIBUTION REQUIREMENT (2026-04-08):
- Every mutation through bt must carry DUAL attribution:
  1. Tool: 'bt' or 'beadstui' (so you know the change came through the TUI, not raw bd CLI)
  2. Human: the user's identity (e.g. 'sms') so you know WHO made the change
- bd already supports: BD_ACTOR env var, created_by field on issues/deps/comments, ClosedBySession field
- bt should set something like BD_ACTOR='sms via bt' or use two fields if bd supports it
- Need to investigate: does bd support structured actor metadata? Or just a string? If string, convention like 'sms@bt' or 'sms (bt)' would work
- The goal: looking at any mutation, you can tell both the human and the tool that authored it
IMPLEMENTATION STRATEGY (2026-04-09 brainstorm):

Phased approach - doesn't need to wait for full refactor:

Phase 1: Create internal/bdcli/ package (~100 lines) - thin wrapper around bd command execution. Centralizes arg building, error parsing, timeout handling.

Phase 2: Status changes from TUI - single keystroke actions (Enter/Space to claim, X to close). Zero new UI components. Just key -> bd command -> status toast -> Dolt poll refresh in 0-5s. This is the proof-of-concept that validates the entire write loop.

Phase 3: Create modal - follows BQL modal pattern (textinput, parse, apply). Title, type, priority, description fields. A form, not new architecture.

Phase 4: Inline edit - most complex (textinput replacing card title, field-by-field in detail panel). Builds on proven bd-exec-poll loop from Phase 2.

The 30-line version: intercept X on board, run bd close <selected-id>, show 'Closed <id>' in status bar, let poll loop refresh. That's the MVP.

See: docs/brainstorms/2026-04-09-product-vision-brainstorm.md

title: Cross-project beads: no raw SQL against shared server
description:
## Why

Sessions have been observed using raw `dolt --data-dir` SQL to mutate issues across databases, bypassing bd's attribution, validation, audit trail, and schema management. This is a guardrail gap, not a feature gap - the tooling exists (bd commands within project directories), but nothing prevents or detects the unsafe path.

## What

1. Document the convention: all cross-project writes go through `bd` within the appropriate project directory, never raw SQL against the shared server
2. Add the rule to AGENTS.md and .beads/conventions/reference.md
3. Optionally: add a SessionStart hook that warns if agents attempt `dolt sql` against the shared data-dir

## Acceptance Criteria

- Convention documented in AGENTS.md and .beads/conventions/reference.md
- No open sessions using raw SQL mutations against the shared server
- Hook exists (or is documented as a follow-up) to detect violations
