title: Documentation: Update AGENTS.md and README for hybrid search
description:
## Objective

Comprehensive documentation for hybrid search, ensuring AI agents and human users can effectively use the new ranking features.

## AGENTS.md Updates

### New Section: Hybrid Search

Add after the robot commands table:

```markdown
#### Hybrid Search Ranking

bv supports graph-aware hybrid search that combines text relevance with graph metrics.

**Quick Start:**
\`\`\`bash
# Default text-only search (backward compatible)
bv --search "authentication" --robot-search

# Hybrid search with default weights
bv --search "authentication" --search-mode hybrid --robot-search

# Hybrid search with bug-hunting preset
bv --search "bug" --search-mode hybrid --search-preset bug-hunting --robot-search
\`\`\`

**Hybrid Ranking Formula:**
\`\`\`
score = 0.40 Г— text_relevance + 0.20 Г— pagerank + 0.15 Г— status + 0.10 Г— impact + 0.10 Г— priority + 0.05 Г— recency
\`\`\`

**Available Presets:**
| Preset | Use Case | Key Boosts |
|--------|----------|------------|
| default | Balanced ranking | All factors equally weighted |
| bug-hunting | Finding high-severity bugs | Priority Г—2, Impact Г—1.5 |
| sprint-planning | Finding actionable work | Status Г—1.7 |
| impact-first | Finding high-impact items | PageRank Г—1.5, Impact Г—2 |
| text-only | Pure text search | Text only (backward compatible) |

**JSON Output (hybrid mode):**
\`\`\`json
{
  "mode": "hybrid",
  "preset": "bug-hunting",
  "weights": {"text": 0.30, "pagerank": 0.15, ...},
  "results": [
    {
      "issue_id": "bv-abc",
      "score": 0.87,
      "text_score": 0.85,
      "component_scores": {
        "pagerank": 0.72,
        "status": 1.0,
        "impact": 0.45,
        "priority": 0.8,
        "recency": 0.95
      }
    }
  ]
}
\`\`\`

**When to use hybrid:**
- Bug triage: Use \`bug-hunting\` to prioritize severity
- Sprint planning: Use \`sprint-planning\` to find actionable items
- Impact analysis: Use \`impact-first\` to find bottlenecks
- Simple lookup: Use \`text-only\` (default) for quick searches

**Environment Variables:**
- \`BV_SEARCH_MODE\`: Default search mode (text|hybrid)
- \`BV_SEARCH_PRESET\`: Default preset when mode=hybrid
\`\`\`
```

## README.md Updates

### Add Hybrid Search to Features

```markdown
## Features

- **Graph-aware search** - Hybrid ranking combines text relevance with PageRank, status, priority, and impact
```

### Add Hybrid Search Example

```markdown
## Hybrid Search

Find high-impact issues matching your query:

\`\`\`bash
# Bug hunting: prioritize severity and impact
bv --search "performance" --search-mode hybrid --search-preset bug-hunting --robot-search | jq '.results[:5]'

# Sprint planning: prioritize actionable items
bv --search "api" --search-mode hybrid --search-preset sprint-planning --robot-search | jq '.results[:5]'
\`\`\`

The hybrid search algorithm combines:
- Text relevance (40%) - How well the issue matches your query
- PageRank (20%) - Importance in the dependency graph
- Status (15%) - Actionability (open > in_progress > blocked > closed)
- Impact (10%) - How many issues this blocks
- Priority (10%) - User-assigned priority (P0 > P4)
- Recency (5%) - Time since last update
\`\`\`
```

## Web Export Documentation

Add to Static Site Export section:

```markdown
### Search in Exported Dashboard

The exported dashboard supports graph-aware search:

1. **Text mode** (default): Traditional full-text search
2. **Hybrid mode**: Graph-aware ranking using presets

Toggle between modes using the dropdown in the search bar. The hybrid mode
uses the same ranking algorithm as the CLI, ensuring consistent results.
\`\`\`
```

## CLI Help Text

Ensure --help includes:

```
Search Options:
  --search <query>            Search issues by text query
  --search-mode text|hybrid   Ranking mode (default: text)
                              - text: Pure text/semantic similarity
                              - hybrid: Graph-aware multi-factor ranking
  --search-preset <name>      Weight preset for hybrid mode
                              - default, bug-hunting, sprint-planning,
                                impact-first, text-only
  --search-weights <json>     Custom weights JSON (advanced)
  --robot-search              Output as JSON for AI agents
```

## Code Comments

Add detailed comments in key locations:

```go
// pkg/search/hybrid_scorer.go

// HybridScorer implements graph-aware search ranking by combining
// text relevance with graph metrics (PageRank, status, priority, impact, recency).
//
// The default weighting formula is:
//   score = 0.40Г—text + 0.20Г—pagerank + 0.15Г—status + 0.10Г—impact + 0.10Г—priority + 0.05Г—recency
//
// Use weight presets for common scenarios:
//   - PresetDefault: Balanced across all factors
//   - PresetBugHunting: Boost priority and impact for bug triage
//   - PresetSprintPlanning: Boost status for actionable work
//   - PresetImpactFirst: Boost PageRank for finding bottlenecks
//   - PresetTextOnly: Pure text search (backward compatible)
//
// See AGENTS.md for detailed usage instructions.
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] AGENTS.md has comprehensive hybrid search section
- [ ] README.md updated with examples
- [ ] CLI --help includes hybrid search options
- [ ] Code has detailed documentation comments
- [ ] Web export docs updated
- [ ] jq examples for common use cases

## Files to Modify

- AGENTS.md (major additions)
- README.md (feature list + examples)
- cmd/bv/main.go (help text)
- pkg/search/hybrid_scorer.go (code comments)
- pkg/search/weights.go (code comments)

- Should be done last to ensure accuracy

title: TUI integration: hybrid search toggle
description:
## Objective

Add hybrid search mode to the TUI with a keyboard toggle, allowing users to switch between text-only and graph-aware ranking.

## Background

TUI semantic search (pkg/ui/semantic_search.go) currently uses pure vector similarity. This task adds:
- 'H' key to toggle hybrid mode (consistent with heatmap toggle in graph view)
- Status bar indicator showing current mode
- Hybrid scores displayed in results

## UI Changes

### Keyboard Shortcut

Add 'H' to toggle hybrid mode in search results:
```
Press 'H' for hybrid ranking (currently: text)
```

### Status Bar

Show current mode in footer:
```
[Search: hybrid/default] | [42 results] | n/N: next/prev | H: toggle mode
```

### Result Display

Show hybrid score when in hybrid mode:
```
[0.87] bv-abc: Fix authentication timeout    [P1] [open]
       в””в”Җв”Җ text: 0.85 | pagerank: 0.72 | impact: 0.45
```

## Implementation

### State Addition (pkg/ui/semantic_search.go)

```go
type SemanticFilter struct {
    // existing fields...
    hybridMode   bool
    hybridPreset search.PresetName
    scorer       search.HybridScorer
}

func (f *SemanticFilter) ToggleHybridMode() {
    f.hybridMode = !f.hybridMode
    f.recomputeResults()
}
```

### Modified ComputeSemanticResults

```go
func (f *SemanticFilter) ComputeSemanticResults(query string, issues []model.Issue) {
    textScores := computeTextScores(query, issues) // existing
    
    if f.hybridMode {
        for i, score := range textScores {
            hybrid, _ := f.scorer.Score(score.IssueID, score.Score)
            textScores[i].Score = hybrid.FinalScore
            textScores[i].ComponentScores = hybrid.ComponentScores
        }
        // Re-sort by hybrid score
        sort.Slice(textScores, func(i, j int) bool {
            return textScores[i].Score > textScores[j].Score
        })
    }
}
```

### Key Handler (pkg/ui/board.go or relevant file)

```go
case "H", "h":
    if m.semanticFilter != nil {
        m.semanticFilter.ToggleHybridMode()
        return m, tea.Batch(refreshCmd)
    }
```

## Preset Cycling

Allow cycling through presets with Shift+H:
- default вҶ’ bug-hunting вҶ’ sprint-planning вҶ’ impact-first вҶ’ text-only вҶ’ default

## Success Criteria

- [ ] 'H' key toggles hybrid mode
- [ ] Status bar shows current mode
- [ ] Results re-ranked when mode toggled
- [ ] Hybrid scores visible in results
- [ ] Preset cycling with Shift+H
- [ ] Mode persists during search session
- [ ] No regression in text-only mode performance

## Files to Modify

- pkg/ui/semantic_search.go (core logic)
- pkg/ui/board.go (key handler)
- pkg/ui/footer.go (status display)

title: Add field boosting for title/labels/description
description:
## Objective

Enhance text relevance scoring by weighting matches differently based on which field they occur in.

## Background

Currently, all text matches are weighted equally regardless of where they occur. A title match should rank higher than a description match because titles are more semantically dense.

## Proposed Field Weights

- **ID exact match**: Г—3.0 (user knows exactly what they want)
- **Title match**: Г—2.0 (most important semantic content)
- **Labels match**: Г—1.5 (categorization is valuable)
- **Description match**: Г—1.0 (baseline)

## Implementation Approaches

### Option A: Modify IssueDocument() (simplest)

Repeat important fields in the document:
```go
func IssueDocument(issue model.Issue) string {
    // Repeat title twice for implicit 2x weight
    title := strings.TrimSpace(issue.Title)
    labels := strings.Join(issue.Labels, " ")
    desc := strings.TrimSpace(issue.Description)
    
    return fmt.Sprintf("%s %s %s %s %s", 
        title, title,  // title appears twice (2x weight)
        labels,        // labels once
        desc)          // description once
}
```

### Option B: Multi-field scoring (more precise)

Score each field separately and combine:
```go
func ScoreWithFieldBoost(query, title, labels, desc string) float64 {
    titleScore := embedAndScore(query, title) * 2.0
    labelScore := embedAndScore(query, labels) * 1.5
    descScore := embedAndScore(query, desc) * 1.0
    return max(titleScore, labelScore, descScore)
}
```

### Option C: BM25F (for FTS5)

SQLite FTS5 supports column weights:
```sql
-- Create FTS with column weights
SELECT bm25(issues_fts, 3.0, 2.0, 1.5, 1.0) -- id, title, labels, desc
FROM issues_fts WHERE issues_fts MATCH ?
```

## Recommendation

- For vector search (TUI/CLI): Use Option A (document repetition) - simplest
- For FTS5 search (Web): Use Option C (BM25F weights)

## ID Exact Match Detection

If query looks like an issue ID (matches prefix pattern), check for exact match first:
```go
func IsLikelyIssueID(query string) bool {
    // Match patterns like "bv-abc", "BV-123", etc.
    matched, _ := regexp.MatchString(`^[a-zA-Z]+-[a-zA-Z0-9]+$`, query)
    return matched
}
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] IssueDocument() repeats title for implicit boosting
- [ ] FTS5 query uses column weights
- [ ] ID exact match ranks first
- [ ] Unit tests verify field boosting effect
- [ ] A/B comparison showing title matches rank higher

## Files to Modify

- pkg/search/documents.go (modify IssueDocument)
- pkg/export/sqlite_schema.go (FTS5 column weights)
- pkg/export/viewer_assets/viewer.js (BM25F in query)

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bv-xbar.3 (HybridScorer)
- Can be done in parallel with integration tasks
notes: Boosted IssueDocument fields; weighted bm25 in viewer search; exact ID promotion

title: Final Review: Documentation Consistency Check
description:
# Final Review: Documentation Consistency Check

## Background
After all documentation updates are complete, we need a final consistency check to ensure:
1. README matches in-app help
2. Help modal matches context help
3. Tutorial matches actual behavior
4. All keyboard shortcuts are consistent across all docs

## What to Check

### Cross-Documentation Consistency:
1. **README Keyboard Map** вҶ” **Help Modal** вҶ” **Context Help**
   - Same keys do same things everywhere
   - Same descriptions used

2. **README Feature Descriptions** вҶ” **Tutorial Content**
   - Features described consistently
   - No contradictions

3. **AGENTS.md Blurb** вҶ” **README Blurb Section**
   - AgentBlurb constant matches README section
   - Commands listed are accurate

### Accuracy Verification:
1. Start bv with test data
2. Try every documented keyboard shortcut
3. Verify every documented feature exists
4. Check edge cases mentioned in docs

### Style Consistency:
1. Terminology is consistent (e.g., "issue" vs "bead" vs "task")
2. Key notation is consistent (e.g., "Esc" vs "ESC" vs "escape")
3. Command notation is consistent (e.g., `bv --pages` vs `--pages`)

### Link Verification:
1. All internal links work (if any)
2. External links are valid
3. GitHub repo references are correct

## Deliverables
- Checklist of verifications performed
- List of any remaining inconsistencies
- Final sign-off that docs are consistent

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All keyboards shortcuts consistent across docs
- [ ] No contradictions between README and in-app help
- [ ] Tutorial content matches actual behavior
- [ ] Style is consistent throughout
- [ ] This is the LAST task in the epic

- README.md keyboard control map (lines 2366-2412)

**1. Navigation Shortcuts (g/G)**

- ACTUAL: `g` toggles Graph View in List view (model.go:1658)

### AGENTS.md Status

### Verdict
CANNOT SIGN OFF as fully consistent. The README keyboard map at line 2370 incorrectly claims `g` is for "Jump to Top" when it actually toggles Graph View.

title: E2E Tests: History View Three-Pane Layout
description:
# E2E Tests: History View Three-Pane Layout

## Background
The history view has a responsive three-pane layout:
- Narrow (<100 cols): Two-pane (commits + details)
- Standard (100-150 cols): Three-pane (commits + beads + details)
- Wide (>150 cols): Three-pane with timeline

This needs E2E testing to ensure layout works at various sizes.

## Existing Tests
- pkg/ui/history_test.go - Basic history tests
- pkg/ui/history_selection_test.go - Selection tests

## E2E Tests Needed

### Layout Breakpoints:
1. 80-col terminal вҶ’ two-pane layout
2. 100-col terminal вҶ’ three-pane standard
3. 150-col terminal вҶ’ three-pane wide
4. Dynamic resize вҶ’ layout adapts

### Navigation in Three-Pane:
1. j/k navigates commits in left pane
2. Tab switches focus between panes
3. Middle pane shows beads for selected commit
4. Right pane shows details for selected bead/commit

### Content Display:
1. Commit list shows correct commits
2. Bead list shows beads affected by commit
3. Details pane shows appropriate content
4. Correlation between commits and beads is accurate

### Edge Cases:
1. Very narrow terminal (< 60 cols)
2. Very wide terminal (> 200 cols)
3. Terminal resize while in history view
4. Empty git history
5. Commits with no bead changes
6. Many commits (scrolling)

### Performance:
1. Initial load time reasonable
2. Navigation is responsive
3. Large git history doesn't cause lag

Key tests: TestHistoryModel_DetermineLayout (breakpoints), TestHistoryModel_PaneCount (2/3 panes), TestHistoryModel_ToggleFocusThreePane (navigation), TestHistoryModel_ViewSmallWidth (edge cases 5-20 cols)

title: E2E Tests: AGENTS.md Integration Flow
description:
# E2E Tests: AGENTS.md Integration Flow

## Background
The AGENTS.md auto-injection feature has several flows:
1. First run detection - offer to add blurb
2. Accept flow - blurb added, preference saved
3. Decline flow - preference saved
4. "Don't ask again" flow - permanent preference
5. Legacy detection - offer to upgrade
6. Manual commands (bd agents --add/--remove/--check)

## Existing Tests
- pkg/agents/file_test.go - File operations
- pkg/agents/prefs_test.go - Preference storage
- pkg/agents/integration_test.go - Some integration

## E2E Tests Needed

### Detection Flow:
1. Project with no AGENTS.md вҶ’ offer to create
2. Project with AGENTS.md but no blurb вҶ’ offer to add
3. Project with AGENTS.md with blurb вҶ’ no prompt
4. Project with CLAUDE.md fallback вҶ’ correct detection

### Accept/Decline Flow:
1. User accepts вҶ’ blurb added, preference saved
2. User declines вҶ’ no blurb, preference saved
3. User declines with "don't ask" вҶ’ permanent preference

### Legacy Migration Flow:
1. Project with legacy blurb вҶ’ offer upgrade
2. Accept upgrade вҶ’ legacy removed, current added
3. Decline upgrade вҶ’ legacy preserved

### Manual Commands:
1. `bd agents --check` on project without blurb
2. `bd agents --check` on project with blurb
3. `bd agents --add` on project without blurb
4. `bd agents --add` on project already has blurb (idempotent)
5. `bd agents --remove` on project with blurb
6. `bd agents --show` displays current blurb content

### Preference Persistence:
1. Preferences survive across sessions
2. Different projects have different preferences
3. Corrupted preferences file handled gracefully

### Edge Cases:
1. Read-only file system
2. AGENTS.md exists but not readable
3. AGENTS.md exists but not writable
4. Very large AGENTS.md file
5. AGENTS.md with unusual encoding

## Test Setup
Create temporary directories with various configurations.
Use mock UI for accept/decline simulation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All flows have E2E coverage
- [ ] Manual commands tested
- [ ] Preferences persist correctly
- [ ] Edge cases don't crash
- [ ] Tests are deterministic (no flaky tests)
close_reason: E2E tests implemented in tests/e2e/agents_integration_e2e_test.go with 15 test cases covering: detection flows (6 cases), accept/decline/never-ask flows, preference persistence, legacy migration, blurb removal, idempotency, and edge cases (large files, unicode, read-only dirs, symlinks, empty/whitespace files). All tests pass.

title: E2E Tests: Pages Export with Closed/History Options
description:
# E2E Tests: Pages Export with Closed/History Options

## Background
The pages export (`bv --export-pages`) has several options:
- --pages-include-closed (default: true, changed recently)
- --pages-include-history (default: true)
- --pages-title
- --pages-subtitle

We need E2E tests to verify these options work correctly.

## Existing Tests
- tests/e2e/export_pages_test.go - Basic export tests
- tests/e2e/export_cloudflare_test.go - Cloudflare deployment tests

## Tests Needed

### Option Combinations:
1. Default export (closed=true, history=true)
   - Verify closed issues appear in SQLite
   - Verify git history data exists

2. Export with --pages-include-closed=false
   - Verify closed issues NOT in SQLite
   - Verify open issues still present
   - Verify issue counts are correct

3. Export with --pages-include-history=false
   - Verify history data absent
   - Verify issues still have created/updated timestamps
   - Verify export is smaller (no git data)

4. Export with both excluded
   - Minimal export with only open issues, no history

### Content Verification:
1. SQLite database has correct schema
2. FTS5 index is created and searchable
3. Issue counts in metadata match actual data
4. Title/subtitle appear in generated HTML

### File Structure:
1. All expected files created (index.html, beads.sqlite3, etc.)
2. Vendored JS files present (not CDN references)
3. CSS files present and referenced
4. No broken asset references

### Edge Cases:
1. Export with no issues (empty project)
2. Export with only closed issues (filtered to nothing)
3. Export with very long issue descriptions
4. Export with Unicode in titles/content
5. Export with large number of issues (performance)

## Test Setup
Use testdata fixtures:
- tests/testdata/minimal.jsonl
- tests/testdata/synthetic_complex.jsonl
- Create new fixture with mix of open/closed

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All option combinations tested
- [ ] Content verification for SQLite data
- [ ] File structure validation
- [ ] Edge cases don't cause failures
- [ ] Tests run in <30s each
close_reason: Added 7 comprehensive E2E tests for pages export options: SQLite verification for --pages-include-closed=false, history exclusion, both flags combined, FTS5 searchability, empty project handling, only-closed filtering, and Unicode content support. All tests pass.

title: Unit Tests: Tutorial Page Rendering Validation
description:
# Unit Tests: Tutorial Page Rendering Validation

## Background
The tutorial system (pkg/ui/tutorial.go) has 17+ content pages. We need tests to ensure:

- Scrolling tests
- Some progress tests

### Edge Cases:

2. Very long page content (scrolling)

title: Tests: Legacy Blurb Migration Edge Cases
description:
# Tests: Legacy Blurb Migration Edge Cases

## Background
The legacy blurb detection and migration code (pkg/agents/blurb.go, detect.go) handles detecting old-format blurbs and upgrading them. The core happy path is tested, but edge cases need coverage.

## Existing Tests
- blurb_test.go: ContainsBlurb, GetBlurbVersion, ContainsLegacyBlurb, RemoveLegacyBlurb
- detect_test.go: DetectAgentFile, NeedsUpgrade

## Edge Cases to Test

### ContainsLegacyBlurb edge cases:
1. Content with only 2 of 4 patterns (should return false)
2. Content with patterns but no start header (should return false)
3. Content with start header but only 1 pattern (should return false)
4. Content that looks similar but isn't legacy (like this project's AGENTS.md)
5. Very large files with patterns scattered throughout

### RemoveLegacyBlurb edge cases:
1. Legacy blurb at start of file (no content before)
2. Legacy blurb at end of file (no content after)
3. Legacy blurb with unusual line endings (CRLF)
4. Legacy blurb with no trailing backticks (already added: TestRemoveLegacyBlurbNoTrailingBackticks)
5. Multiple sections after legacy blurb (should preserve all)
6. Content with ### headers that aren't the legacy start

### GetBlurbVersion edge cases:
1. v0 marker (should this exist?)
2. Very high version numbers (v99)
3. Malformed markers (v1a, v-1, v)
4. Multiple version markers in same file (take first? error?)

### Integration scenarios:
1. UpdateBlurb on file with BOTH legacy AND current blurb (should remove both, add current once)
2. Detect + Update flow on real-world AGENTS.md files
3. Atomic write safety (file permissions preserved)

## Test Data
Consider adding testdata files:
- testdata/legacy_blurb_standard.md - Standard legacy format
- testdata/legacy_blurb_no_backticks.md - Legacy without code fence
- testdata/not_legacy_documentation.md - Similar content but not legacy

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All edge cases have test coverage
- [ ] Tests use table-driven style
- [ ] Edge cases are documented in test names
- [ ] No panic on malformed input
close_reason: Added 27 edge case tests for ContainsLegacyBlurb, RemoveLegacyBlurb, GetBlurbVersion, RemoveBlurb, ContainsAnyBlurb. All tests pass.

description:
# Help Modal: Audit and Update Keyboard Shortcuts

## Background
The help modal (?) shows keyboard shortcuts, but as features evolve, the listed shortcuts may become outdated or incomplete.

## What to Verify

### Cross-Reference These Sources:
1. **pkg/ui/model.go** - Actual key handling in Update() method
2. **pkg/ui/context_help.go** - Context-specific help content
3. **README.md** Keyboard Control Map (line ~2306)
4. **In-app help modal content** - What actually displays

### Known Shortcuts to Verify:
**Global:**
- `?` - Help overlay
- `` ` `` - Tutorial
- `~` - Context help
- `q` - Quit
- `Esc` - Close/back
- `;` - Shortcuts sidebar toggle

**Navigation:**
- `j/k` - Up/down
- `h/l` - Left/right (in some views)
- `g/G` - Top/bottom

*   **History View:** Press `h` to see the timeline of changes, correlating git commits with bead modifications. Wider terminals show a three-pane layout with commits, affected beads, and details.

title: README: Document AGENTS.md Auto-Injection Feature
description:
# README: Document AGENTS.md Auto-Injection Feature

## Feature Overview
When bv detects a beads project without the standard blurb in AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md, it offers to automatically inject usage instructions for AI coding agents. This helps AI agents work effectively with beads projects without manual setup.

## Current State
- The blurb itself is documented in README (lines 90-180)
- The AUTO-INJECTION feature is NOT documented
- The `bd --agents` command is not mentioned
- Legacy blurb detection/migration is not documented

## What to Document

### New Section: "AGENTS.md Integration" (after the blurb section)
```markdown
### Automatic Integration

bv can automatically add the above instructions to your project's AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md:

- On first run, bv offers to inject the blurb if not present
- Choose "Yes" to add, "No" to skip, or "Don't ask again" to remember
- Preferences are stored per-project in ~/.config/bv/agent-prompts/

**Supported Files** (checked in order):
1. AGENTS.md (preferred)
2. CLAUDE.md
3. agents.md
4. claude.md

**Manual Control:**
- `bd agents --show` - Display current blurb content
- `bd agents --check` - Check if blurb is present
- `bd agents --add` - Add blurb to agent file
- `bd agents --remove` - Remove blurb from agent file
```

### Note About Versioning
The blurb uses HTML comment markers for version tracking:
```
<!-- bv-agent-instructions-v1 -->
... content ...
<!-- end-bv-agent-instructions -->
```

This allows future blurb updates to be detected and offered.

## Style Guidelines
- Frame as a helpful automation feature
- Don't mention "legacy" migration - that's internal
- Keep it brief - the blurb itself is already documented

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Auto-injection behavior is documented
- [ ] Supported file names are listed
- [ ] Manual control commands are documented
- [ ] Versioning approach is briefly explained
close_reason: Documented auto-injection, supported files, bd agents commands, and version tracking in README

title: README: Document Pure-Go SQLite and FTS5
description:
# README: Document Pure-Go SQLite and FTS5

## What Changed
We switched from `mattn/go-sqlite3` (CGO-based, requires C compiler) to `modernc.org/sqlite` (pure-Go). This means:
- FTS5 (full-text search) is available out of the box
- No CGO build requirements
- Cross-compilation works seamlessly
- The static site export has instant search via FTS5

## Current State
- Line 2510 mentions modernc.org/sqlite in acknowledgments
- Lines 1632 mentions FTS5 briefly: "SQLite FTS5 enables instant search"
- No explanation of WHY this matters or HOW it benefits users

## Changes Needed

### In "Static Site Export" section (~line 1590):
Add a note explaining the search capability:
- "Powered by SQLite FTS5 full-text search"
- "Search works offline - no server required"
- "Instant results as you type"

### In acknowledgments or architecture section:
Explain the technical choice:
- Pure-Go means no C compiler needed
- Cross-compile to any platform
- FTS5 available without special build flags

## Style Guidelines
- Don't frame as "we recently changed" - write as if it was always this way
- Keep technical details appropriate for the README audience
- Maintain the existing formatting/tone

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Static Site Export section mentions FTS5 search capability
- [ ] Benefits of pure-Go SQLite are documented (no CGO, cross-compile)
- [ ] No references to "migration" or "change"

title: Insights: Enhanced Heatmap Visualization
description:
## Overview
Dramatically improve the heatmap visualization in Insights view using advanced lipgloss features.

## Current State
```
рҹ“Ҡ Priority Heatmap  Score vs Depth (H=toggle view)
      в”Ӯ  0-.2  .2-.4  .4-.6  .6-.8  .8-1
в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
  D=0 в”Ӯ    В·     в–Ҳ2    в–Ҳв–Ҳ5    в–Ҳ3     В·
 D1-2 в”Ӯ    В·      В·    в–Ҳв–Ҳ8   в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ12   в–Ҳ4
 D3-5 в”Ӯ    В·      В·     в–Ҳ3    в–Ҳв–Ҳ7    в–Ҳ2
D6-10 в”Ӯ    В·      В·      В·     в–Ҳ2     В·
 D10+ в”Ӯ    В·      В·      В·      В·     В·

Legend: В· empty в–Ҳ few в–Ҳв–Ҳ some в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ many/urgent
```

Problems:
- ASCII table borders
- Cluttered cell content (в–Ҳ2)
- No background colors
- No interactivity
- Cramped legend

## Enhanced Design

### Visual Improvements

#### 1. Solid Background Cells
Use `lipgloss.Background()` for true heatmap cells:
```go
cellStyle := t.Renderer.NewStyle().
    Background(heatColor).
    Foreground(contrastColor).  // White or black for readability
    Padding(0, 1).
    Align(lipgloss.Center)
```

#### 2. Color Gradient
Implement proper heat gradient (cool вҶ’ hot):
```
0 items:    #1a1a2e (dark blue/gray)
1-2 items:  #16213e (navy)
3-5 items:  #0f4c75 (blue)
6-10 items: #e94560 (coral)
10+ items:  #ff2e63 (hot pink/red)
```

Or use existing theme colors intelligently:
```go
func GetHeatGradient(intensity float64, t Theme) lipgloss.Color {
    // Interpolate between t.Secondary (cold) and t.Primary (hot)
}
```

#### 3. Clean Cell Content
Show EITHER:
- Just the count number (with background color indicating intensity)
- Just a solid block (for minimal look)

Recommended: Number with colored background
```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ  2  в”Ӯ  5  в”Ӯ  3  в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ  <- Numbers only, background = heat
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ  8  в”Ӯ 12  в”Ӯ  4  в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

#### 4. Proper Axis Labels
```
         в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ Priority Score в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
         Low                            High
        в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
 Leaf   в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ  2  в”Ӯ  5  в”Ӯ  3  в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ
 в”Җв”Җв”Җ    в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
 Depth  в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ  8  в”Ӯ 12  в”Ӯ  4  в”Ӯ
 вҶ“      в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
 Root   в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ     в”Ӯ  3  в”Ӯ  7  в”Ӯ  2  в”Ӯ
        в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

#### 5. Marginal Totals
Show row/column sums:
```
        0-.2  .2-.4  .4-.6  .6-.8  .8-1  в”Ӯ Total
  D=0     -      2      5      3     -   в”Ӯ  10
 D1-2     -      -      8     12     4   в”Ӯ  24
 D3-5     -      -      3      7     2   в”Ӯ  12
D6-10     -      -      -      2     -   в”Ӯ   2
 D10+     -      -      -      -     -   в”Ӯ   0
в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
Total     0      2     16     24     6   в”Ӯ  48
```

### Interactivity Improvements

#### Cell Selection
- j/k: Move selection between rows
- h/l: Move selection between columns
- Enter: Drill down into selected cell (show issues in that bucket)

#### Drill-Down View
When pressing Enter on a cell:
```
в”Ңв”Җ Issues in Score .6-.8, Depth 1-2 (12 items) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ                                                          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ bt-123  P1  Fix auth timeout                             в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ bt-456  P1  API rate limiting                            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ bt-789  P2  Session handling                             в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ ...                                                      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ [Esc: back]  [Enter: view issue]                         в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

### Layout Improvements

#### Responsive Sizing
- Calculate cell width based on available space
- Minimum cell width: 5 chars
- Expand cells on wide terminals

#### Legend Redesign
Vertical legend on the side or expanded horizontal:
```
Heat Scale:
  в–‘в–‘в–‘  0     (empty)
  в–’в–’в–’  1-2   (few)
  в–“в–“в–“  3-5   (some)
  в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ  6-10  (many)
  в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ  10+   (hot)  вҶҗ Brightest
```

## Implementation

### New Model State
```go
type InsightsModel struct {
    // Existing...
    
    // Heatmap navigation
    heatmapRow    int  // Selected row (depth bucket)
    heatmapCol    int  // Selected column (score bucket)
    heatmapDrill  bool // In drill-down view?
    heatmapIssues []string // IDs in selected cell
}
```

### New Methods
```go
func (m *InsightsModel) HeatmapMoveUp()
func (m *InsightsModel) HeatmapMoveDown()
func (m *InsightsModel) HeatmapMoveLeft()
func (m *InsightsModel) HeatmapMoveRight()
func (m *InsightsModel) HeatmapEnter() // Drill into cell
func (m *InsightsModel) HeatmapBack()  // Exit drill-down

func getHeatGradientColor(intensity float64, t Theme) lipgloss.TerminalColor
func getContrastColor(bg lipgloss.TerminalColor) lipgloss.TerminalColor
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cells use background colors for heat visualization
- [ ] Proper color gradient from cool to hot
- [ ] Cell content is clean (just numbers)
- [ ] Axis labels explain dimensions
- [ ] Row/column totals shown (marginals)
- [ ] Cell selection with j/k/h/l navigation
- [ ] Drill-down into cell shows issue list
- [ ] Responsive layout adapts to terminal width
- [ ] Legend clearly explains color scale
close_reason: Closed

title: Cass Integration Safety Tests
description:
# Cass Integration Safety Tests

## Purpose
Verify the critical safety guarantee: cass integration is completely invisible when cass is not installed or not working. This is the most important requirement of the entire feature.

## Background

### The Core Promise
From the epic: "Users without cass must NEVER see error messages, 'no sessions found' states, broken UI, or loading indicators for cass features."

This bead ensures we have comprehensive tests proving this guarantee.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: No cass Binary
**Environment:** cass not in PATH
**Expected:** All bv functionality works exactly as before

Tests:
- [ ] Startup completes normally (no delay)
- [ ] No error messages in any view
- [ ] Status bar shows no session indicator
- [ ] V key does nothing (no empty modal, no error)
- [ ] History view shows commits only
- [ ] No loading spinners or delays

### Scenario 2: cass Health Check Fails  
**Environment:** cass binary exists but returns exit 1 or 3
**Expected:** Same as no cass

Tests:
- [ ] Detection marks status as unhealthy
- [ ] All features silently disabled
- [ ] No user-visible error messages
- [ ] Re-check after TTL (5 min) works correctly

### Scenario 3: cass Search Times Out
**Environment:** cass healthy but search hangs
**Expected:** Graceful degradation

Tests:
- [ ] 5-second timeout enforced
- [ ] Empty results returned (not error)
- [ ] UI not blocked during timeout
- [ ] Can still navigate beads while search pending
- [ ] No "loading" indicator shown

### Scenario 4: cass Returns Malformed JSON
**Environment:** cass outputs garbage/invalid JSON
**Expected:** Treated as no results

Tests:
- [ ] JSON parse errors caught and logged (debug only)
- [ ] Empty results returned to UI
- [ ] No panic or crash
- [ ] Subsequent searches still work

### Scenario 5: cass Returns Empty Results
**Environment:** cass healthy, no matching sessions
**Expected:** No indicator shown

Tests:
- [ ] Status bar shows no session indicator
- [ ] V key does nothing
- [ ] No 'no sessions found' message
- [ ] No visual difference from cass-not-installed state

### Scenario 6: cass Becomes Unhealthy Mid-Operation (Race Condition)
**Environment:** cass healthy at detection, fails during search
**Expected:** Graceful degradation

Tests:
- [ ] Detection cache is NOT invalidated on search failure (avoid thrashing)
- [ ] Empty results returned for that search
- [ ] Subsequent operations continue normally
- [ ] If failures persist, detection eventually re-checks

### Scenario 7: Startup Time Impact
**Environment:** cass not installed
**Expected:** Zero startup time impact

Tests:
- [ ] Measure startup time WITHOUT cass
- [ ] Measure startup time WITH cass (healthy)
- [ ] Difference must be < 50ms (detection is async or deferred)
- [ ] Detection does NOT block TUI rendering

## Implementation

### Test Infrastructure
```go
// MockCassExecutor for controlled testing
type MockCassExecutor interface {
    LookPath(name string) (string, error)
    RunHealth(ctx context.Context) (int, string, error)
    RunSearch(ctx context.Context, args []string) ([]byte, error)
}

type TestCassExecutor struct {
    HealthExitCode  int
    HealthOutput    string
    HealthDelay     time.Duration
    SearchOutput    []byte
    SearchDelay     time.Duration
    SearchError     error
    LookPathError   error
}
```

### Testing Functions
```go
func TestInvisibleWithoutCass(t *testing.T) {
    exec := &TestCassExecutor{LookPathError: os.ErrNotExist}
    // Start bv with mock executor
    // Verify no cass-related UI elements
    // Verify no errors logged at INFO level or above
}

func TestGracefulDegradation(t *testing.T) {
    exec := &TestCassExecutor{
        SearchDelay: 10 * time.Second, // Will timeout
    }
    // Verify UI remains responsive during timeout
    // Verify empty results after timeout
}

func TestStartupTimeImpact(t *testing.T) {
    // Measure baseline startup time
    // Measure with mock cass (slow health check)
    // Assert difference < 50ms
}

func TestRaceCondition(t *testing.T) {
    exec := &TestCassExecutor{
        HealthExitCode: 0, // Healthy initially
    }
    // Start operation
    // Mid-operation, make search fail
    // Verify graceful handling
}
```

### E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# test_cass_invisible.sh
set -e

# Test 1: No cass in PATH
echo "Testing without cass..."
PATH="/usr/bin:/bin" bv --version  # Should work

# Test 2: With broken cass
echo "Testing with broken cass..."
cat > /tmp/fake-cass << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
exit 3
EOF
chmod +x /tmp/fake-cass
PATH="/tmp:$PATH" bv --version  # Should work

# Test 3: Startup time comparison
echo "Measuring startup times..."
time1=$(PATH="/usr/bin:/bin" time -p bv --version 2>&1 | grep real | awk '{print $2}')
# Compare with baseline

echo "All safety tests passed!"
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 7 scenarios have passing tests
- [ ] Tests run in CI (with mocked cass)
- [ ] No test pollution (tests clean up mock state)
- [ ] Coverage report shows safety paths tested
- [ ] Startup time test has measurable threshold

## Test Organization
```
pkg/cass/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ detect_test.go       # Scenario 1, 2, 7
в”ңв”Җв”Җ search_test.go       # Scenario 3, 4
в”ңв”Җв”Җ correlate_test.go    # Scenario 5, 6
в””в”Җв”Җ integration_test.go  # E2E scenarios

test/
в””в”Җв”Җ cass_invisible_test.go  # Safety guarantee integration tests
```

## Why This Matters
This is not optional. The user explicitly stated: "nothing can break or look incomplete if cass isn't detected." These tests are the **proof** we meet that requirement. Every scenario must pass before the epic can be closed.
close_reason:
All 7 safety scenarios comprehensively tested in pkg/cass/safety_test.go (26KB, 60+ test cases):
1. No cass binary - TestSafety_NoBinary_* (6 tests)
2. Health check fails - TestSafety_HealthCheckFails_* (4 tests)
3. Search timeout - TestSafety_SearchTimeout_* (4 tests)
4. Malformed JSON - TestSafety_MalformedJSON_* (8 tests)
5. Empty results - TestSafety_EmptyResults_* (2 tests)
6. Race condition - TestSafety_RaceCondition_* (3 tests)
7. Startup time - TestSafety_StartupTime_* (4 tests)
Plus TestSafety_EndToEnd_InvisibilityGuarantee covering all scenarios in integration. All tests pass, proving the core promise: cass is completely invisible when unavailable.

title: Session Preview Modal (V key)
description:
# Session Preview Modal (V key)

## Purpose
When user presses **V** on a bead with correlated sessions, show a modal with session previews. This is the primary interaction for exploring related coding context.

## Key Binding Rationale
**Why V?** The obvious choice 'C' (for Cass) is already used:
- In List view: 'c' filters to closed issues
- In History view: 'C' cycles confidence threshold

**V = "View sessions"** is:
- Semantically meaningful (verb: view)
- Not used elsewhere in bv
- Easy to remember and discover

## Background

### UX Decision: Toggle vs Hold
Original plan suggested "hold key to show, release to dismiss." After consideration:

**Problems with hold:**
- Terminal key detection unreliable for hold
- Awkward UX (must keep holding)
- Inconsistent with other bv modals (all use toggle)

**Decision: Toggle mode**
- Press V once вҶ’ show modal
- Press V again, Esc, or Enter вҶ’ dismiss
- Consistent with help overlay, quit confirm, etc.

### Modal Design
```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

в”Ӯ                                                                в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  [1] Claude Code вҖў 2 hours ago                                в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ      Matched via: keyword "token refresh"                      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ You: The auth token keeps timing out after 5 minutes     в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Claude: Looking at your token refresh logic, I see the   в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ issue. The refresh window is set to exactly the expiry...в”Ӯ в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                                в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  [2] Cursor вҖў yesterday                                       в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ      Matched via: keyword "backoff"                            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ӯ

в”Ӯ                                                                в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  (2 more sessions - run: cass search "bv-abc123")             в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                                в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  [j/k] Navigate    [y] Copy search cmd    [V/Esc] Close       в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

### Modal Content
- Header: emoji, title, bead ID
- Per session:
  - Agent name and timestamp
  - Match reason (how we found it)
  - Snippet preview (2-3 lines of relevant content)
- Footer: action hints and search command for more

## UX Decision: No "Open in cass" Action

**Original plan:** Press 'o' to open session in cass TUI.

**Problem:** cass TUI would take over the terminal, which is confusing:
- User loses bv context
- After exiting cass, where are they?
- Platform differences in terminal handling

**Decision for MVP:** Remove "open in cass" feature entirely. Instead:
- Show preview snippets in modal (usually enough)
- Display search command user can copy/run later
- Press 'y' copies the search command to clipboard

**Future enhancement:** Could add subprocess handling to suspend bv, run cass, then resume. But this is complex and not MVP.

## Implementation

### Modal Component
```go
type CassSessionModal struct {
    beadID     string
    sessions   []cass.SearchResult
    reason     string // Overall match reason
    selected   int    // For keyboard navigation
    searchCmd  string // "cass search \"bv-abc123\""
    theme      Theme
    width      int
    height     int
}

func NewCassSessionModal(beadID string, hint *cass.CorrelationHint, theme Theme) CassSessionModal

func (m CassSessionModal) Update(msg tea.Msg) (CassSessionModal, tea.Cmd)
func (m CassSessionModal) View() string
```

### Key Bindings
| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| V | Toggle modal (close if open) |
| Esc | Close modal |
| Enter | Close modal |
| y | Copy search command to clipboard |
| j/k | Navigate between sessions (if >1) |

### Clipboard Copy
```go
func copyToClipboard(text string) error {
    // Use pbcopy on macOS, xclip on Linux
    // Silent failure if clipboard unavailable
}
```

### Snippet Rendering
- Extract 2-3 most relevant lines from session
- Show role prefix (You:, Claude:, Cursor:, etc.)
- Truncate long lines with "..."
- Highlight matched keywords (optional, future)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] V toggles modal on/off
- [ ] Modal shows session previews
- [ ] Match reason displayed
- [ ] Search command shown for manual use
- [ ] 'y' copies search command (with visual feedback)
- [ ] Esc/Enter/V close modal
- [ ] Modal centered and styled consistently
- [ ] Does not appear if no sessions (V key does nothing)

## Visual Design Considerations
- Width: min(70, terminal_width - 10)
- Height: adaptive based on session count
- Max 3 sessions shown (others summarized)
- Snippet box uses subtle border
- Agent name uses muted color for visual hierarchy

## Edge Cases
- Terminal too small for modal вҶ’ show simplified version
- Session file no longer exists вҶ’ show "(session unavailable)"
- Very long snippet вҶ’ truncate intelligently
- No sessions вҶ’ V key does nothing (no empty modal)
- Clipboard unavailable вҶ’ show message "copied\!" anyway, or fallback

## Testing Strategy
- Test modal toggle behavior
- Test keyboard navigation between sessions
- Test clipboard copy command building
- Test snippet truncation
- Visual regression test for layout

**New files:**

**Modal features:**

title: Cass Result Caching System
description:
# Cass Result Caching System

## Purpose
Avoid redundant cass searches by caching correlation results. Critical for performanceвҖ”without caching, navigating the issue list would trigger many slow external calls.

## Background

### Why Cache?
- cass searches take 50-500ms each
- Users navigate between beads frequently
- Same bead is often selected multiple times
- Correlation results change slowly (sessions rarely deleted)

### Cache Requirements
- Fast lookup (sub-ms)
- Bounded memory (max 100 entries)
- Automatic expiration (10-minute TTL)
- Thread-safe (concurrent UI access)
- LRU eviction (keep most recently used)

## Implementation

### Cache Structure
```go
type CacheEntry struct {
    Hint      *CorrelationHint
    CachedAt  time.Time
    ExpiresAt time.Time
}

type Cache struct {
    entries map[string]*CacheEntry  // key = bead ID
    order   []string                 // LRU order (newest at end)
    maxSize int
    ttl     time.Duration
    mu      sync.RWMutex
}
```

### Cache Operations
```go
// Get returns cached hint or nil if miss/expired
func (c *Cache) Get(beadID string) *CorrelationHint

// Set stores a correlation hint
func (c *Cache) Set(beadID string, hint *CorrelationHint)

// Invalidate removes a specific entry
func (c *Cache) Invalidate(beadID string)

// Clear removes all entries
func (c *Cache) Clear()

// Stats returns cache statistics (for debugging)
func (c *Cache) Stats() CacheStats
```

### Eviction Policy
When cache is full:
1. Remove expired entries first
2. If still full, remove least recently used
3. Never exceed maxSize

### Cache Key Design
Simple: use bead ID directly (`"bv-abc123"`)

Could extend later with workspace prefix if supporting multiple projects.

## Configuration
```go
const (
    DefaultCacheSize = 100       // Max entries
    DefaultCacheTTL  = 10 * time.Minute
)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] LRU eviction works correctly
- [ ] TTL expiration works
- [ ] Thread-safe for concurrent access
- [ ] Get/Set operations are O(1) for hit
- [ ] Memory bounded by maxSize
- [ ] Clear operation works

## Testing Strategy
- Test cache hit/miss
- Test TTL expiration
- Test LRU eviction order
- Test concurrent access (race detector)
- Benchmark operations
close_reason: Implemented LRU cache with TTL expiration, bounded memory (100 entries max), O(1) Get/Set, thread-safe concurrent access. 19 cache tests pass with -race.

title: Session-Bead Correlation Engine
description:
# Session-Bead Correlation Engine

## Purpose
The "brain" of the integrationвҖ”intelligently match cass sessions to beads using multiple strategies. This determines which sessions are relevant to which issues.

## Background

### The Correlation Challenge
Sessions and beads are created independently:
- Users do not explicitly tag sessions with bead IDs
- Session titles may not match bead titles exactly
- Timestamps may not align perfectly
- Same session might relate to multiple beads

We need smart heuristics to find meaningful connections.

### Correlation Quality Matters
False positives (irrelevant sessions shown) erode trust. False negatives (missing relevant sessions) reduce value. We optimize for **precision over recall**вҖ”better to show fewer, highly relevant sessions than many questionable ones.

## Correlation Strategies

### Strategy 1: ID Mention (Highest Confidence)
Search for the bead ID literally in sessions:
```go
query := fmt.Sprintf("%q", bead.ID) // e.g., "bv-abc123"
```
**Score bonus**: +100 (definitive match)
**When useful**: When users or agents explicitly mention issue IDs

### Strategy 2: Keyword Extraction (Medium Confidence)
Extract meaningful words from bead title, search with time filter:
```go
keywords := extractKeywords(bead.Title)
// "Fix token refresh timeout" вҶ’ ["token", "refresh", "timeout"]
query := strings.Join(keywords[:3], " ")
```
**Score bonus**: +30 to +60 (depends on match quality)
**When useful**: Most common caseвҖ”thematic matching

### Strategy 3: Timestamp Proximity (Lower Confidence)
Sessions occurring near bead activity (creation, updates, closure):
```go
days := daysBetween(bead.CreatedAt, bead.ClosedAt)
if days == 0 { days = 1 }
opts.Days = days + 1 // Search window
```
**Score bonus**: +10 to +30 (time proximity weighting)
**When useful**: Closed beads where work was recent

### Strategy 4: Combined Scoring
Blend strategies for final ranking:
```go
finalScore := baseScore * (1 + timeDecay) * workspaceBoost
// timeDecay: Recent sessions score higher
// workspaceBoost: Same workspace scores 2x
```

## Implementation

### Correlation Hint Type
```go
type CorrelationHint struct {
    BeadID       string          // Which bead this is for
    SessionCount int             // Total matching sessions
    TopSessions  []SearchResult  // Up to 3 best matches
    TopScore     float64         // Highest session score
    Reason       string          // "id_mention", "keywords", "timestamp"
    Keywords     []string        // Keywords used (for display)
}
```

### Correlation Flow
```
For each bead:
1. Check cache first (return if fresh hit)
2. Try ID mention search
   в””в”Җ Found вҶ’ return with high confidence
3. Extract keywords from title
4. Search keywords with time filter
5. Filter by workspace (if available)
6. Score and rank results
7. Cache and return top matches
```

### Keyword Extraction
```go
func extractKeywords(title string) []string {
    // Remove common words: "the", "a", "and", "fix", "add", "update", etc.
    // Remove short words (< 3 chars)
    // Lowercase and dedupe
    // Return up to 5 keywords
}

var stopWords = map[string]bool{
    "the": true, "a": true, "an": true, "and": true, "or": true,
    "fix": true, "add": true, "update": true, "remove": true,
    "implement": true, "create": true, "delete": true,
    "for": true, "to": true, "in": true, "on": true, "at": true,
    "bug": true, "issue": true, "feature": true, "task": true,
}
```

### Workspace Filtering
Critical for relevanceвҖ”sessions from unrelated projects pollute results:
```go
func workspaceFromBeadsPath(beadsPath string) string {
    // /path/to/project/.beads/beads.jsonl вҶ’ /path/to/project
    return filepath.Dir(filepath.Dir(beadsPath))
}
```

## Scoring Configuration

### вҡ пёҸ TUNABILITY NOTE
All scoring weights should be **exported constants** that can be adjusted based on real-world usage. Initial values are educated guesses; expect to tune after deployment.

```go
// pkg/cass/scoring.go - All values are tunable
const (
    // Base scores by match type
    ScoreIDMention      = 100  // Definitive evidence
    ScoreExactKeyword   = 50   // Strong thematic link  
    ScorePartialKeyword = 30   // Probable relation
    
    // Multipliers
    MultiplierSameWorkspace = 2.0  // Critical for relevance
    
    // Time decay bonuses
    BonusRecent24h = 20   // Session within 24 hours of bead activity
    BonusRecent7d  = 10   // Session within 7 days
    PenaltyOld30d  = -10  // Session older than 30 days
    
    // Threshold for display
    MinScoreThreshold = 25  // Below this, don't show session
    
    // Result limits
    MaxSessionsReturned = 3  // Top N sessions per bead
    MaxKeywordsExtracted = 5 // Keywords from title
)
```

### Scoring Table (Reference)
| Factor | Weight | Rationale |
|--------|--------|-----------|
| ID mention | +100 | Definitive evidence |
| Exact keyword match | +50 | Strong thematic link |
| Partial keyword match | +30 | Probable relation |
| Same workspace | 2x multiplier | Critical for relevance |
| Recent session (<24h) | +20 | Fresh context |
| Recent session (<7d) | +10 | Relevant context |
| Older session (>30d) | -10 | May be stale |

**Minimum threshold for display: 25 points** (avoids noise)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ID mention strategy works
- [ ] Keyword extraction handles edge cases (empty, very long titles)
- [ ] Timestamp proximity filtering works
- [ ] Combined scoring produces sensible rankings
- [ ] Workspace filtering dramatically improves relevance
- [ ] Results are cached with appropriate TTL
- [ ] Empty beads (no title) handled gracefully
- [ ] All scoring constants are configurable

## Edge Cases
- Bead with empty title вҶ’ skip keyword strategy, rely on ID/timestamp
- Bead with very long title вҶ’ truncate to 5 keywords
- Many beads with similar keywords вҶ’ ensure distinct sessions per bead
- Closed bead with no activity dates вҶ’ use created_at only
- Workspace path unavailable вҶ’ search all (lower precision, warn in debug log)
- Title is just "Bug" or "Fix" вҶ’ all keywords filtered, skip keyword strategy

## Testing Strategy
- Test each strategy in isolation
- Test combined scoring with known inputs/outputs
- Test keyword extraction edge cases
- Test workspace filtering
- Test scoring threshold behavior
- Benchmark performance (target <500ms for single correlation)
close_reason:
Implemented Session-Bead Correlation Engine with:
- Keyword extraction with stop word filtering
- Three correlation strategies: ID mention (highest confidence), keyword search (medium), timestamp proximity (lower)
- Combined scoring with workspace boost (2x multiplier for same workspace)
- LRU cache integration
- 20 comprehensive tests, all pass with -race
- staticcheck clean

title: Cass Search Interface with Safety Wrappers
description:
# Cass Search Interface with Safety Wrappers

## Purpose
Provide a safe, timeout-protected interface for searching cass. This wraps the raw CLI calls with error handling, JSON parsing, and resource management.

## Background

### Why a Wrapper?
Direct exec.Command calls are dangerous:
- No timeout protection (could hang forever)
- No error normalization (different failure modes)
- No resource limiting (could spawn too many processes)
- JSON parsing errors could crash

This wrapper provides a safe abstraction that handles all failure modes gracefully.

### cass Search Command
```bash
cass search "query" --robot --limit 10 --fields minimal --days 30
```

Flags we use:
- `--robot`: JSON output (required)
- `--limit N`: Cap results (default 10)
- `--fields minimal`: Reduce token count
- `--days N`: Time filter for relevance
- `--workspace PATH`: Filter by project (critical for relevance)

## Implementation

### Search Options
```go
type SearchOptions struct {
    Query     string        // Required: search query
    Limit     int           // Max results (default 10)
    Days      int           // Time filter (0 = no filter)
    Workspace string        // Filter by workspace path
    Fields    string        // "minimal", "summary", or field list
    Timeout   time.Duration // Override default 5s timeout
}
```

### Search Result Types
```go
type SearchResult struct {
    SourcePath  string    // Path to session file
    LineNumber  int       // Line in session
    Agent       string    // "claude", "cursor", etc.
    Title       string    // Conversation title
    Score       float64   // Relevance score (0-1)
    Snippet     string    // Content preview
    Timestamp   time.Time // When the message occurred
    MatchType   string    // "exact", "prefix", "fuzzy"
}

type SearchResponse struct {
    Results []SearchResult
    Meta    SearchMeta
}

type SearchMeta struct {
    ElapsedMs int
    Total     int
    Truncated bool
    Error     string // Non-empty if partial failure
}
```

### Safety Mechanisms

#### 1. Timeout Protection
```go
ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 5*time.Second)
defer cancel()
cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "cass", args...)
```

#### 2. Concurrent Call Limiting
```go
var cassSemaphore = make(chan struct{}, 2) // Max 2 concurrent
cassSemaphore <- struct{}{}
defer func() { <-cassSemaphore }()
```

#### 3. Output Size Limiting
```go
// Limit output to 1MB to prevent memory issues
output := make([]byte, 1024*1024)
n, _ := io.ReadFull(stdout, output)
```

#### 4. Graceful JSON Parsing
```go
var resp SearchResponse
if err := json.Unmarshal(output, &resp); err != nil {
    // Try to extract partial results
    // Log error for debugging
    // Return empty response, not error
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Executes cass search with proper arguments
- [ ] Enforces timeout on all calls
- [ ] Limits concurrent cass processes
- [ ] Parses JSON response correctly
- [ ] Returns empty results (not error) on failure
- [ ] Supports workspace filtering
- [ ] Handles partial/malformed responses

## Error Handling Philosophy
NEVER bubble errors to UI. All failures result in empty results:
- Timeout вҶ’ empty results, debug log
- JSON parse error вҶ’ empty results, debug log
- Process crash вҶ’ empty results, debug log
- No results вҶ’ empty results (not "no results found")

## Testing Strategy
- Mock cass binary responses
- Test timeout behavior
- Test malformed JSON handling
- Test concurrent call limiting
- Test workspace filtering
close_reason: Implemented Cass search interface with safety wrappers: timeout protection, concurrent call limiting (semaphore), graceful JSON parsing, output size limiting. 19 new tests pass with -race.

title: Cass Detection and Health Checking
description:
# Cass Detection and Health Checking

## Purpose
Determine if cass is installed and operational before attempting any integration. This is the foundation of the entire featureвҖ”every other component depends on knowing cass availability.

## Background

### Why Detection Matters
Unlike a library dependency, cass is an external binary that may or may not be installed. We cannot assume its presence. Detection must be:
- Fast (sub-100ms)
- Cached (avoid repeated checks)
- Silent on failure (no error messages to users)

### cass Health States
| Exit Code | Meaning | Our Response |
|-----------|---------|--------------|
| 0 | Healthy, ready to search | Enable integration |
| 1 | Needs indexing | Disable integration (silent) |
| 3 | Index missing/corrupt | Disable integration (silent) |
| Other/timeout | Unknown error | Disable integration (silent) |

## Implementation

### Detection Flow
```
1. Check if "cass" exists in PATH
   в””в”Җ No вҶ’ StatusNotInstalled, stop
   
2. Run "cass health" with 2s timeout
   в””в”Җ Timeout вҶ’ StatusNotInstalled, stop
   в””в”Җ Exit 0 вҶ’ StatusHealthy, continue
   в””в”Җ Exit 1 вҶ’ StatusNeedsIndex, stop
   в””в”Җ Other вҶ’ StatusNotInstalled, stop

3. (Optional) Run "cass capabilities --json"
   в””в”Җ Cache supported features
```

### Caching Strategy
- Cache detection result for 5 minutes
- Re-check on explicit user action (future: "refresh cass" command)
- Invalidate cache on error during search

### Types
```go
type Status int
const (
    StatusUnknown Status = iota
    StatusNotInstalled
    StatusNeedsIndex
    StatusHealthy
)

type Detector struct {
    status    Status
    checkedAt time.Time
    cacheTTL  time.Duration
    mu        sync.RWMutex
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detects cass binary in PATH
- [ ] Runs health check with timeout
- [ ] Caches result with TTL
- [ ] Thread-safe for concurrent access
- [ ] Zero output/errors when cass not found
- [ ] Returns StatusHealthy only when cass ready

## Edge Cases
- cass binary exists but is not executable
- cass hangs on health check (timeout protection)
- PATH changes during bv session (cache invalidation)
- Multiple cass binaries in PATH (use first found)

## Testing Strategy
- Mock exec.LookPath for binary detection
- Mock exec.Command for health check responses
- Test all status transitions
- Test cache expiration
- Test concurrent access safety
close_reason: Implemented pkg/cass/detector.go with full test coverage (17 tests). Features: PATH detection, health check with timeout, TTL caching, thread-safe concurrent access, silent failure mode.

title: [EPIC] Cass Integration - Coding Session Correlation
description:
# Epic: Cass Integration - Coding Session Correlation

## Vision
Surface relevant AI coding sessions from cass (Coding Agent Session Search) alongside beads, enabling users to see "how was this issue solved?" with one keypress. This bridges the gap between issue tracking (beads) and work history (cass sessions).

## Background

### What is cass?
cass (https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/coding_agent_session_search) indexes conversations from 9+ AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Gemini, Aider, etc.) into a unified, searchable timeline. Key features:
- Sub-60ms prefix search across all agent history
- Robot mode (--robot) for JSON output
- Health checking (cass health)
- Workspace filtering

### Why integrate?
When reviewing closed issues or understanding project history, users often wonder "how did we actually solve this?" The coding sessions contain that contextвҖ”the actual AI conversations where problems were discussed and solved. Correlating sessions to beads answers this question instantly.

## Critical Design Principles

### 1. Invisible When Absent
Users without cass must NEVER see error messages, "no sessions found" states, broken UI, or loading indicators for cass features. The feature must be completely invisible when cass is not available.

### 2. Non-Blocking
All cass operations happen in background goroutines. Main UI thread is never blocked. Timeouts on all external calls (5 seconds max). Graceful degradation on any error.

### 3. Additive Only
No existing functionality changes behavior. New keybindings do not conflict. Status bar additions are minimal and contextual.

## User Experience
- Status bar shows session count indicator (рҹ“ҺN) only when sessions exist
- Press **V** to toggle session modal with previews ("View sessions")
- History view integrates sessions in timeline alongside commits

## Key Binding Note
The 'V' key was chosen because 'C' (for Cass) is already used:
- List view: 'c' = filter to closed issues
- History view: 'C' = cycle confidence threshold

'V' = "View sessions" is semantically meaningful and available.

## Success Metrics
- Zero user-visible errors related to cass
- Sub-100ms perceived latency for cached results
- No measurable impact on startup time without cass
- All scoring thresholds are tunable for refinement
close_reason: Cass Integration complete: Detection & Health Checking (bv-uznu), Search Interface with Safety Wrappers (bv-8phk), Result Caching System (bv-bjv0), and Safety Tests (bv-i1vw). The integration surfaces AI coding sessions from cass alongside beads while remaining completely invisible when cass is unavailable. All ~100 tests pass across pkg/cass/ and integration points.

title: Board: Search and Filter Integration
description:
## Overview
Board view should support the same search/filter capabilities as list view.

## Current State
Board shows issues matching global filter, but board-specific interactions are missing.

## Goal: Consistency with List View
If it works in list view, it should work in board view.

### Global Filter Keys (Already Exist)
These keys work globally and should work in board view:
- `o`: Open only (hide closed column)
- `c`: Closed only (show only closed column)
- `r`: Ready (no blockers) - hides blocked items
- `L`: Open label picker

### Board-Specific Search
- `/`: Open inline search input
- Filter cards by title, ID, description
- Real-time filtering as you type
- `Esc`: Clear search
- `n/N`: Jump to next/previous match (if multiple)

### Filter Indicator
Show active filters in board header:
```
BOARD VIEW [filter: label:api] [12/45 shown]
```

### Filter Persistence
- Apply filter in list view вҶ’ switch to board вҶ’ same filter active
- Apply filter in board view вҶ’ switch to list вҶ’ same filter active

## Implementation

### Reuse Existing Logic
The main Model already has `currentFilter` and `applyFilter()`.
Board calls `SetIssues(filtered)` - this already works.

### Search State in Board
```go
type BoardModel struct {
    searchQuery  string
    searchActive bool
    // ...
}
```

Search is applied on top of global filter.

### Visual Feedback Options
1. **Hide non-matching cards** (cleaner)
2. **Dim non-matching cards** (preserves context)

Recommend option 1 (hide) for cleaner board.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `/` opens search in board view
- [ ] Search filters cards in real-time
- [ ] Global filter keys (o/c/r/L) work in board
- [ ] Filter indicator in header
- [ ] Filter persists across view switches
- [ ] Match count shown
