```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ рҹ“Ӣ OPEN (15)                        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ рҹ”ҙ 3 P0  рҹҹЎ 5 P1  в”Ӯ  oldest: 14d    в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

## Statistics to Show

### Priority Breakdown (Most Important)
- Count of P0 (critical) and P1 (high) items
- Format: `3рҹ”ҙ 5рҹҹЎ` or `P0:3 P1:5`
- Helps identify columns with urgent work

### Staleness Indicator
- Age of oldest item in column
- Color coded: green(<7d), yellow(7-30d), red(>30d)
- Surfaces neglected work

### Blocked Count (In Progress column only)
- How many items in "In Progress" are actually blocked
- `вҡ пёҸ 3 blocked` - indicates flow problems

## Adaptive Display
- **Narrow (<100)**: Just count: `OPEN (15)`
- **Medium (100-140)**: Count + P0/P1: `OPEN (15) 3рҹ”ҙ`
- **Wide (>140)**: Full stats: `OPEN (15) 3рҹ”ҙ 5рҹҹЎ oldest:14d`

## Deferred: WIP Limits
~~Visual WIP limit bars and warnings~~

**Why deferred**:
- Requires user configuration (what's the limit?)
- Adds complexity without clear agent workflow benefit
- Can be added later if users request it

## Implementation

```go
type ColumnStats struct {
    Total       int
    P0Count     int
    P1Count     int
    BlockedCount int  // only for InProgress
    OldestAge   time.Duration
}

func computeColumnStats(issues []model.Issue) ColumnStats
```

Compute on SetIssues, cache until next update.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] P0/P1 counts shown in header
- [ ] Oldest item age shown
- [ ] Staleness color-coded
- [ ] Blocked count in In Progress column
- [ ] Adapts to terminal width
close_reason: Implemented column statistics with P0/P1 counts, oldest age, and blocked count. Adaptive display based on terminal width. 9 tests added. Commit 053999d.

title: Board: Visual Dependency Indicators
description:
## Overview
Show blocking relationships visually on cards without leaving the board view.

## Problem
Currently, you can't tell which cards are blocked or blocking others. The only indication is being in the "Blocked" column, but you don't know WHAT is blocking.

## Solution: Card Badges (Core Feature)

### Blocked-By Badge
Show what's blocking this card:
```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ рҹҗӣ P1 bt-123            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Fix auth timeout        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ рҹҡ« вҶҗ bt-456 (API...)   в”Ӯ  <- Blocker ID + truncated title
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

### Blocking Badge (High Impact Card)
Show when this card blocks others:
```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ рҹ”§ P0 bt-456            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ API redesign            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ вҡЎ вҶ’ blocks 3           в”Ӯ  <- Impact indicator
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

### Color Coding
- рҹ”ҙ Red border/accent: Blocked card
- рҹҹЎ Yellow/orange badge: Blocks others (high impact - fix this to unblock work)
- рҹҹў Green accent: Ready to work (open, no blockers)

### Selection Detail
When card is selected, show in detail panel (not on card):
```
Blocked by:
  вҖў bt-456: API redesign (in_progress)
  
Blocks:
  вҖў bt-789: Token refresh
  вҖў bt-234: Session handling
  
рҹ’Ў Completing bt-456 would unblock 3 cards including this one.
```

## Deferred: Connection Lines
~~Visual ASCII lines connecting blocked/blocking cards across columns.~~

**Why deferred**: 
- Complex to render cleanly across variable-width columns
- With many dependencies, lines overlap and create visual noise
- The graph view already visualizes dependencies excellently
- Card badges + detail panel provide the essential information

## Implementation

### Card Badge Rendering
```go
// In renderCard()
if len(issue.BlockedBy) > 0 {
    blockerID := issue.BlockedBy[0]
    blockerTitle := truncate(getTitle(blockerID), 12)
    badge := fmt.Sprintf("рҹҡ« вҶҗ %s (%s)", blockerID, blockerTitle)
    // Add to card lines
}

if blockCount := countBlocking(issue.ID, allIssues); blockCount > 0 {
    badge := fmt.Sprintf("вҡЎ вҶ’ blocks %d", blockCount)
    // Add to card lines, style as high-impact
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Blocked cards show blocker ID and title snippet
- [ ] High-impact cards show "blocks N" badge
- [ ] Color coding distinguishes blocked/blocking/ready states
- [ ] Detail panel shows full dependency info when selected
close_reason: All acceptance criteria implemented: blocker title snippets, blocks badge, color coding (red/yellow/green for blocked/blocking/ready), and detail panel with full dependency info

title: Board: Enhanced Keyboard Navigation
description:
## Overview
Add power-user keyboard shortcuts for efficient board navigation.

## Current Keys
- `h/l`: Move between columns
- `j/k`: Move within column
- `G`: Go to bottom
- `Ctrl+D/U`: Page down/up
- `Enter`: Exit to detail view

## New Keys

### Column Jumping
- `1-4`: Jump directly to column 1/2/3/4
- `H`: Jump to first column
- `L`: Jump to last column

### Navigation Enhancements
- `gg`: Go to top of current column (vim consistency)
- `0`: Jump to first item in column
- `$`: Jump to last item in column

### Search/Filter
- `/`: Open search (filter cards by title/ID)
- `n/N`: Next/previous search match
- `Esc`: Clear search

### View Toggles
- `d`: Toggle inline detail expansion for selected card
- `Tab`: Toggle side detail panel
- `s`: Cycle swimlane grouping

### Utility
- `y`: Copy card ID to clipboard

## Removed from Scope
- ~~`m/M`: Move card status~~ - Board is READ-ONLY. Status changes belong in a future "quick actions" feature, not basic navigation.
- ~~`p`: Priority filter~~ - Use global `L` for label picker or `/` for search instead. Avoid key conflicts.

## Visual Hints
Update footer to show context-appropriate hints:
```
1-4:jump  h/l:col  j/k:nav  d:peek  Tab:detail  /:search  ?:help
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Number keys jump to columns
- [ ] gg/G work like vim
- [ ] Search filters cards in real-time
- [ ] Tab toggles detail panel
- [ ] Footer shows relevant hints
close_reason: Added enhanced keyboard navigation: column jumping (1-4, H/L), vim-style nav (gg, 0, $), search (/,n/N), copy ID (y), updated footer hints and context help

title: Board: Swimlane Grouping Options
description:
## Overview
Allow grouping cards by different dimensions, not just status.

## Current
Fixed 4 columns: Open | In Progress | Blocked | Closed

## Proposed Swimlane Modes (3 modes, not 5)

### By Status (default)
```
OPEN | IN PROGRESS | BLOCKED | CLOSED
```

### By Priority
```
P0 CRITICAL | P1 HIGH | P2 MEDIUM | P3+ OTHER
```
Useful for: Severity distribution, finding highest priority work
Note: Combine P3/P4 into "OTHER" to avoid too many columns

### By Type
```
BUG | FEATURE | TASK | EPIC
```
Useful for: Understanding work mix, bug vs feature ratio

## Deferred to Future
- **By Label**: Could create too many columns, complex to implement well
- **By Epic**: Requires epic detection logic, niche use case

## Implementation

### Key Binding
`s`: Cycle swimlane mode (status вҶ’ priority вҶ’ type вҶ’ status...)

### Model Changes
```go
type SwimLaneMode int

const (
    SwimByStatus SwimLaneMode = iota  // default
    SwimByPriority
    SwimByType
)
```

### Visual Indicator
Show current grouping in header:
```
BOARD VIEW [by: Status]  s:change
```

### Empty Column Behavior
In non-status modes, hide columns with no items (e.g., if no EPICs exist, don't show EPIC column).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `s` cycles through 3 swimlane modes
- [ ] Each mode groups cards correctly
- [ ] Column headers update for mode
- [ ] Empty columns auto-hide in non-status modes
- [ ] Current mode shown in header
close_reason: Implemented swimlane grouping modes with s key cycling between Status/Priority/Type views

title: Board: Rich Card Content with Markdown
description:
## Overview
Improve the DEFAULT card display to show more useful information while remaining compact and scannable.

## Design Principle
Cards should be **information-dense but compact**. The board is for OVERVIEW - you scan many cards quickly. Full details are for:
- `d`: Inline expansion (quick peek)
- `Tab`: Side panel (persistent detail)

## Current Card (3 lines, poor info density)
```
рҹҗӣ рҹ”ҙ bt-123
Fix auth timeout when...
@bob вҶ’2 api+1
```

## Improved Compact Card (4 lines, better info)
```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ рҹҗӣ P1 bt-123            3d вҸұ  в”Ӯ  <- Type, priority, ID, age
в”Ӯ Fix auth timeout when token.. в”Ӯ  <- Title (more space)
в”Ӯ рҹҡ«вҶҗbt-456  вҡЎвҶ’2  api,backend  в”Ӯ  <- Blocked-by, blocks, labels
в”Ӯ в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘в–‘ в”Ӯ  <- (optional) progress/activity bar
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

## What Changed vs Current

### Line 1: Header
- Show priority as P0/P1/P2 (clearer than emoji flame levels)
- Add age indicator with color: green(<7d), yellow(7-30d), red(>30d stale)

### Line 2: Title
- Use full available width (now that we removed the 60-char cap)
- Smart truncation with ellipsis

### Line 3: Metadata Row (NEW - replaces old line 3)
- Blocked-by indicator: `рҹҡ«вҶҗbt-456` (compact)
- Blocks indicator: `вҡЎвҶ’2` (this card blocks 2 others)
- Labels: First 2-3 labels, no "+N" - just truncate
- Remove @assignee (not useful for agent workflows)

### Line 4: (Optional) Activity Indicator
- Thin bar showing recency of activity
- Or skip this for even more compact cards

## NOT in This Task
- Description preview (that's for inline expansion with `d`)
- Full dependency list (that's for detail panel)
- Markdown rendering (that's for expansion/detail)

## Implementation

### Card Height
Fixed 4 lines for consistency. Variable height creates scroll calculation complexity and visual inconsistency.

### Color Coding
- Border color by status (existing)
- Age indicator: green/yellow/red
- Blocked badge: red tint
- High-impact (blocks others): yellow/orange highlight

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cards show priority as P0/P1/P2/P3
- [ ] Age indicator with color coding
- [ ] Blocked-by shows blocker ID (compact)
- [ ] Blocks count shown for high-impact cards
- [ ] Labels shown as names (2-3 max)
- [ ] Fixed 4-line height for consistency
close_reason: Implemented 4-line card format with P0/P1/P2 priority text, age color coding (green<7d, yellow 7-30d, red>30d), blocked-by indicator, blocks count from reverse index, and 2-3 label names. Added blocksIndex to BoardModel for reverse dependency tracking. Tests added and passing.

title: History: Related Work Discovery
description:
## Overview
When working on a bead, surface OTHER beads an agent should be aware of.

## Why Agents Need This
Agents work in isolation but need context:
- What other open beads touch the same files?
- What recently-closed beads might have relevant context?
- Are there beads that LOOK unrelated but share hidden coupling?

## Implementation

### Related Work Query
```go
func (h *HistoryReport) RelatedWork(beadID string) RelatedWorkResult

type RelatedWorkResult struct {
    // Beads touching same files
    FileOverlap []RelatedBead
    
    // Beads with shared commits  
    CommitOverlap []RelatedBead
    
    // Beads in same dependency cluster
    DependencyCluster []RelatedBead
    
    // Beads from same time window
    Concurrent []RelatedBead
}

type RelatedBead struct {
    BeadID    string
    Status    string
    Title     string
    Relevance int     // 0-100
    Reason    string  // why it's related
}
```

### Display
```
рҹ”— Related Work for bt-123

   рҹ“Ғ Same Files (auth/token.go):
   вҖў bt-456 (open): API auth refactor - HIGH RELEVANCE
   вҖў bt-089 (closed 3d): OAuth setup - context
   
   рҹ”Җ Shared Commits:
   вҖў bt-789 (open): Rate limiting - shares 2 commits
   
   вҸ° Concurrent (same time window):
   вҖў bt-234 (closed): Session timeout - likely related context
```

### Robot Command
`bv robot related bt-123` вҶ’ JSON with all related work categorized

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Multiple relationship types detected
- [ ] Relevance scored for prioritization
- [ ] Status clearly indicated (open = might conflict)
- [ ] Robot command for agent consumption
close_reason: Implemented RelatedWork discovery with file overlap, commit overlap, dependency cluster, and concurrent detection. Added --robot-related command and tests.

title: History: Correlation Confidence Audit
description:
## Overview
Explain WHY a commit is linked to a bead, and allow corrections.

## Why Agents Need This
Agents making decisions based on correlations need to know:
- Is this linkage trustworthy?
- What signals drove the correlation?
- Can I correct bad correlations to improve future accuracy?

## Implementation

### Confidence Breakdown
```go
type CorrelationExplanation struct {
    CommitSHA   string
    BeadID      string
    Confidence  int  // 0-100
    Signals     []Signal
}

type Signal struct {
    Type   string  // message_match, timing, file_overlap, author_match
    Weight int     // contribution to confidence
    Detail string  // human/agent readable explanation
}
```

### Display
```
рҹ”Қ Correlation: a1b2c3d вҶ” bt-123 (87%)

   Signals:
   в”ңв”Җ message_match (+40): commit contains 'bt-123'
   в”ңв”Җ timing (+25): commit within 2h of status change
   в”ңв”Җ file_overlap (+15): 2/3 files in bead scope
   в””в”Җ proximity (+7): adjacent to confirmed linked commits
   
   [c]onfirm  [r]eject  [i]gnore
```

### Feedback Loop
When agent confirms/rejects:
- Store in .beads/correlation_feedback.jsonl
- Use to weight future correlations
- Track accuracy over time

### Robot Command
`bv robot explain-correlation a1b2c3d bt-123`
`bv robot confirm-correlation a1b2c3d bt-123`
`bv robot reject-correlation a1b2c3d bt-123`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Correlation signals exposed in explanation
- [ ] Feedback mechanism stores confirmations/rejections
- [ ] Robot commands for programmatic audit
- [ ] Future correlations influenced by feedback
close_reason: Implemented correlation confidence audit with explanation, feedback storage, and robot commands

title: History: Impact Network Graph
description:
## Overview
Visualize how beads connect through shared files and commits.

## Why Agents Need This
Understanding the STRUCTURE of work helps agents:
- See which beads are tightly coupled (change one, check the other)
- Identify clusters of related work
- Understand the 'shape' of a feature area

## Implementation

### Network Construction
Nodes: Beads
Edges: 
- Shared commits (weighted by count)
- Shared files (weighted by overlap %)
- Explicit dependencies (blocking relationships)

### ASCII Visualization
```
           в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
           в”Ӯ bt-123  в”Ӯ Auth Token
           в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                в”Ӯ 3 shared commits
     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
     в–ј          в–ј          в–ј
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ bt-456  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ bt-789  в”Ӯ в”Ӯ bt-234  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   API   в”Ӯ в”Ӯ  Rate   в”Ӯ в”Ӯ Session в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                 в”Ӯ blocks
                 в–ј
           в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
           в”Ӯ bt-567  в”Ӯ
           в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

### Cluster Detection
Identify tightly-coupled bead clusters:
```
Cluster 'auth-system' (5 beads, 89% internal connectivity):
  bt-123, bt-456, bt-789, bt-234, bt-567
```

### Robot Command
`bv robot impact-network --bead=bt-123 --depth=2`
Returns JSON graph structure for agent analysis.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Network graph computed from commit/file overlap
- [ ] Clusters identified automatically
- [ ] ASCII rendering for TUI display
- [ ] JSON output for robot command
close_reason: Implemented impact network graph with shared commit/file edges, cluster detection, and --robot-impact-network command. Commit 7729029.

title: History: Dependency Chain Visualization
description:
## Overview
Show the full dependency chain explaining WHY a bead is blocked.

## Why Agents Need This
When a bead is blocked, agents need to understand:
- What's the root blocker?
- How deep is the chain?
- Is there actionable work on the blockers?

## Current State
We show 'blocked by: bv-xyz' but not the full chain.

## Implementation

### Chain Resolution
```go
func (b *Bead) BlockerChain() []BeadSummary

// Returns full chain: 
// bt-100 blocked by bt-200 blocked by bt-300 (root)
```

### Display
```
рҹ”— Dependency Chain for bt-100

   bt-100 (your bead)
      в”Ӯ
      в–ј blocked by
   bt-200: API redesign (in_progress, @agent working)
      в”Ӯ
      в–ј blocked by  
   bt-300: Schema migration (open, READY - no blockers)
      в”Ӯ
      в””в”Җв–ә ROOT BLOCKER
   
   рҹ’Ў bt-300 is actionable - completing it unblocks the chain
```

### Robot Command
`bv robot blocker-chain bt-100` вҶ’ JSON with full chain + actionability flags

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Full blocker chain resolved (handles cycles gracefully)
- [ ] Root blocker identified
- [ ] Actionability indicated (is root blocker ready to work?)
- [ ] Robot command for agent use
close_reason: Implemented GetBlockerChain method and --robot-blocker-chain command

title: History: Orphan Commit Detection
description:
## Overview
Detect commits that probably SHOULD link to a bead but don't.

## Why Agents Need This
Data quality matters. If commits are missing bead linkages:
- Context is lost
- Impact analysis is incomplete
- Agent decisions may miss relevant history

## Detection Heuristics

### Orphan Signals
1. **Timing**: Commit made while a bead was in_progress, but not linked
2. **Files**: Commit touches files that other commits linked to a bead
3. **Message**: Commit message mentions bead-like patterns but no correlation found
4. **Author**: Same author has linked commits before/after this one

### Confidence Scoring
```go
type OrphanCandidate struct {
    CommitSHA       string
    SuspicionScore  int      // 0-100
    ProbableBeads   []string // beads this MIGHT belong to
    Signals         []string // why we think it's orphaned
}
```

## Display
```
вҡ пёҸ  Potential Orphan Commits (3 found)
   
   a1b2c3d "fix token expiry" (Score: 85)
   вҶ’ Probable link: bt-123 (touches same files, same timeframe)
   
   d4e5f6g "update config" (Score: 62)
   вҶ’ Probable link: bt-456 (commit during bead's in_progress period)
```

### Robot Command
`bv robot orphans` вҶ’ JSON list of orphan candidates

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Orphan detection runs on history analysis
- [ ] Probable bead matches suggested
- [ ] Robot command for agent consumption
- [ ] Can mark false positives to improve detection
close_reason: Implemented orphan commit detection with smart heuristics. Added --robot-orphans flag with --orphans-min-score filter.

title: History: Bead-File Reverse Index
description:
## Overview
Answer: 'What beads have touched this file, and why?'

## Why Agents Need This
Before modifying a file, an agent should understand:
- What work has been done on this file before
- What the intent/context was for previous changes
- Whether there are open beads that might conflict

## Current State
We have CommitIndex (SHA вҶ’ []BeadID) but no File вҶ’ []BeadID index.

## Implementation

### Build File Index
```go
type FileBeadIndex struct {
    // file path -> list of beads that touched it
    FileToBeads map[string][]BeadReference
}

type BeadReference struct {
    BeadID     string
    CommitSHAs []string  // which commits linked this bead to this file
    Status     string    // open/closed
    Title      string
    LastTouch  time.Time
}
```

### Display in History
When viewing file history:
```
рҹ“Ғ auth/token.go - Bead History
   
   Open beads touching this file:
   вҖў bt-123: Token refresh edge cases (2 commits)
   
   Closed beads (last 30 days):
   вҖў bt-089: OAuth foundation (5 commits)
   вҖў bt-067: Session timeout fix (1 commit)
```

### Robot Command
`bv robot file-beads auth/token.go` вҶ’ JSON with bead references

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] File вҶ’ Bead index built from history
- [ ] Open vs closed beads distinguished
- [ ] Recency shown (agents care about recent context)
- [ ] Robot command for programmatic access
close_reason: Closed

title: History: File Co-Change Pattern Detection
description:
## Overview
Detect and surface files that frequently change together across commits.

## Why Agents Need This
When an agent modifies a file, it should know what OTHER files typically change alongside it. This prevents incomplete changes and reveals hidden coupling.

## Data Available
```go
type CorrelatedCommit struct {
    Files []string  // Files changed in this commit
}
```

## Implementation

### Co-Change Analysis
- Build adjacency matrix: file A changed with file B in N commits
- Identify clusters of frequently co-changed files
- Surface as 'related files' when viewing any file's history

### Display
When viewing a file in history:
```
рҹ“Ғ auth/token.go
   Co-changes with (80%+ correlation):
   вҖў auth/session.go (changed together in 12/14 commits)
   вҖў middleware/auth.go (changed together in 9/14 commits)
   вҖў config/auth.yaml (changed together in 7/14 commits)
```

### Robot Command Integration
`bv robot file-relations auth/token.go` вҶ’ JSON of co-change patterns

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Co-change matrix computed from commit history
- [ ] Related files shown in file-centric view
- [ ] Robot command exposes data for agent consumption
- [ ] Threshold configurable (default 50% co-occurrence)
close_reason: Closed

title: History: Author Filter Fix & Enhancement
description:
## Bug Description
The author filter ('f' key) is broken - currently just shows a hint but doesn't actually filter.

## Current Code (history.go)
```go
case "f":
    // Author filter hint
    m.filterHint = "Author filter: type author name..."
```

This sets a hint but never implements actual filtering.

## Fix Required

### Author Filter Modal
- Similar to label picker UI
- List unique authors from commits
- Fuzzy search by author name
- Show commit count per author

### Data Source
```go
// Already available in CorrelatedCommit
type CorrelatedCommit struct {
    Author  string
    // ...
}
```

### Implementation
1. Extract unique authors from all commits in history
2. Create author picker similar to LabelPickerModel
3. Apply filter to both git-centric and bead-centric views
4. Show filter badge when active

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 'f' key opens author filter modal
- [ ] Authors listed with commit counts
- [ ] Fuzzy search works
- [ ] Filter applies to current view
- [ ] Filter can be cleared
close_reason: Author filtering not relevant for coding agent use case - most commits are by same agent/user

title: History: Keyboard Navigation Improvements
description:
## Overview

## Improvements

### Key Bindings
- `j/k`: Navigate items in focused pane


title: History: Search & Filter Infrastructure
description:
## Overview
Implement comprehensive search and filtering for history view.

## Search Capabilities
- **Text search**: Filter commits by message content
- **SHA search**: Jump directly to commit by partial SHA
- **Bead search**: Filter by bead ID or title
- **Author search**: Filter by commit author

## Filter UI
- Inline filter input (similar to label picker fuzzy search)
- Filter chips showing active filters
- Quick clear all filters

## Implementation
- Add `filterQuery string` to HistoryModel
- Add `activeFilters []HistoryFilter` for compound filtering
- Implement fuzzy matching using existing `fuzzyScore` from label_picker.go
- Show filter hint in status bar

## Key Bindings
- `/`: Open search input
- `Esc`: Clear search/close filter
- `f`: Quick filter menu (author, date range, etc.)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Multiple filters can be combined
- [ ] Filter state persists during view mode switches
- [ ] Visual indication of active filters
close_reason: Implemented search/filter infrastructure with text input, multiple search modes, and real-time filtering

title: History: Statistics Header Bar
description:
# History: Statistics Header Bar

## Problem Statement
The \`HistoryReport\` contains valuable statistics that are completely hidden from users:

\`\`\`go
type HistoryStats struct {
    TotalBeads         int
    BeadsWithCommits   int
    TotalCommits       int
    UniqueAuthors      int
    AvgCommitsPerBead  float64
    AvgCycleTimeDays   *float64
    MethodDistribution map[string]int
}
\`\`\`

Users can't see the big picture: How much history is there? Who's been active? What's the average cycle time?

## Design

### Current Header (boring)
\`\`\`
BEAD HISTORY                      5/12 beads | [H] to close
в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\`\`\`

### Improved Header (informative)
\`\`\`
BEAD HISTORY                                             [Git Mode в–ҫ]
в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
47 beads вҖў 156 commits вҖў 5 authors вҖў Avg cycle: 2.3d вҖў 3.3 commits/bead
Filter: All authors в”Ӯ Confidence: вүҘ0% в”Ӯ 47/52 beads shown
в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ
\`\`\`

### Visual Badges
Use styled badges for key metrics:
\`\`\`
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ HISTORY  в”Ӯ 47 beads в”Ӯ 156 commits в”Ӯ 5 authors в”Ӯ вҢҖ 2.3d cycle в”Ӯ h в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
\`\`\`

### Implementation

\`\`\`go
func (h *HistoryModel) renderHeader() string {
    stats := h.report.Stats
    t := h.theme
    
    // Title with mode indicator
    title := titleStyle.Render("HISTORY")
    modeIndicator := modeStyle.Render(h.modeLabel())
    
    // Stats badges
    beadsBadge := badgeStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%d beads", stats.TotalBeads))
    commitsBadge := badgeStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%d commits", stats.TotalCommits))
    authorsBadge := badgeStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%d authors", stats.UniqueAuthors))
    
    var cycleBadge string
    if stats.AvgCycleTimeDays != nil {
        cycleBadge = badgeStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("вҢҖ %.1fd", *stats.AvgCycleTimeDays))
    }
    
    // Filter status line
    filterLine := h.renderFilterStatus()
    
    // Combine
    statsLine := lipgloss.JoinHorizontal(lipgloss.Top,
        beadsBadge, " вҖў ", commitsBadge, " вҖў ", authorsBadge,
    )
    if cycleBadge != "" {
        statsLine += " вҖў " + cycleBadge
    }
    
    return lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left,
        lipgloss.JoinHorizontal(lipgloss.Top, title, spacer, modeIndicator),
        statsLine,
        filterLine,
        separator,
    )
}
\`\`\`

### Filter Status Line
Show current filter state:
\`\`\`
Filter: @alice в”Ӯ Confidence: вүҘ75% в”Ӯ 12/47 beads shown
\`\`\`

Or when no filters:
\`\`\`
Showing all 47 beads with commits (52 total in project)
\`\`\`

### Correlation Method Distribution (tooltip/expandable)
On a keypress (maybe 's' for stats), show detailed breakdown:
\`\`\`
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ CORRELATION METHODS                 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ                в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ explicit_id:    45 commits (29%)    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ co_committed:   67 commits (43%)    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ temporal:       44 commits (28%)    в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Header shows key statistics inline
- [ ] Mode indicator visible (Git/Bead)
- [ ] Filter status line shows current filters
- [ ] Filtered/total count visible
- [ ] Average cycle time shown (if available)
- [ ] Stats expand/detail on 's' key press

## Visual Polish
- Use theme colors for badges
- Muted color for separator lines
- Consistent spacing
- Responsive - hides less important stats on narrow screens
close_reason: Implemented statistics header bar with badges for beads, commits, authors, cycle time, and commits/bead. Added filter status line showing active filters and filtered/total counts.

title: History: Timeline Visualization Panel
description:
# History: Timeline Visualization Panel

## Problem Statement
The History view has rich timestamp data (lifecycle events, commits) but presents it as a flat list. Users can't visualize "when things happened" at a glance. A visual timeline would make patterns immediately apparent.

## What Data We Have
\`\`\`go
type BeadHistory struct {
    Events    []BeadEvent        // created, claimed, closed, modified
    Milestones BeadMilestones    // Quick access to key events
    Commits   []CorrelatedCommit // Each has Timestamp
    CycleTime *CycleTime         // claim_to_close, create_to_close
}
\`\`\`

## Design

### Timeline Panel (appears in wide layout)
\`\`\`
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ TIMELINE: bt-123                в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  Jan 10 в”ғ в—Ӣ Created             в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ         в”ғ в”Ӯ                     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  Jan 12 в”ғ в—Ҹ Claimed by Alice    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ         в”ғ в”Ӯ                     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  Jan 13 в”ғ в”ңв”Җ a1b2c3d 95%        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ         в”ғ в”Ӯ   Fix auth bug      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ         в”ғ в”Ӯ                     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  Jan 14 в”ғ в”ңв”Җ b2c3d4e 87%        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ         в”ғ в”Ӯ   Add session mgmt  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ         в”ғ в”Ӯ                     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  Jan 15 в”ғ в”ңв”Җ c3d4e5f 65%        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ         в”ғ в”Ӯ   Refactor          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ         в”ғ в”Ӯ                     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  Today  в”ғ вң“ Closed              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ         в”ғ                       в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Cycle: 5d 4h (createвҶ’close)     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Commits: 3 (avg 87% confidence) в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
\`\`\`

### Visual Elements

#### Event Markers
- в—Ӣ Created (open circle)
- в—Ҹ Claimed/In Progress (filled circle)
- вң“ Closed (checkmark)
- вҶ» Reopened (cycle arrow)
- в—Ҷ Modified (diamond)

#### Commit Markers
- в”ңв”Җ Commit branch from timeline
- Colored by confidence: green (вүҘ80%), yellow (50-79%), gray (<50%)
- Show short SHA and abbreviated message

#### Time Scale
- Smart date formatting: "2h ago", "Yesterday", "Jan 15", etc.
- Compress gaps when there's no activity
- Show relative timestamps on left axis

### Compact Timeline (for bead list)
When selecting a bead in narrow view, show mini-timeline:
\`\`\`
bt-123: Auth refactor
в—Ӣв”Җв”Җв—Ҹв”Җв”Җв”ңв”Җв”Җв”ңв”Җв”Җв”ңв”Җв”Җвң“  5d cycle, 3 commits
   Jan 10 в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ Jan 15
\`\`\`

### Implementation

\`\`\`go
type TimelineEntry struct {
    Timestamp time.Time
    Type      string  // "event" or "commit"
    Label     string  // Event type or commit SHA
    Detail    string  // Full message or event detail
    Color     lipgloss.TerminalColor
}

func (h *HistoryModel) buildTimeline(hist BeadHistory) []TimelineEntry {
    var entries []TimelineEntry
    
    // Add lifecycle events
    for _, event := range hist.Events {
        entries = append(entries, TimelineEntry{
            Timestamp: event.Timestamp,
            Type:      "event",
            Label:     string(event.EventType),
            Color:     h.colorForEvent(event.EventType),
        })
    }
    
    // Add commits
    for _, commit := range hist.Commits {
        entries = append(entries, TimelineEntry{
            Timestamp: commit.Timestamp,
            Type:      "commit",
            Label:     commit.ShortSHA,
            Detail:    commit.Message,
            Color:     h.colorForConfidence(commit.Confidence),
        })
    }
    
    // Sort chronologically
    sort.Slice(entries, func(i, j int) bool {
        return entries[i].Timestamp.Before(entries[j].Timestamp)
    })
    
    return entries
}
\`\`\`

### Rendering with Box Drawing
Use Unicode box-drawing characters for clean vertical timeline:
- в”Ӯ Vertical line
- в”ңв”Җ Branch to commit
- в”ғ Bold vertical for emphasis
- в—Ӣв—Ҹвң“ Event markers

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Timeline panel renders in wide layout (> 150 cols)
- [ ] Shows lifecycle events with distinct markers
- [ ] Shows commits with confidence coloring
- [ ] Timestamps use smart relative formatting
- [ ] Cycle time summary at bottom
- [ ] Compact timeline available for narrow views
- [ ] Scrollable if many entries

## Visual Polish
- Smooth color gradients for confidence
- Consistent spacing between entries
- Elegant time axis labeling
- Proper alignment of all elements
close_reason: Timeline panel implemented with tests

title: History: Adaptive Three-Pane Layout
description:
# History: Adaptive Three-Pane Layout

## Problem Statement
The current History view uses a fixed 40%/60% split regardless of terminal width. On modern wide screens (150+ columns), this wastes valuable space. Users with wide terminals should see more information at once.

## Design

### Responsive Breakpoints
\`\`\`
< 100 cols:  Two-pane (current behavior, optimized)
100-150 cols: Three-pane standard
> 150 cols:  Three-pane wide with timeline
\`\`\`

### Layout Configurations

#### Narrow (< 100 cols)
\`\`\`
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ BEADS            в”Ӯ COMMITS              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ                      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 45%              в”Ӯ 55%                  в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
\`\`\`

#### Standard (100-150 cols)
\`\`\`
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ BEADS         в”Ӯ COMMITS           в”Ӯ DETAILS                  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ               в”Ӯ                   в”Ӯ                          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 30%           в”Ӯ 35%               в”Ӯ 35%                      в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
\`\`\`

#### Wide (> 150 cols)
\`\`\`
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ BEADS        в”Ӯ TIMELINE         в”Ӯ COMMITS          в”Ӯ DETAILS              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ                  в”Ӯ                      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 20%          в”Ӯ 25%              в”Ӯ 25%              в”Ӯ 30%                  в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
\`\`\`

### Implementation

\`\`\`go
type historyLayout int

const (
    layoutNarrow   historyLayout = iota // < 100 cols
    layoutStandard                      // 100-150 cols
    layoutWide                          // > 150 cols
)

func (h *HistoryModel) determineLayout() historyLayout {
    if h.width < 100 {
        return layoutNarrow
    } else if h.width < 150 {
        return layoutStandard
    }
    return layoutWide
}

func (h *HistoryModel) View() string {
    layout := h.determineLayout()
    
    switch layout {
    case layoutNarrow:
        return h.renderTwoPaneView()
    case layoutStandard:
        return h.renderThreePaneView()
    case layoutWide:
        return h.renderWidePaneView() // With timeline
    }
}
\`\`\`

### Pane Focus Management
With three panes, Tab cycles through:
1. Primary list (beads or commits)
2. Secondary list (commits or beads)
3. Details panel

Visual indicator shows which pane is focused (border color).

### Responsive Details
- Narrow: Truncate aggressively, show essentials
- Standard: Show full commit messages, file counts
- Wide: Show file list inline, full timestamps

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Layout adapts based on terminal width
- [ ] Breakpoints at 100 and 150 columns
- [ ] Tab cycles through all visible panes
- [ ] Focus indicator (border color) accurate
- [ ] Content adjusts to available width
- [ ] No layout jumps on small width changes (hysteresis)

## Technical Notes
- Consider caching layout calculation (avoid recomputing on every render)
- Test with various terminal widths: 80, 100, 120, 150, 200
- Handle terminal resize gracefully
close_reason: Implemented adaptive three-pane layout with breakpoints at 100/150 cols

title: History: Git-Centric View Mode
description:
# History: Git-Centric View Mode

## Problem Statement
Currently the History view is bead-centric only: you select a bead and see its commits. But users often want the inverse: browse commits chronologically and see which beads each commit relates to.

The data already supports this - \`HistoryReport.CommitIndex\` maps SHA вҶ’ []BeadID - but the UI doesn't expose it.

## User Stories
1. "I want to see recent commits and understand what work they represent"
2. "A commit looks suspicious, which issues does it relate to?"
3. "What has the team been working on (git activity perspective)?"

## Design

### View Mode Toggle
Press \`v\` to toggle between:
- **Bead Mode** (current): Left=Beads, Right=Commits for selected bead
- **Git Mode** (new): Left=Commits, Right=Beads for selected commit

### Git Mode Layout
\`\`\`
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ HISTORY VIEW [Git Mode]                          47 beads вҖў 156 commits в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ COMMITS                     в”Ӯ RELATED BEADS                            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ           в”Ӯ в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ                        в”Ӯ

в”Ӯ   3 files changed           в”Ӯ                                          в”Ӯ

в”Ӯ   Bob вҖў 1d ago              в”Ӯ COMMIT DETAILS                           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   12 files changed          в”Ӯ в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ                        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                             в”Ӯ SHA: a1b2c3d4e5f67890abcdef...           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   d4e5f6g Update docs       в”Ӯ Author: Alice <alice@example.com>        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   Carol вҖў 2d ago            в”Ӯ Date: 2025-01-15 14:30:45                в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   2 files changed           в”Ӯ                                          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                             в”Ӯ Message:                                 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                             в”Ӯ Fix auth bug causing session timeout     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                             в”Ӯ                                          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                             в”Ӯ Files:                                   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                             в”Ӯ  M src/auth.go         +45 -12          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                             в”Ӯ  M src/auth_test.go    +89 -0           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                             в”Ӯ  A src/session.go      +120 -0          в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ j/k:commits  Tab:pane  v:bead-mode  y:copy-sha  Enter:jump  esc:close  в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
\`\`\`

### Implementation

#### New Data Structure
\`\`\`go
type CommitListEntry struct {
    SHA         string
    ShortSHA    string
    Message     string
    Author      string
    Timestamp   time.Time
    FileCount   int
    BeadIDs     []string  // From CommitIndex
    BeadCount   int
}

type HistoryModel struct {
    // ... existing fields ...
    viewMode      historyViewMode // beadMode or gitMode
    commitList    []CommitListEntry
    selectedCommit int
}
\`\`\`

#### Building Commit List
On mode switch to Git mode, build commit list from all correlated commits:
\`\`\`go
func (h *HistoryModel) buildCommitList() {
    seen := make(map[string]bool)
    for _, hist := range h.report.Histories {
        for _, commit := range hist.Commits {
            if !seen[commit.SHA] {
                h.commitList = append(h.commitList, CommitListEntry{
                    SHA: commit.SHA,
                    // ... fill from commit ...
                    BeadIDs: h.report.CommitIndex[commit.SHA],
                })
                seen[commit.SHA] = true
            }
        }
    }
    // Sort by timestamp descending (most recent first)
    sort.Slice(h.commitList, ...)
}
\`\`\`

### Keyboard Changes
- \`v\` - Toggle view mode (new)
- In Git mode:
  - j/k navigates commits (was beads)
  - J/K navigates related beads (new)
  - Enter jumps to selected bead in main list

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] \`v\` key toggles between Bead and Git modes
- [ ] Git mode shows commits sorted by recency
- [ ] Selected commit shows related beads with confidence
- [ ] Commit details panel shows files changed
- [ ] Enter on a bead jumps to it in main list
- [ ] y copies selected commit SHA
- [ ] Mode persists during session (resets on close)

## Visual Polish
- Mode indicator in header: [Git Mode] vs [Bead Mode]
- Smooth transition between modes (no jarring rerender)
- Consistent styling with existing views
close_reason: Added Git-centric view mode with v toggle, commits on left, related beads on right

title: Help Modal Tutorial Entry Point (Space key)
description:
# Help Modal Tutorial Entry Point (Space key)

## Background
The **primary discoverable path** to the tutorial is through the help modal. When user presses \`?\`, they see the shortcuts reference. From there, pressing \`Space\` launches the full interactive tutorial.

This is better than relying solely on CapsLock because:
1. **Discoverable** - Help modal can show hint
2. **Reliable** - Space works on all terminals
3. **Progressive** - Natural flow from quick reference to detailed tutorial
4. **Intuitive** - "I need more help" вҶ’ press Space for more

## User Flow

\`\`\`
User presses ? 
    в”Ӯ
    в–ј
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  Keyboard Shortcuts                                    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ                                     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  Navigation                                            в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ    j/k     Move up/down                                в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ    Enter   View details                                в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ    ...                                                 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  Views                                                 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ    b       Kanban board                                в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ    g       Graph view                                  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ    ...                                                 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  Space: Interactive Tutorial в”Ӯ Esc: Close в”Ӯ ;: Sidebar в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                    в”Ӯ
                    в–ј (user presses Space)
                    в”Ӯ
            Full Tutorial opens
\`\`\`

## Implementation

### Update Help Modal View
Add footer hint in the help overlay:

\`\`\`go
func (m Model) renderHelp() string {
    // ... existing help content ...
    
    footer := helpFooterStyle.Render(
        "Space: Interactive Tutorial в”Ӯ Esc: Close в”Ӯ ;: Toggle Sidebar",
    )
    
    return lipgloss.JoinVertical(lipgloss.Left, content, footer)
}
\`\`\`

### Handle Space in Help Modal
\`\`\`go
func (m Model) handleHelpKeys(msg tea.KeyMsg) Model {
    switch msg.String() {
    case " ": // Space
        m.showHelp = false
        m.showTutorial = true
        m.tutorialModel.SetContextMode(false, "") // Full tutorial
        return m
    case "escape", "q", "?":
        m.showHelp = false
        return m
    // ... existing scroll handling ...
    }
    return m
}
\`\`\`

## Visual Design for Hint
- Footer should be subtle but visible
- "Space" highlighted in key style (like other shortcuts)
- "Interactive Tutorial" clearly indicates what happens
- Consistent with overall help modal styling

## Alternative Entry Points (for reference)
After this task, tutorial can be accessed via:
1. \`?\` вҶ’ \`Space\` (primary, discoverable)
2. \`CapsLock\` (power user shortcut)
3. \`CapsLock\` double-tap (context help)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Help modal footer shows Space hint
- [ ] Space from help modal opens full tutorial
- [ ] Help modal closes when tutorial opens
- [ ] Hint styling is consistent with theme
- [ ] No conflicts with existing help modal keys

## Testing
- Verify Space opens tutorial
- Verify other help modal functionality unaffected
- Verify hint is visible at various terminal sizes

## Dependencies
Depends on: Tutorial Model Infrastructure (needs tutorial to open)
Should coordinate with: CapsLock Key Detection (alternative entry)

в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   [ Yes, add it ]  [ No thanks ]  [ Don't ask again ]    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                          в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
\`\`\`

## Implementation

### Modal Component
\`\`\`go
type AgentPromptModal struct {
    selection  int       // 0=yes, 1=no, 2=never
    filePath   string    // Which file we're offering to modify
    theme      Theme
    width      int
    height     int
}

func NewAgentPromptModal(filePath string, theme Theme) AgentPromptModal
func (m AgentPromptModal) Update(msg tea.Msg) (AgentPromptModal, tea.Cmd)
func (m AgentPromptModal) View() string
\`\`\`

### Key Handling
- Tab / вҶҗ вҶ’ to switch between buttons
- Enter to confirm selection
- Escape to dismiss (same as "No thanks")
- y/n/d shortcuts for quick response

### Return Values
\`\`\`go
type agentPromptResult int

const (
    agentPromptAccept agentPromptResult = iota
    agentPromptDecline
    agentPromptNeverAsk
    agentPromptDismissed
)
\`\`\`

## Visual Styling
- Use theme colors consistently
- Primary color for "Yes" button (recommended action)
- Subtle styling for "Don't ask again"
- Content preview is scrollable if long

## Accessibility
- Clear button labels
- Keyboard navigation
- No auto-timeout (user controls dismissal)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Modal renders correctly
- [ ] Three-button layout works
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works
- [ ] Escape dismisses without action
- [ ] Result is returned to caller

## Dependencies
Depends on: AGENTS.md Blurb Content Definition (for preview)
close_reason: Implemented prompt modal UI with full test coverage

title: AGENTS.md Blurb Content Definition
description:
# AGENTS.md Blurb Content Definition

## Background
Define the exact content to be added to AGENTS.md files. This should be comprehensive, helpful for AI agents, and versioned for future updates.

## Blurb Content

\`\`\`markdown
<!-- bv-agent-instructions-v1 -->
## Beads Workflow Integration

This project uses [beads_viewer](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer) for issue tracking. Issues are stored as markdown files in \`.beads/\` and tracked in git.

### Essential Commands

\`\`\`bash
# View issues (launches TUI)
bv

# CLI commands for agents
bd ready              # Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
bd list --status=open # All open issues  
bd show <id>          # Full issue details
bd create --title="..." --type=task --priority=2
bd update <id> --status=in_progress
bd close <id> --reason="Completed"
bd sync               # Commit and push changes
\`\`\`

### Workflow Pattern

1. **Start**: Run \`bd ready\` to find actionable work
2. **Claim**: Use \`bd update <id> --status=in_progress\`
3. **Work**: Implement the task
4. **Complete**: Use \`bd close <id>\`
5. **Sync**: Always run \`bd sync\` at session end

### Key Concepts

- **Dependencies**: Issues can block other issues. \`bd ready\` shows only unblocked work.
- **Priority**: P0=critical, P1=high, P2=medium, P3=low, P4=backlog
- **Types**: task, bug, feature, epic, question, docs

### Best Practices

- Check \`bd ready\` at session start
- Update status as you work
- Create new issues with \`bd create\` when you discover tasks
- Use \`--body\` for rich descriptions
- Always \`bd sync\` before ending session
<!-- end-bv-agent-instructions -->
\`\`\`

## Version Strategy
- v1: Initial release
- Future versions: Add new sections, don't remove existing
- Marker includes version for upgrade detection

## Go Constant
\`\`\`go
const AgentBlurbV1 = \`<!-- bv-agent-instructions-v1 -->
## Beads Workflow Integration
...
<!-- end-bv-agent-instructions -->\`

const CurrentBlurbVersion = 1
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Blurb content is accurate and helpful
- [ ] Version marker is correct
- [ ] End marker present for future parsing
- [ ] Content tested with actual AI agents
- [ ] Matches content in README.md (or README references this)

## Review
- Should we link to full docs?
- Should we include TUI shortcuts?
- Is the workflow pattern clear?

## Dependencies
None - can be defined independently, just needs review.
close_reason: Implemented blurb content in pkg/agents/blurb.go with tests

title: AGENTS.md Project Preference Storage
description:
# AGENTS.md Project Preference Storage

## Background
When user declines the AGENTS.md prompt with "Don't ask again," we need to remember this per-project.

## Storage Design

### Location
\`\`\`
~/.config/bv/agent-prompts/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ <project-hash-1>.json
в”ңв”Җв”Җ <project-hash-2>.json
в””в”Җв”Җ ...
\`\`\`

### Project Hash
Use SHA256 of absolute working directory path (truncated):
\`\`\`go
func projectHash(workDir string) string {
    abs, _ := filepath.Abs(workDir)
    hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(abs))
    return hex.EncodeToString(hash[:8]) // 16 char hex
}
\`\`\`

### Preference File Format
\`\`\`json
{
    "project_path": "/Users/dev/myproject",
    "dont_ask_again": true,
    "declined_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z",
    "blurb_version_offered": 1
}
\`\`\`

### Operations

\`\`\`go
type AgentPromptPreference struct {
    ProjectPath         string    \`json:"project_path"\`
    DontAskAgain        bool      \`json:"dont_ask_again"\`
    DeclinedAt          time.Time \`json:"declined_at,omitempty"\`
    BlurbVersionOffered int       \`json:"blurb_version_offered"\`
    BlurbVersionAdded   int       \`json:"blurb_version_added,omitempty"\`
    AddedAt             time.Time \`json:"added_at,omitempty"\`
}

func LoadAgentPromptPreference(workDir string) (*AgentPromptPreference, error)
func SaveAgentPromptPreference(workDir string, pref AgentPromptPreference) error
func ShouldPromptForAgentFile(workDir string, currentVersion int) bool
\`\`\`

### Decision Logic
\`\`\`go
func ShouldPromptForAgentFile(workDir string, currentVersion int) bool {
    pref, err := LoadAgentPromptPreference(workDir)
    if err != nil {
        return true // New project, never prompted
    }
    
    if pref.DontAskAgain {
        // BUT: if we have a newer blurb version, maybe ask again?
        // For now: respect "don't ask again" absolutely
        return false
    }
    
    // They declined but didn't check "don't ask again"
    // For now: respect that too (prompt only on explicit request)
    return false
}
\`\`\`

## Future Consideration
- What if blurb version updates? Could offer upgrade prompt.
- For now, "don't ask again" means never for this project.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Preferences saved per-project
- [ ] Path hashing is consistent
- [ ] Load/save work correctly
- [ ] Missing preference file means "new project"
- [ ] Decision logic respects preferences

## Dependencies
None - can be developed independently.
close_reason: Implemented preference storage with full test coverage

title: Tutorial Progress Persistence
description:
# Tutorial Progress Persistence

## Background
Remember which tutorial pages the user has viewed so they can resume and see progress. Store in the same config system as other preferences.

## Data Structure

\`\`\`go
// In config package or similar
type TutorialProgress struct {
    ViewedPages    map[string]bool   \`json:"viewed_pages"\`      // page ID вҶ’ viewed
    LastPageID     string            \`json:"last_page_id"\`      // Resume point
    LastViewedTime time.Time         \`json:"last_viewed_time"\`
    CompletedOnce  bool              \`json:"completed_once"\`    // Saw all pages
}
\`\`\`

## Storage Location
\`\`\`
~/.config/bv/tutorial-progress.json
\`\`\`

## Operations

### Save Progress
\`\`\`go
func (m *TutorialModel) saveProgress() error {
    progress := TutorialProgress{
        ViewedPages:    m.viewedPages,
        LastPageID:     m.pages[m.currentPage].ID,
        LastViewedTime: time.Now(),
        CompletedOnce:  m.allPagesViewed(),
    }
    return config.SaveTutorialProgress(progress)
}
\`\`\`

### Load Progress
\`\`\`go
func (m *TutorialModel) loadProgress() {
    progress, err := config.LoadTutorialProgress()
    if err != nil {
        return // Fresh start
    }
    m.viewedPages = progress.ViewedPages
    if progress.LastPageID != "" {
        // Optionally resume at last page
        // Or always start at beginning but show checkmarks
    }
}
\`\`\`

### Mark Viewed
Called when user navigates away from a page:
\`\`\`go
func (m *TutorialModel) markCurrentPageViewed() {
    pageID := m.pages[m.currentPage].ID
    if !m.viewedPages[pageID] {
        m.viewedPages[pageID] = true
        // Debounced save or save on exit
    }
}
\`\`\`

## UI Integration
- TOC shows вң“ next to viewed pages
- Progress bar counts viewed/total
- "Resume where you left off?" prompt if applicable

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Progress persists across sessions
- [ ] Viewed pages show checkmarks in TOC
- [ ] Progress indicator reflects actual progress
- [ ] No data loss on crash (atomic writes)
- [ ] Works with --config flag for alternate location

## Considerations
- Don't auto-save on every page view (too many writes)
- Save on tutorial exit or periodically
- Handle corrupted config gracefully

## Dependencies
Depends on: Tutorial Model Infrastructure, Tutorial Keyboard Navigation
close_reason: Implemented tutorial progress persistence with TutorialProgress struct, tutorialProgressManager singleton, atomic file writes, and integration methods

title: Context-Specific Help Content
description:
# Context-Specific Help Content

## Background
When user double-taps CapsLock, they get a focused modal showing help for their current context - not the full tutorial, just what's immediately relevant.

## Content Structure

Each context gets a compact help card:

### List View Context Help
\`\`\`markdown
# List View Quick Reference

## Navigation
- j/k - Move selection up/down
- Enter - View issue details
- G/gg - Jump to end/start

## Filtering
- o - Show open issues only
- c - Show closed issues only
- r - Show ready (unblocked) issues
- / - Fuzzy search
- ~ - Semantic search

## Views
- b - Kanban board
- g - Graph view
- i - Insights panel

Press CapsLock for full tutorial
\`\`\`

### Graph View Context Help
\`\`\`markdown
# Graph View Quick Reference

## Navigation
- h/j/k/l - Navigate between nodes
- H/L - Scroll left/right
- Enter - Jump to selected issue
- Esc - Return to list

## Understanding the Graph
- Arrows show blocking direction (A вҶ’ B means A blocks B)
- Node size indicates priority
- Color indicates status

Press CapsLock for full tutorial
\`\`\`

### Similar cards for:
- detail, split, board, insights, history
- time-travel, filter, label-picker, recipe-picker

## Design
- Smaller modal than full tutorial (centered, ~60 chars wide)
- No TOC, no progress - just the content
- Dismiss with Escape or any navigation key
- Link to full tutorial at bottom

## Implementation
\`\`\`go
var contextHelp = map[string]string{
    "list":   listHelpContent,
    "graph":  graphHelpContent,
    // ...
}

func (m TutorialModel) ViewContextHelp(ctx string) string {
    content, ok := contextHelp[ctx]
    if !ok {
        content = genericHelpContent
    }
    // Render in compact modal style
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Help content for all ~10 contexts
- [ ] Each card fits on one screen (no scrolling needed)
- [ ] Most useful shortcuts prominently displayed
- [ ] Clear visual distinction from full tutorial
- [ ] Easy to dismiss

## Dependencies
Depends on: Context Detection System, Tutorial Model Infrastructure
close_reason: Added context_help.go with ContextHelpContent map, GetContextHelp(), and RenderContextHelp() for 15+ contexts. Tests pass.

title: Tutorial Keyboard Navigation
description:
# Tutorial Keyboard Navigation

## Background
The tutorial should feel like the rest of bv - vim-style navigation that's intuitive for the target audience.

## Key Bindings

### Page Navigation
- \`n\` / \`Space\` / \`вҶ’\` / \`l\` - Next page
- \`p\` / \`вҶҗ\` / \`h\` - Previous page
- \`g\` - Go to beginning (first page)
- \`G\` - Go to end (last page)
- Number + \`g\` - Go to page N

### Content Scrolling (within page)
- \`j\` / \`вҶ“\` - Scroll down
- \`k\` / \`вҶ‘\` - Scroll up
- \`Ctrl+d\` - Half page down
- \`Ctrl+u\` - Half page up
- \`gg\` - Top of page
- \`G\` (in scroll mode) - Bottom of page

### Table of Contents
- \`t\` - Toggle TOC visibility
- When TOC focused:
  - \`j/k\` to navigate sections
  - \`Enter\` to jump to section
  - \`Tab\` to switch focus back to content

### Exit
- \`Esc\` - Close tutorial, return to main view
- \`q\` - Same as Escape

## State Management
\`\`\`go
type tutorialFocus int

const (
    focusTutorialContent tutorialFocus = iota
    focusTutorialTOC
)

func (m TutorialModel) handleKeys(msg tea.KeyMsg) (TutorialModel, tea.Cmd) {
    switch m.focus {
    case focusTutorialContent:
        return m.handleContentKeys(msg)
    case focusTutorialTOC:
        return m.handleTOCKeys(msg)
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Progress Updates
- Mark page as viewed when user leaves it
- Store in persistent config
- Show checkmarks in TOC for viewed pages

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All navigation keys implemented
- [ ] Smooth scrolling within pages
- [ ] TOC navigation works
- [ ] Focus management between content and TOC
- [ ] Progress tracked as user navigates

## Dependencies
Depends on: Tutorial Model Infrastructure, Tutorial UI Layout
close_reason: Implemented focus management, half-page scroll, Space navigation, exit keys, and TOC cursor navigation

title: Tutorial Glamour Markdown Rendering
description:
# Tutorial Glamour Markdown Rendering

## Background
We already use Glamour for markdown rendering in detail views. This task extends that to tutorial content with consistent theming.

## Technical Requirements

### Glamour Configuration
\`\`\`go
func createTutorialRenderer(t Theme) (*glamour.TermRenderer, error) {
    // Create style that matches our theme
    style := glamour.DarkStyleConfig  // or create custom
    
    // Override colors to match theme
    style.Document.Color = pointer(colorToHex(t.Base.GetForeground()))
    style.Heading.Color = pointer(colorToHex(t.Primary))
    style.Code.Color = pointer(colorToHex(t.Accent))
    style.CodeBlock.Theme = "dracula"  // or theme-appropriate
    
    return glamour.NewTermRenderer(
        glamour.WithStyles(style),
        glamour.WithWordWrap(80),
    )
}
\`\`\`

### Content Preprocessing
- Embed markdown files with go:embed
- Or store as string constants for simpler builds
- Handle relative image paths (probably skip images)

### Special Rendering
- Callout boxes: custom syntax вҶ’ lipgloss boxes
- Key hints: \`вҢҳK\` style rendering
- Interactive prompts: "Try pressing 'g' now!"

### Syntax Highlighting
- Code blocks with language tags
- Use chroma via glamour's built-in support
- Theme-appropriate colors

## Integration
\`\`\`go
func (m *TutorialModel) renderContent(page TutorialPage) string {
    rendered, err := m.glamourRenderer.Render(page.Content)
    if err != nil {
        return page.Content // Fallback to raw
    }
    return rendered
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Markdown renders with theme-consistent colors
- [ ] Code blocks have syntax highlighting
- [ ] Headers have visual hierarchy
- [ ] Lists, blockquotes, links all render properly
- [ ] Word wrapping respects terminal width

## Reuse Existing Code
Check what's already in pkg/ui for markdown rendering (detail view uses it).
Reuse rather than duplicate.

## Dependencies
Depends on: Tutorial Model Infrastructure, Tutorial UI Layout
close_reason: Integrated Glamour markdown renderer with theme support, code highlighting, tables, and proper formatting

The tutorial needs beautiful, consistent visual presentation. This task defines the layout, navigation chrome, and visual hierarchy.

title: Tutorial Content: Real-World Workflows
description:
# Tutorial Content: Real-World Workflows

## Background
Theory is great, but users need to see how everything fits together in practice. This section provides complete, realistic scenarios.

## Content Outline

### Scenario 1: Starting a New Feature
Walk through:
1. Check bd ready for actionable work
2. Find the feature request, review in detail view
3. Update status to in_progress
4. Discover sub-tasks needed вҶ’ create them with dependencies
5. Work through sub-tasks, closing as completed
6. Final testing, close parent feature
7. bd sync to commit all changes

### Scenario 2: Triaging a Bug Report
Walk through:
1. Bug comes in (maybe from agent or external)
2. Use S key for triage suggestions
3. Set priority based on severity
4. Add labels for categorization
5. Identify if it blocks other work
6. Assign or leave for ready queue

### Scenario 3: Sprint Planning Session
Walk through:
1. Review Insights panel for priority scores
2. Use Graph view to understand dependencies
3. Filter to ready issues
4. Discuss and assign (labels or status)
5. Export sprint plan with x key
6. Time-travel at sprint end to see progress

### Scenario 4: Onboarding a New Team Member
Walk through:
1. Point them to bv (it's in the repo!)
2. They run through this tutorial
3. Show them the ? help and ; sidebar
4. Assign a starter issue
5. They find it with bd ready

### Scenario 5: Weekly Review with Stakeholders
Walk through:
1. Use --pages to generate static site
2. Deploy to GitHub Pages (or share locally)
3. Non-technical stakeholders can browse
4. Link specific issues in discussions

## Visual Elements
- Step-by-step command sequences
- Screenshots/representations of each state
- "Your turn" practice suggestions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 5 complete workflow scenarios
- [ ] Each scenario end-to-end realistic
- [ ] Covers both human and agent workflows
- [ ] Includes common gotchas/tips

## Dependencies
Depends on: All other content tasks (references features explained elsewhere)

## Background
A key differentiator: beads_viewer is designed to work with AI coding agents. This section explains how to maximize that synergy.

\`\`\`bash
# Agent workflow pattern
bd ready                    # Find actionable work
bd show bt-123             # Get full context
# ... do the work ...
bd update bt-123 --status=in_progress
# ... complete work ...
bd close bt-123 --reason="Implemented feature"
bd sync                    # Commit and push
\`\`\`

### Page 4: Best Practices for Agents
- Start session: check bd ready
- Update status as you work
- Use bd create for new issues discovered
- Always bd sync at end
- Use --body for rich descriptions

title: CapsLock Key Detection
description:
# CapsLock Key Detection

## Background
CapsLock is a "power user" shortcut for direct tutorial access. The **primary discoverable path** is pressing `?` (help modal) then `Space` for the tutorial. CapsLock provides a quicker bypass for users who know it exists.

## Design Decision: CapsLock as Secondary Entry Point

The user flow priority is:
1. **Primary (discoverable)**: `?` вҶ’ Help modal вҶ’ `Space` вҶ’ Tutorial
2. **Power user shortcut**: `CapsLock` вҶ’ Tutorial directly
3. **Context help**: `CapsLock` double-tap вҶ’ Context-specific help

This ensures users can always access the tutorial even if their terminal doesn't handle CapsLock well.

## Technical Challenge
BubbleTea represents CapsLock differently across platforms:
- Some terminals send it as a regular key event
- Some send it as a special key
- Some don't send it at all (captured by OS)
- vim users often remap CapsLock to Escape

## Implementation Strategy

### CapsLock Detection (Best Effort)
\`\`\`go
func isCapsLock(msg tea.KeyMsg) bool {
    // Terminal-dependent detection
    // May need to check msg.Type and msg.String()
    // Return false if uncertain (user can use ? вҶ’ Space)
}
\`\`\`

### Double-Tap Detection
\`\`\`go
type capsLockTracker struct {
    lastPress time.Time
    threshold time.Duration // 300ms for double-tap
}

func (c *capsLockTracker) HandlePress() tutorialTrigger {
    now := time.Now()
    if now.Sub(c.lastPress) < c.threshold {
        c.lastPress = time.Time{} // Reset
        return triggerContextHelp
    }
    c.lastPress = now
    return tea.Tick(c.threshold, func(time.Time) tea.Msg {
        return capsLockTimerExpiredMsg{}
    })
}
\`\`\`

## Graceful Degradation
If CapsLock detection proves unreliable on certain terminals:
- Users always have `?` вҶ’ `Space` path
- Could show hint on first run: "Tip: CapsLock opens tutorial (or use ? then Space)"
- Consider making CapsLock binding configurable

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CapsLock detected in common terminals (iTerm2, Terminal.app, etc.)
- [ ] Double-tap within 300ms triggers context help
- [ ] Single tap triggers full tutorial after delay
- [ ] Graceful fallback when CapsLock not detected
- [ ] No interference with actual CapsLock toggle

## Testing Notes
- Document which terminals work/don't work
- Ensure ? вҶ’ Space path always works as fallback

## Dependencies
None - but coordinates with Help Modal Update task.
close_reason: Closed

title: Tutorial Model Infrastructure
description:
# Tutorial Model Infrastructure

## Background
The tutorial system needs a robust state machine to manage multi-page navigation, progress tracking, and integration with the main BubbleTea model. This is the foundation all other tutorial work builds upon.

## Technical Design

### TutorialModel struct
\`\`\`go
type TutorialModel struct {
    pages         []TutorialPage
    currentPage   int
    scrollOffset  int
    tocVisible    bool      // Table of contents sidebar
    progress      map[string]bool  // Sections viewed
    width, height int
    theme         Theme
    contextMode   bool      // true = context-specific help
    context       string    // Current view context (list, board, graph, etc.)
}

type TutorialPage struct {
    ID          string    // e.g., "intro", "navigation", "graph-view"
    Title       string
    Content     string    // Markdown content
    Section     string    // Parent section for TOC grouping
    Context     []string  // Which contexts this page applies to
}
\`\`\`

### Key Methods
- `Init() tea.Cmd` - Initialize tutorial
- `Update(msg tea.Msg) (TutorialModel, tea.Cmd)` - Handle navigation
- `View() string` - Render current page with chrome
- `NextPage()`, `PrevPage()` - Navigation
- `JumpToSection(id string)` - TOC navigation
- `SetContext(ctx string)` - Filter to context-relevant pages
- `MarkViewed(pageID string)` - Track progress

### Integration Points
- Main Model gets `showTutorial bool` and `tutorialModel TutorialModel`
- Tutorial is an overlay (like help), not a new view mode
- Can exit anytime with Escape

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TutorialModel struct defined with all fields
- [ ] Basic page navigation (next/prev) works
- [ ] Scroll within long pages works
- [ ] Progress tracking persists to config
- [ ] Integrates with main Model as overlay
- [ ] Escape closes tutorial, returns to previous state

## Implementation Notes
- Use atomic.Value for thread-safe progress updates if needed
- Consider whether to pause file watcher during tutorial (probably not)
- Page content loaded lazily or all at once? All at once is fine for ~10 pages

## Estimated Complexity
Medium - This is foundational but well-understood patterns from existing overlays (help, label picker).

## Dependencies
None - this is the foundation.
close_reason: Implemented TutorialModel with page navigation, scrolling, TOC, context filtering, and progress tracking. All 18 tests pass.

title: Perf: avoid duplicate analyzer/triage computations in TUI startup
description:
## Problem
`pkg/ui/model.go` currently does redundant work:
- builds an Analyzer + starts AnalyzeAsync
- calls `analysis.ComputeTriage(issues)` which builds another Analyzer + AnalyzeAsync
- later recomputes triage on Phase2Ready

This can double/triple analysis costs on large graphs.

## Goal
Reuse the already-constructed analyzer/stats for triage and related UI panels.

## Constraints
- Do not block startup waiting for Phase 2.
- Preserve semantics (including graceful degradation when Phase 2 isnвҖҷt ready).
close_reason: Accomplished via bv-runn.11 (ComputeTriageFromAnalyzer entrypoint) and bv-runn.12 (wired TUI to reuse analyzer/stats). Now TUI startup reuses single analyzer for both graph stats and triage.

title: analysis: add triage entrypoint that reuses analyzer/stats
description:
## Goal
Add a triage helper that avoids rebuilding the graph.

## Direction
Refactor the triage core so it can operate using:
- an existing `*analysis.Analyzer` (dependency queries)
- an existing `*analysis.GraphStats` (centrality metrics, phase2 status)
- a fixed `now`

## Acceptance
Must match `ComputeTriageWithOptionsAndTime` outputs given equivalent inputs.
close_reason: Added ComputeTriageFromAnalyzer function that accepts pre-built analyzer and stats. This enables TUI startup to reuse existing analysis instead of rebuilding. Includes 3 tests verifying equivalence.

title: ui: wire TUI startup to reuse analyzer/stats for triage
description:
## Goal
Update `pkg/ui/model.go` to stop calling `analysis.ComputeTriage()` redundantly.

## Notes
- Keep Phase 1 fast.
- Ensure list sort/filter correctness after Phase 2 completes.
close_reason: Wired TUI startup to use ComputeTriageFromAnalyzer at 3 locations (init, Phase2 handler, insights refresh) to reuse analyzer/stats instead of recomputing

title: Robot diff-since auto-enable should not write to stderr
description: In robot contexts BV_ROBOT=1 can cause --diff-since to auto-enable --robot-diff. That auto-enable path must not emit advisory text to stderr, and related comments should reflect that warnings are suppressed in robot mode.
close_reason: Fixed: removed stderr advisory on diff-since auto-enable; clarified loader/sprint robot warning suppression.

title: Diff-since auto-JSON should set BV_ROBOT early
description:
When stdout is non-TTY, bv --diff-since REF auto-emits JSON diff (per README). However, issues are loaded before auto-enabling --robot-diff, so malformed JSONL lines can still emit parse warnings to stderr and break JSON consumers.

Acceptance:
- bv --diff-since REF in non-TTY context produces JSON-only stdout and empty stderr even with malformed JSONL lines.
- Suppress non-fatal warnings to stderr for robot JSON output paths (e.g., drift config fallback, recipe load errors, empty label scope) when BV_ROBOT=1.
- Add an E2E test covering auto-JSON diff with malformed issues.
close_reason: Treat --diff-since + non-TTY as robot mode early (set BV_ROBOT before loading issues) so parse warnings donвҖҷt hit stderr when diff auto-emits JSON. Also suppress non-fatal warnings on robot JSON paths (drift config fallback, recipe load, empty label scope, workspace failures). Added E2E test ensuring auto-JSON diff stays stderr-clean with malformed issues.jsonl.

title: workspace loader: avoid stderr log noise (keep robot JSON clean)
description: pkg/workspace/NewAggregateLoader currently defaults to log.Default() and prints a completion log line for parallel loading. This can pollute stderr and break callers/tests that capture CombinedOutput for robot JSON. Make workspace loading silent by default and add an E2E test ensuring --robot-triage --workspace emits clean JSON with empty stderr.
acceptance_criteria:
- Running bv --robot-triage --workspace <config> emits valid JSON on stdout with empty stderr in tests
- No behavioral regression for workspace loading
- go test ./... passes
close_reason: Fixed: workspace loader defaults to silent logger; added E2E to ensure robot triage stdout clean

title: watcher: Debouncer should drop stale timer callbacks
description: Debouncer currently uses time.AfterFunc + Stop(), which can still allow the previous callback to run if it has already started. This can violate the documented 'only last callback runs' behavior and can fire after Cancel/Trigger races. Make Debouncer generation-based so only the latest trigger executes.
acceptance_criteria:
- Debouncer guarantees at-most-once callback for a burst (ignoring stale timers)
- Cancel prevents callback even if timer fires concurrently
- Existing watcher tests remain stable
close_reason: Fixed: generation-based debouncer ignores stale timer callbacks

title: Fix staticcheck warnings across analysis/export/ui
description: Run staticcheck and fix obvious findings: remove dead code/unused fields, fix minor style warnings, update deprecated viewport scroll calls, and address potential nil deref in export tests.
notes: Fixed staticcheck ./... findings: removed dead/unused helpers and fields (analysis/ui/cmd), cleaned up minor style issues (error strings, unnecessary fmt.Sprintf), updated viewport scroll calls to ScrollUp/Down, and tightened nil-safety in export tests. go test ./... and staticcheck clean.
