title: Add skip guards for Unix-only permission tests
description: 7 tests use chmod 0000/0555 which has no effect on Windows. Add runtime.GOOS skip guards. Affects pkg/agents (atomic write), pkg/drift (config load/save), pkg/ui (background worker permission error), tests/e2e (binary permissions).
close_reason: All 39 Windows test failures fixed - go test ./... passes with 0 failures

title: Redesign issue list column header (TYPE PRI STATUS ID TITLE)
description:
## Why

The column header bar ('TYPE PRI STATUS ID TITLE') works but feels utilitarian. It's a prominent visual element - full-width blue banner at the top of the issues panel - and could be cleaner.

## What

Redesign the issue list column header. Current implementation: hardcoded string with manual spacing (model.go:4802), Primary background + BgContrast foreground.

Ideas to explore:
- Better column alignment (proportional to actual content widths)
- Subtle separators between columns instead of just spaces
- Lighter touch - dimmer background, or just an underline instead of a full banner
- Whether all columns need to be shown (TYPE could be an icon only)

Implementation details:
- pkg/ui/model.go:4795-4807
- Workspace mode variant adds REPO column
- Header must align with the list item rendering (delegate pattern in Bubble Tea)
- Column widths are currently implicit in the delegate's Render method
- Split view (issues + details) means the header width varies

## Acceptance Criteria

- Column header is visually polished and aligned with item rendering
- Design works in both single-repo and workspace mode (with REPO column)
- Works correctly in split view where header width varies
notes:
## Current implementation

pkg/ui/model.go:4795-4807
- Hardcoded string: "  TYPE PRI STATUS      ID                                   TITLE"
- Full-width Primary background banner
- Workspace mode variant adds REPO column

## Design considerations

- Header must align with the list item rendering below it (delegate pattern in Bubble Tea)
- Column widths are currently implicit in the delegate's Render method
- Any redesign needs to stay in sync with how items are rendered
- The split view (issues + details) means the header width varies

## Related

Part of broader UX polish. Could tie into bt-ks0w (mouse click) since clickable column headers for sorting would be a natural extension.

title: Investigate Dolt changelog/history view in bt
description:
## Why

Dolt's core value prop is git-for-data - it has dolt_log and dolt_diff system tables that track every commit to the database. bt doesn't surface any of this yet. This aligns bt with beads' architectural direction (Dolt was chosen specifically for its versioning/branching capabilities).

## What

Investigate what Dolt exposes via SQL and determine what would be useful to show in bt.

Potential uses:
- Seeing what changed and when (commit log for issues)
- Diffing between two points in time
- Understanding who changed what (if multiple actors)
- Audit trail / accountability

Relevant Dolt system tables:
- dolt_log: commit history (hash, author, date, message)
- dolt_diff_<table>: row-level diffs between commits
- dolt_commit_diff_<table>: diffs between any two commits
- dolt_status: working set changes (uncommitted)
- dolt_branches: branch info

Open questions:
- Does beads auto-commit on every write, or batch? (Check bd's dolt-auto-commit config)
- What granularity do commits have? Per-issue-update or per-bd-command?
- Are commit messages meaningful or generic?
- How much history is typical? (Performance concern for large projects)

UI placement TBD: History view? Separate panel? Detail pane section?

Related: bt-spzz (smarter reload status) could use dolt_diff for precise change detection.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Dolt history/diff capabilities documented with what's available via SQL
- Recommendation on what to surface in bt and where in the UI
notes:
## Dolt system tables to investigate

- dolt_log: commit history (hash, author, date, message)
- dolt_diff_<table>: row-level diffs between commits
- dolt_commit_diff_<table>: diffs between any two commits
- dolt_status: working set changes (uncommitted)
- dolt_branches: branch info

## Questions

- Does beads auto-commit on every write, or batch? (Check bd's dolt-auto-commit config)
- What granularity do commits have? Per-issue-update or per-bd-command?
- Are commit messages meaningful or generic?
- How much history is typical? (Performance concern for large projects)

## Why Dolt was chosen for beads

Per upstream context: Dolt was chosen specifically for its versioning/branching capabilities. The changelog/history is a first-class feature of the storage layer that bt should eventually expose. This aligns bt with beads' architectural direction.

title: Audit bt CLI ergonomics for agent/robot usage
description:
Dogfood finding: the --robot-search command requires a separate --search flag for the query text (e.g. 'bt --robot-search --search "ports dev tools"' instead of 'bt --robot-search "ports dev tools"'). This feels unintuitive, especially for agents who might try the natural form first.

Broader scope: audit the full bt CLI surface for agent-friendliness. Are flags composed logically? Are error messages clear when args are wrong? Could positional args work where we currently require named flags?

This came from real usage where the error message on the wrong form was cryptic rather than helpful.

Not urgent - investigate when there's bandwidth. Implementation is separate from the audit.
notes:
## Scope

Two levels of work:

1. **Audit** (this bead) - catalog every CLI flag/subcommand and evaluate ergonomics
2. **Fixes** (separate beads) - individual improvements found during the audit

## What to audit

- All --robot-* flags: are they composable? Do they accept positional args where it makes sense?
- Error messages on malformed commands: are they actionable or cryptic?
- Flag naming consistency: do similar flags use similar names across subcommands?
- Positional arg support: where named flags add friction without adding clarity
- Help text: does --help surface the right info for both humans and agents?
- Exit codes: do failures return distinct codes agents can branch on?

## Known issues

1. --robot-search requires separate --search flag for query text (screenshot in bt-pfic creation context, session 13). Error message on wrong form was "cryptic" per user - need to reproduce and capture exact message.

## Agent context

bt has a --robot mode (AGENTS.md / SKILL.md) designed for AI agent consumption. The CLI should be optimized for both human and agent callers - agents shouldn't need to guess flag composition or parse ambiguous errors.

## Approach

- Run through every CLI path in --help
- Try natural/intuitive flag forms and document where they fail
- Compare against CLI conventions (cobra best practices, POSIX standards)
- File individual beads for each fix, link back to this audit
close_reason: Audit complete: 97 flags inventoried, 3 critical robot-mode envelope bugs, agent-friendliness 3.4/5. Report at docs/audit/cli-ergonomics-audit.md. Fixes are separate beads.

title: Investigate: manual Dolt refresh/reconnect keybind
description:
## Why

bt already prevents Dolt idle timeout via 5s polling that touches .beads/dolt-server.activity. But there are edge cases where a manual refresh would help:
1. Connection lost + exponential backoff (up to 2min) - manual refresh could force immediate reconnect
2. User wants to force-pull fresh data NOW instead of waiting for next tick

Original idea included cross-repo keepalive while multitasking, but bt can't solve this since it only runs for one repo at a time - that would need a beads-level background daemon.

## What

Add a keybind (e.g. R or Ctrl+R) to manually trigger an immediate Dolt poll + activity file write, bypassing the 5s interval and any backoff state.

Implementation context:
- Poll loop: pkg/ui/background_worker.go:1937-2014
- Activity write: pkg/ui/background_worker.go:2019-2028 (touchDoltActivity)
- Backoff on failure: 5s -> 2min cap
- Env: BT_DOLT_POLL_INTERVAL_S (default 5s)

Verdict: Low priority QoL. Main value is resetting stuck backoff after transient failures.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Keybind triggers immediate Dolt poll + activity file write
- Resets any exponential backoff state
- Visual feedback confirms refresh was triggered
notes:
DOGFOOD CONTEXT (2026-04-08):
Ctrl+R (Force refresh) shown in help overlay under Actions. But in global mode, neither automatic polling nor manual refresh seems to trigger data reload. Related to bt-pjvi (global polling broken). The manual refresh keybind may work in single-repo mode but not global.

title: Center divider in details pane
description: Center the horizontal separator line in the details pane meta table (the line between 'ID STATUS PRIORITY ASSIGNEE CREATED' header and the field values). It should span border-to-border. Currently rendered as a markdown table via Glamour - may need custom Glamour style or switch to hand-rendered lipgloss layout for the meta table header.

title: Viewport: Validate performance benchmarks
description:
## Task: Validate Performance Benchmarks

### Background

After implementing viewport scrolling, we need to verify the performance improvements meet our targets. The benchmarks in `tree_bench_test.go` should show significant improvement.

### Current benchmark results (before viewport)

```
BenchmarkTreeRender/50_visible_80x24:   ~1.8ms
BenchmarkTreeRender/100_visible_120x40: ~9.9ms
BenchmarkTreeRender/200_visible_160x50: ~38ms  <- EXCEEDS TARGET
BenchmarkTreeRender100:                 ~6.6ms
```

### Target results (after viewport)

```
BenchmarkTreeRender/50_visible_80x24:   < 1ms
BenchmarkTreeRender/100_visible_120x40: < 2ms
BenchmarkTreeRender/200_visible_160x50: < 5ms  <- Now within target
BenchmarkTreeRender100:                 < 2ms
```

The key insight: with viewport scrolling, render time should be O(viewport height), not O(total nodes). So 200 nodes with viewport of 50 should render as fast as 50 nodes.

### Validation steps

1. **Run benchmarks before changes** (baseline)
   ```bash
   go test -bench=BenchmarkTreeRender -benchmem ./pkg/ui/... > bench_before.txt
   ```

2. **Run benchmarks after changes**
   ```bash
   go test -bench=BenchmarkTreeRender -benchmem ./pkg/ui/... > bench_after.txt
   ```

3. **Compare results**
   ```bash
   benchstat bench_before.txt bench_after.txt
   ```

### Expected improvements

| Benchmark | Before | After | Improvement |
|-----------|--------|-------|-------------|
| Render 50 nodes | ~1.8ms | ~0.5ms | 3.6x |
| Render 100 nodes | ~9.9ms | ~1ms | 10x |
| Render 200 nodes | ~38ms | ~2ms | 19x |

### Memory allocation improvements

With viewport rendering:
- String builder allocates only for visible lines
- No style computation for non-visible nodes
- Expected: 50-80% reduction in allocations

### Test to add

```go
// BenchmarkTreeRenderLargeWithViewport specifically tests viewport impact
func BenchmarkTreeRenderLargeWithViewport(b *testing.B) {
    theme := DefaultTheme(lipgloss.NewRenderer(nil))
    issues := generateHierarchyIssues(100, 4, 4) // ~1000 issues
    
    tree := NewTreeModel(theme)
    tree.Build(issues)
    tree.SetSize(120, 40) // Viewport of 40 lines
    tree.ExpandAll()
    
    // Scroll to middle
    tree.cursor = tree.NodeCount() / 2
    tree.ensureCursorVisible()
    
    b.ReportAllocs()
    b.ResetTimer()
    
    for i := 0; i < b.N; i++ {
        _ = tree.View()
    }
    
    // Target: < 5ms for 1000 issues with 40-line viewport
}
```

### Files to modify
- `pkg/ui/tree_bench_test.go` - Add new benchmarks, update existing if needed

### Success Criteria
- [ ] All render benchmarks meet targets (< 5ms for visible viewport)
- [ ] Memory allocations reduced by > 50%
- [ ] No performance regression for small trees
- [ ] benchstat shows statistically significant improvement

### Dependencies
- bv-db02 (windowed rendering) - must be complete to measure

### Notes
- Run benchmarks multiple times for statistical significance
- Use `-count=10` for benchstat comparison
- Document results in commit message
close_reason: Benchmark validation should be part of implementation, not a separate task. Folded into bv-db02.

title: Viewport: Add scrollbar position indicator
description:
## Task: Add Scrollbar Position Indicator

### Background

When viewing a large tree with viewport scrolling, users need visual feedback about their position in the list. A scrollbar indicator shows:
- Current position (where am I?)
- Total size (how much is there?)
- Viewport proportion (how much am I seeing?)

### Design Options

#### Option A: Text-based indicator (Recommended for MVP)
Simple text showing position:
```
[50-60 of 100]
```
or
```
50% вҶ“
```

#### Option B: ASCII scrollbar
Visual indicator on right edge:
```
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Epic 1                          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Task 1                      в”ғ  <- scrollbar thumb
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Task 2                      в”ғ
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Epic 2                          в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Task 3                      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Task 4                      в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җ Epic 3                          в”Ӯ
```

#### Option C: Unicode block scrollbar
Using Unicode block characters for smoother appearance:
```
в–Ғ (1/8 block) - top of range
в–Ӯв–ғв–„в–…в–Ҷв–Үв–Ҳ - increasing density
```

### Recommendation

Start with **Option A** (text indicator) as it's simplest and doesn't affect tree rendering. Can add visual scrollbar later.

### Implementation (Option A)

```go
// In View(), add position indicator at bottom or top
func (t *TreeModel) View() string {
    // ... existing rendering ...
    
    // Add position indicator
    if len(t.flatList) > t.height {
        start, end := t.visibleRange()
        indicator := fmt.Sprintf(" [%d-%d of %d]", start+1, end, len(t.flatList))
        sb.WriteString(t.theme.Muted.Render(indicator))
    }
    
    return sb.String()
}
```

### Implementation (Option B - Future)

Would require:
1. Calculate scrollbar height based on viewport/total ratio
2. Calculate thumb position based on offset/total
3. Render scrollbar characters in right column
4. May need to reduce tree width by 1-2 chars

### Test cases

```go
func TestScrollbarIndicator(t *testing.T) {
    tree := createTreeWithNodes(100)
    tree.SetSize(80, 10)
    tree.viewportOffset = 50
    
    output := tree.View()
    
    // Should contain position indicator
    if !strings.Contains(output, "51-60 of 100") && 
       !strings.Contains(output, "[51-60") {
        t.Error("position indicator not found")
    }
}

func TestScrollbarNotShownForSmallTree(t *testing.T) {
    tree := createTreeWithNodes(5)
    tree.SetSize(80, 10)
    
    output := tree.View()
    
    // Should NOT contain position indicator (all visible)
    if strings.Contains(output, " of ") {
        t.Error("position indicator should not show for small tree")
    }
}
```

### Files to modify
- `pkg/ui/tree.go` - Add scrollbar rendering to View()

### Success Criteria
- [ ] Position indicator shows when scrolling is needed
- [ ] Indicator hidden when all nodes fit
- [ ] Numbers are accurate (1-indexed for user display)
- [ ] Styling matches theme (muted/subtle)

### Dependencies
- bv-db02 (windowed rendering) - needs viewport to be working

### Priority
P3 - Nice to have, not critical for functionality. Can be skipped in MVP.

### Notes
- Keep it simple for now - text indicator is sufficient
- Visual scrollbar can be a separate enhancement later
- Consider keyboard shortcut to show/hide indicator
close_reason: Added text-based position indicator [start-end of total] that shows when scrolling is needed. Hidden for small trees.

title: Viewport: Update View() for windowed rendering
close_reason: Updated View() to use visibleRange() for O(viewport) performance. Only renders visible nodes based on viewportOffset. Added comprehensive tests.

title: Viewport: Track offset and implement cursor-follows-viewport
close_reason: All navigation methods now call ensureCursorVisible(). Implementation verified: MoveDown, MoveUp, JumpToTop, JumpToBottom, PageUp, PageDown, JumpToParent, ExpandOrMoveToChild, CollapseOrJumpToParent. Tests added: TestEnsureCursorVisible (13 cases), TestEnsureCursorVisibleNegativeOffset, TestNavigationCallsEnsureCursorVisible. All tests pass.

close_reason: Added viewportOffset field and visibleRange() method with O(1) calculation. 12 tests cover all edge cases including empty tree, boundary conditions, and performance.

title: Epic: Tree View Viewport Scrolling
description:
## Background & Motivation

The tree view (`pkg/ui/tree.go`) currently renders ALL visible nodes in the `View()` method. For typical usage (50-100 visible nodes), performance is acceptable:

- 50 nodes @ 80x24: ~1.1ms вң“
- 100 nodes @ 120x40: ~6.3ms вң“ (close to 5ms target)
- 200 nodes @ 160x50: ~31ms вҡ пёҸ (edge case - rarely seen in practice)

**This epic is P3 (nice-to-have)** because current performance is acceptable for typical usage. Only pursue if users report actual slowness with large expanded trees.

## Key Insight: USE EXISTING BUBBLES VIEWPORT

The `TreeModel` already has an unused `viewport.Model` field:
```go
type TreeModel struct {
    viewport viewport.Model  // <-- EXISTS BUT UNUSED
    // ...
}
```

**Instead of reimplementing**, we should leverage `github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/viewport` which handles:
- Content scrolling
- Cursor-follows-viewport
- Page up/down
- Keyboard bindings

This significantly reduces implementation effort.

## Approach

1. Feed rendered tree content to viewport.Model
2. Let viewport handle scrolling logic
3. Only implement custom logic if viewport doesn't meet needs

## Success Criteria

- [ ] BenchmarkTreeRender100 < 5ms (currently ~6.3ms - already close)
- [ ] Memory allocations reduced for large trees
- [ ] Smooth scrolling using bubbles viewport
- [ ] Cursor always visible

## When to Implement

- **Now**: If users report tree view slowness with 100+ visible nodes
- **Defer**: If no user complaints - current performance is acceptable

None - tree view is stable (bv-gllx complete вң“)

title: History View Session Timeline Integration
description:
# History View Session Timeline Integration

## Purpose
Integrate cass sessions into the History view timeline, showing sessions alongside commits in chronological order. This provides the richest context for understanding how work was done.

## вҡ пёҸ COORDINATION NOTE
This bead should be implemented **after or in coordination with** the History View Overhaul epic (bv-1d4i). The History view is being redesigned, and implementing session integration before that is complete would require rework.

**Recommended approach:** 
1. Complete History view overhaul (bv-1d4i) first
2. Then add session integration to the new architecture
3. OR coordinate closely if implementing in parallel

## Background

### Why History View?
The History view is specifically designed for retrospective analysisвҖ”understanding "what happened" for a bead. Coding sessions are a crucial part of that story:

- Commits show WHAT changed
- Sessions show HOW/WHY it was changed

Combining both in a timeline tells the complete story.

### Current History View Structure
```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ bv-abc123: Fix token refresh timeout                        в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ Timeline                                                    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                             в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Dec 17, 3:45pm  рҹ“қ e1f2a3b "Fix retry logic"              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Dec 17, 2:00pm  рҹ“қ 9d8c7b6 "Add timeout handling"         в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Dec 16, 5:00pm  рҹ“қ a1b2c3d "Initial auth implementation"  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                             в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Dec 15, 10:00am вңЁ Created                                  в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

### With Session Integration
```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ bv-abc123: Fix token refresh timeout                        в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ Timeline                                                    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                             в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Dec 17, 5:30pm  вң“ Closed                                   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Dec 17, 3:45pm  рҹ“қ e1f2a3b "Fix retry logic"              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Dec 17, 3:00pm  рҹ“Һ Claude Code session (42 messages)       в”Ӯ  вҶҗ NEW
в”Ӯ Dec 17, 2:00pm  рҹ“қ 9d8c7b6 "Add timeout handling"         в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Dec 17, 1:30pm  рҹ“Һ Cursor session (18 messages)            в”Ӯ  вҶҗ NEW
в”Ӯ Dec 16, 5:00pm  рҹ“қ a1b2c3d "Initial auth implementation"  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                                             в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Dec 15, 10:00am вңЁ Created                                  в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

## Implementation

### Timeline Entry Types
```go
type TimelineEntryType int
const (
    EntryCommit TimelineEntryType = iota
    EntrySession  // NEW
    EntryCreated
    EntryClosed
    EntryMilestone
)

type TimelineEntry struct {
    Type      TimelineEntryType
    Timestamp time.Time
    // For commits:
    CommitHash string
    CommitMsg  string
    // For sessions:
    SessionPath string
    Agent       string
    MessageCount int
}
```

### Correlation Strategy for History View
Unlike the main list view (which correlates on selection), History view should:
1. Correlate **only the selected bead** (not all visible)
2. Use cached results if available
3. Trigger background fetch if not cached
4. Display commits immediately, add sessions when ready

This avoids the performance problem of batch-correlating many beads.

### Merging Timelines
```go
func mergeTimelines(commits []TimelineEntry, sessions []TimelineEntry) []TimelineEntry {
    // Combine and sort by timestamp descending
    // Handle timestamp ties: commits before sessions at same timestamp
    // This reflects that commits are the "result" of session work
}
```

### Session Entry Rendering
```go
func renderSessionEntry(entry TimelineEntry, theme Theme) string {
    // Dec 17, 3:00pm  рҹ“Һ Claude Code session (42 messages)
    timestamp := entry.Timestamp.Format("Jan 2, 3:04pm")
    return fmt.Sprintf("%s  рҹ“Һ %s session (%d messages)",
        timestamp, entry.Agent, entry.MessageCount)
}
```

### Selecting a Session Entry
When user navigates to and selects a session entry:
- Show expanded preview inline (2-3 key snippets)
- Or: Show message "Press C for full session preview"
- Consistent with main list view modal behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sessions appear in timeline with correct timestamps
- [ ] Sessions sorted correctly with commits (commits first on ties)
- [ ] Session entries show agent and message count
- [ ] Can navigate to session entries with j/k
- [ ] Selecting session shows preview or hint
- [ ] Timeline loads immediately (sessions added async)
- [ ] Timeline still works perfectly when cass unavailable

## Graceful Degradation
If cass unavailable or returns empty:
- Timeline shows commits only (existing behavior)
- No "no sessions" message
- No visual indication of missing feature
- No loading spinners

## Performance Considerations
- Correlate only selected bead (not batch)
- Use cached results aggressively
- Limit session search to bead activity window (+/- 7 days)
- Sessions appear async (don't block commit display)

## Testing Strategy
- Test timeline merging algorithm
- Test various timestamp orderings
- Test tie-breaking (commits before sessions)
- Test empty session lists
- Test navigation behavior
- Test async loading (sessions appear after commits)
- Visual regression test
close_reason: Session timeline integration complete: Sessions appear in timeline with timestamps, sorted correctly with commits, entries show agent/message count, navigation works, async loading implemented, graceful degradation when cass unavailable. All tests pass.

title: Status Bar Session Indicator
description:
# Status Bar Session Indicator

## Purpose
Show a subtle indicator in the status bar when the selected bead has correlated coding sessions. This is the "discovery" mechanismвҖ”how users learn that session context is available.

## Background

### Design Philosophy
- **Subtle**: Should not dominate the status bar
- **Contextual**: Only appears when relevant (sessions exist)
- **Informative**: Shows count, not just presence
- **Non-intrusive**: No animation, no color change

### Placement
Current status bar:
```
Open: 42  Ready: 18  в–јP  [Filter: all]
```

With session indicator (when applicable):
```
Open: 42  Ready: 18  в–јP  рҹ“Һ3  [Filter: all]
                         ^^^^
```

The `рҹ“Һ3` means "3 related sessions available."

## Implementation

### Conditional Rendering
```go
func (m Model) renderStatusBar() string {
    // ... existing status items ...
    
    // Only show if cass is healthy AND selected bead has sessions
    if m.cassStatus == cass.StatusHealthy {
        if hint := m.cassCorrelations[m.selectedBeadID()]; hint \!= nil {
            if hint.SessionCount > 0 {
                sessionIndicator = fmt.Sprintf("рҹ“Һ%d", hint.SessionCount)
            }
        }
    }
    
    // ... rest of status bar ...
}
```

### Indicator Styling
- Use paperclip emoji (рҹ“Һ) for visual association with "attachments"
- Number shows count (1-9, then "9+")
- No special color (blends with status bar)
- Positioned after priority indicator, before filter

### Alternative Indicators Considered
| Option | Pros | Cons |
|--------|------|------|
| рҹ“Һ3 | Clear meaning, compact | Might not render on all terminals |
| [3 sessions] | Very clear | Too verbose |
| S:3 | Minimal | Unclear meaning |
| рҹ”Қ3 | Search connotation | Overloaded meaning |

Decision: Use рҹ“Һ with fallback to "S:" if emoji rendering fails.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shows only when cass healthy
- [ ] Shows only when current bead has sessions
- [ ] Disappears when selecting bead without sessions
- [ ] Count is accurate
- [ ] Does not break status bar layout
- [ ] Works with/without other status indicators

## Edge Cases
- Very wide status bar (ultra-wide terminal)
- Very narrow terminal (indicator may need to hide)
- Session count > 99 (show "99+")
- No bead selected (no indicator)

## Testing Strategy
- Verify indicator appears/disappears correctly
- Test with various session counts
- Test status bar width calculations
- Visual regression test
close_reason:
Implemented Status Bar Session Indicator:

**Changes:**
- Added GetCached() method to Correlator for non-blocking cache lookup
- Added getCassSessionCount() helper to Model
- Added session indicator badge (рҹ“ҺN) in footer between alerts and workspace sections
- Shows only when cass is healthy AND selected bead has cached sessions
- Count shows 1-9 or 9+ for larger numbers

**Files modified:**
- pkg/cass/correlation.go - Added GetCached() method
- pkg/cass/correlation_test.go - Added tests for GetCached
- pkg/ui/model.go - Added getCassSessionCount() and session badge in renderFooter()

**Testing:**
- New tests for GetCached (3 subtests: hit, miss, nil_cache)
- Full test suite passes with -race flag
- staticcheck clean

This indicator only shows when user presses V on a bead (which populates the cache). Future enhancement could add async correlation on selection change.

title: Board: Inline Card Expansion
description:
## Overview
Allow expanding a SINGLE card in-place to peek at full details without opening the side panel.

## Use Case
You're scanning the board and want to quickly check one card's description. You don't want to:
1. Open the side detail panel (consumes space, changes layout)
2. Press Enter to leave the board entirely

## How It Differs from Other Features

| Feature | Purpose | Interaction |
|---------|---------|-------------|
| Compact cards | Scannable overview | Always visible |
| **Inline expansion** | Quick peek at ONE card | `d` toggles, auto-closes on nav |
| Side detail panel | Persistent detail while browsing | `Tab` toggles, stays open |

## Interaction

### Normal State
```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ рҹҗӣ P1 bt-123      3d    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Fix auth timeout...     в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ рҹҡ«вҶҗ456 вҡЎвҶ’2 api,backend в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

### Expanded State (after pressing 'd')
```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ рҹҗӣ P1 bt-123                        3d  в–ј в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Fix auth timeout when refresh fails       в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ The token refresh logic fails to handle   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ the case where the auth server returns    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ a 503 during high load periods.           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ **Blocked by:**                           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   вҖў bt-456: API redesign (in_progress)    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ **Blocks:**                               в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   вҖў bt-789: Token refresh                 в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   вҖў bt-234: Session handling              в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ рҹҸ· api, backend, auth | Updated: 3d ago   в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

## Behavior

### Toggle
- `d`: Expand selected card / collapse if already expanded
- Only ONE card can be expanded at a time
- Expanding a different card closes the previous

### Auto-Collapse
- Moving selection (j/k) collapses the expanded card
- This keeps the expansion as a "quick peek" not persistent view
- For persistent detail, use Tab for side panel

### Content
- Full description (first ~10 lines, scrollable if longer)
- Dependencies with titles
- Full label list
- Markdown rendered via Glamour

### Layout
- Expanded card takes extra vertical space
- Other cards in column shift down
- Column scrolls to keep expanded card visible

## Removed from Scope
- ~~`D` to expand all cards~~ - Doesn't make sense. For density control, we could add a separate "compact/comfortable" toggle in the future.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `d` toggles expansion for selected card
- [ ] Only one card expanded at a time
- [ ] Auto-collapses on navigation
- [ ] Shows markdown-rendered description
- [ ] Shows dependencies with titles
- [ ] Column scrolls to show full expanded card
close_reason: Closed

title: Board: Smart Empty Column Handling
description:
## Overview
Empty columns should not waste horizontal space.

## Current Behavior
Empty columns take the same width as columns with items.
```
OPEN(15)     PROGRESS(8)  BLOCKED(0)   CLOSED(50)
в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ     в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ     (empty)      в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ
в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ     в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ                  в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ
```
The empty BLOCKED column wastes ~25% of width.

## Solution: Two Modes (Simple)

### Mode 1: Show All (Default for Status swimlane)
Keep all columns visible, even if empty. Users expect to see the full workflow.
Empty columns show as minimal-width placeholders.

### Mode 2: Hide Empty (Default for other swimlanes)
For Priority/Type groupings, hide columns with no items.
```
P0(3)          P1(8)           P2(12)
в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ   в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ    в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ
```
(P3 and P4 columns hidden because they have no items)

## Implementation

### Automatic Behavior
- **Status swimlane**: Always show all 4 columns (even if empty)
- **Priority swimlane**: Hide empty priority columns
- **Type swimlane**: Hide empty type columns

### Manual Toggle
`e`: Toggle empty column visibility (override auto behavior)

### Visual Indicator
When columns are hidden:
```
BOARD [by: Priority] [+2 hidden]
```

### Navigation
When navigating with `h/l`:
- If empty columns are hidden, skip them
- If empty columns are shown (minimal), allow navigating into them

## Removed from Scope
- ~~Three modes (collapse/minimize/show)~~ - Too complex. Two modes is sufficient.
- ~~Auto-expand on content~~ - Confusing behavior. Keep it simple.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Status view always shows all 4 columns
- [ ] Priority/Type views hide empty columns by default
- [ ] `e` toggles empty column visibility
- [ ] Hidden column count shown in header
- [ ] Navigation skips hidden columns
close_reason: Implemented smart empty column handling: Status mode shows all columns, Priority/Type hide empty. 'e' key toggles visibility. Title bar shows swimlane mode and hidden count. Commit b827f64.

title: History: Temporal Causality View
description:
## Overview
Show the CAUSAL FLOW of how a bead evolved, not just a timeline.

## Why Agents Need This
Understanding causality helps agents:
- See what TRIGGERED state changes
- Understand why delays happened
- Identify the critical path through work

## Implementation

### Event Causality Graph
Instead of: 'event1, event2, event3...'
Show: 'event1 CAUSED event2, which ENABLED event3...'

```go
type CausalEvent struct {
    Type      string    // created, commit, status_change, blocked, unblocked
    Timestamp time.Time
    CausedBy  *CausalEvent  // what triggered this
    Enables   []*CausalEvent // what this enables
    Duration  time.Duration  // time until next event
}
```

### Display
```
вҸі Causality View: bt-123

   created в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–ә claimed (2h later)
       в”Ӯ                        в”Ӯ
       в”Ӯ                        в–ј
       в”Ӯ                   commit_1 в”Җв”Җв”Җв–ә commit_2
       в”Ӯ                                    в”Ӯ
       в”Ӯ                                    в–ј
       в”Ӯ                            blocked_by:bt-456
       в”Ӯ                                    в”Ӯ
       в”Ӯ                               (6 day wait)
       в”Ӯ                                    в”Ӯ
       в”Ӯ                                    в–ј
       в”Ӯ                              unblocked
       в”Ӯ                                    в”Ӯ
       в”Ӯ                                    в–ј
       в”Ӯ                   commit_3 в”Җв”Җв”Җв–ә commit_4 в”Җв”Җв”Җв–ә closed
       в”Ӯ                                                  в”Ӯ
       в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                        Total: 8 days (6 days blocked)
```

### Insights Derived
- 'This bead spent 75% of its time blocked'
- 'Critical path: create вҶ’ block вҶ’ unblock вҶ’ close'
- 'Without the block, estimated 2 days'

### Robot Command
`bv robot causality bt-123` вҶ’ JSON graph of causal relationships

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Events linked by causality, not just time
- [ ] Blocked time clearly highlighted
- [ ] Critical path identified
- [ ] Useful for agents understanding delays
close_reason: Closed

title: History: File-Centric Drill-Down
description:
## Overview
Allow drilling into history by file path - see which beads and commits touched a specific file.

## Use Case
Developer wants to understand the history of a specific file:
- Which beads involved changes to this file?
- What commits modified it?
- Who authored changes to it?

## Data Available
```go
type CorrelatedCommit struct {

title: Perf (optional): reduce Phase2 map copy allocations in hot paths
description:
## Idea
`GraphStats.PageRank()` / `Betweenness()` etc return copies for thread safety.

For very large graphs and repeated scoring passes, these copies can be a meaningful allocation cost.

## Potential approach
Provide a callback-based accessor holding an RLock that exposes internal maps read-only.

## Risk
Must prevent mutation and keep lock holds short.
close_reason: Not worth the complexity - map copies are small and infrequent

title: Support BEADS_DIR Environment Variable for Custom Directory Location
description:
## Feature Request

Allow users to specify a custom beads directory location via the BEADS_DIR environment variable. When set, it overrides the default `.beads` directory lookup.

## Use Cases

1. **Monorepos**: Single beads directory shared across multiple packages
2. **Non-standard layouts**: Projects where `.beads` isn't in the working directory
3. **Testing**: Point to test fixtures without changing directory
4. **Cross-directory access**: View beads from anywhere on the filesystem

## User Request

Suggested by @burke in GitHub PR #15:
> Allow users to specify a custom beads directory location via the BEADS_DIR environment variable. When set, it overrides the default .beads directory lookup. This enables more flexible project layouts and cross-directory beads file access.

## Proposed Implementation

### New function in loader.go

```go
// GetBeadsDir returns the beads directory path, respecting BEADS_DIR env var.
// If BEADS_DIR is set, it is used directly.
// Otherwise, falls back to .beads in the given repoPath (or cwd if empty).
func GetBeadsDir(repoPath string) (string, error) {
    // Check BEADS_DIR environment variable first
    if envDir := os.Getenv("BEADS_DIR"); envDir != "" {
        return envDir, nil
    }

    // Fall back to .beads in repo path
    if repoPath == "" {
        var err error
        repoPath, err = os.Getwd()
        if err != nil {
            return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get cwd: %w", err)
        }
    }

    return filepath.Join(repoPath, ".beads"), nil
}
```

### Update LoadIssues to use GetBeadsDir

```go
func LoadIssues(repoPath string) ([]model.Issue, error) {
    beadsDir, err := GetBeadsDir(repoPath)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    // ... rest of function
}
```

### Update main.go for beadsPath

```go
// Get beads file path for live reload (respects BEADS_DIR)
beadsDir, _ := loader.GetBeadsDir("")
beadsPath, _ = loader.FindJSONLPath(beadsDir)
```

## Design Considerations

1. **Precedence**: BEADS_DIR should always win when set (explicit user intent)
2. **Validation**: Should we validate the directory exists? Or let it fail naturally?
3. **CLI flag alternative**: Could also add `--beads-dir` flag for one-off use
4. **Documentation**: Add to README and help output

## Priority Justification

P3 (Low) because:
- Not a bug, purely enhancement
- Workarounds exist (cd to directory, symlinks)
- Useful for power users but not essential
- Should wait until data loading bugs (bv-mvvu) are fixed first

## Dependencies

**Blocked by:** bv-mvvu (Data Integrity: Merge Artifact Files)
- Should implement after file selection logic is corrected
- Both touch loader.go and file resolution

## Test Plan

1. Test: BEADS_DIR set - uses that directory
2. Test: BEADS_DIR overrides repoPath parameter
3. Test: BEADS_DIR unset - falls back to .beads in cwd
4. Test: BEADS_DIR points to non-existent dir - appropriate error

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] BEADS_DIR environment variable respected
- [ ] Overrides default .beads directory lookup
- [ ] Error handling for invalid directories
- [ ] Tests cover all scenarios
- [ ] Documentation updated

## References

- GitHub PR #15: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer/pull/15
- Related: bv-mvvu (should be fixed first)
close_reason: Implemented BEADS_DIR environment variable support with tests and documentation

description:
# Document Static Pages Feature in robot-help and README

## Context
New features need documentation so users and AI agents can discover and use them.

## Requirements

### robot-help Output (cmd/bv/main.go)
Add to --robot-help output:
```
  --export-pages <dir>
      Export static HTML site to directory.
      Creates a self-contained bundle viewable in any browser.
      Output: index.html, data/*.json, viewer assets
      Example: bv --export-pages ./bv-pages
      
  --pages
      Launch interactive Pages deployment wizard.
      Guides you through export вҶ’ preview вҶ’ deploy to GitHub/Cloudflare.
      
  --preview-pages <dir>
      Start local server to preview existing export.
      Opens http://localhost:9000 in your browser.
      Press 'r' to regenerate, 'd' to deploy, 'q' to quit.
      
  --pages-title <title>
      Custom title for the static site (default: "Project Issues")
      
  --pages-include-closed
      Include closed issues in export (default: open only)
```

### README.md Section
Add section to existing README:
```markdown
## Static Site Export

Generate a beautiful static website from your beads data and deploy to GitHub Pages:

### Quick Start
```bash
# Interactive wizard (recommended)
bv --pages

# Or export locally
bv --export-pages ./my-dashboard
open ./my-dashboard/index.html
```

### Features
- **Dashboard**: Key metrics, recommendations, quick wins
- **Issues List**: Filter by status, type, priority, labels
- **Issue Detail**: Full description, dependencies, graph visualization
- **Insights**: PageRank, bottlenecks, critical paths
- **Works Offline**: Everything runs in the browser

### Deployment Options
1. **GitHub Pages** (recommended)
   - Free hosting
   - Automatic HTTPS
   - Wizard handles everything
   
2. **Cloudflare Pages**
   - Global CDN
   - Faster load times
   - `bv --pages` вҶ’ select Cloudflare
   
3. **Local/Custom**
   - Export with `--export-pages`
   - Host anywhere (Netlify, S3, etc.)

### Updating a Deployment
```bash
# Re-export and push
bv --export-pages ./existing-bundle
cd ./existing-bundle
git add . && git commit -m "Update dashboard" && git push
```

### Customization
- `--pages-title "My Project"` - Custom title
- `--pages-include-closed` - Show all issues
- Edit generated CSS for branding
```

### AGENTS.md Addition
```markdown
### Static Site for Stakeholder Reporting

Generate a static dashboard for non-technical stakeholders:
```bash
bv --export-pages ./dashboard --pages-title "Sprint 42 Status"
```

The output is a self-contained HTML/JS bundle that:
- Shows triage recommendations (from --robot-triage)
- Visualizes dependencies
- Requires no installation to view
- Can be deployed to GitHub Pages via `bv --pages`
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --robot-help includes all new flags
- [ ] README has complete usage section
- [ ] AGENTS.md updated for AI context
- [ ] Examples are copy-pasteable
- [ ] Covers common use cases
- [ ] Links to any additional docs
close_reason: Added static pages documentation to robot-help output and AGENTS.md. Covers all flags (--pages, --export-pages, --preview-pages, etc.) with examples.

title: Support incremental history updates
description:
# Incremental History Updates

## Context
When only a few new commits exist since last cache, we shouldn't re-parse the entire history. Parse only new commits and merge.

## Algorithm
1. Find new commits since cached report (using LatestCommitSHA)
2. If few new commits (<100), parse only those
3. Merge new events with existing correlations
4. If too many new commits, full refresh is more efficient

## Track Latest Commit
Add to HistoryReport: LatestCommitSHA string

## Merge Logic
- New beads: Add to correlations
- Updated beads: Merge events, re-correlate if needed
- Existing correlations: Keep if still valid

## File Location
\`pkg/correlation/incremental.go\`

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Detects when incremental update is possible
2. Only parses new commits
3. Correctly merges with existing data
4. Falls back to full refresh when appropriate
5. Performance improvement measurable
close_reason: Implemented IncrementalCorrelator with LatestCommitSHA tracking, merge logic, and 100-commit threshold for fallback

title: Optimize for large repositories
description:
# Large Repository Optimization

## Context
For repos with 10,000+ commits, naive parsing is too slow. Need smarter strategies.

## Strategies

### 1. Default Commit Limit
\`\`\`go
const DefaultHistoryLimit = 500
\`\`\`

### 2. Streaming Parser
Don't load all git output into memory. Stream and parse line-by-line.

### 3. Parallel File Stats
Batch git show requests for multiple SHAs.

### 4. Skip Old Beads
\`--history-closed-since "90 days"\` - Only beads closed in last 90 days.

### 5. Progress Indicator
For TUI, show progress while loading:
\`\`\`
Loading history... 234/500 commits
\`\`\`

## Benchmarking Targets
- 100 commits: instant (<100ms)
- 1,000 commits: fast (<1s)
- 10,000 commits: acceptable (<5s)

## File Location
- \`pkg/correlation/stream.go\` (streaming parser)
- \`pkg/correlation/batch.go\` (batched git calls)

## Acceptance Criteria
1. 500 commit default limit
2. Streaming parser doesn't OOM
3. Batch file stats is faster than sequential
4. Progress indicator in TUI
5. Meets benchmark targets
close_reason: Implemented streaming parser with 500 default limit, batch file stats extractor, progress callback, and ClosedSince option

title: Implement caching layer for history data
description:
# History Caching Layer

## Context
Parsing git history is expensive. We need to cache results to avoid re-parsing on every access.

## Cache Key
\`\`\`go
type CacheKey struct {
    HeadSHA       string  // Current git HEAD
    BeadsHash     string  // Hash of beads.jsonl content
    Options       string  // Serialized filter options
}
\`\`\`

## Cache Structure
\`\`\`go
type HistoryCache struct {
    mu      sync.RWMutex
    entries map[string]*CacheEntry
    maxAge  time.Duration
    maxSize int  // Max entries
}
\`\`\`

## Invalidation Strategy
Cache is invalid when:
- git HEAD changed (new commits)
- beads.jsonl content changed
- Cache entry older than maxAge (default: 5 minutes)

## Memory Management
- Default max entries: 10
- LRU eviction when full

## File Location
\`pkg/correlation/cache.go\`

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Cache hit returns data without git calls
2. Cache invalidates on HEAD change
3. Cache invalidates on beads.jsonl change
4. LRU eviction works correctly
5. Thread-safe for concurrent access
6. Significant speedup on repeated access
close_reason: Implemented HistoryCache with LRU eviction, thread-safe access, auto-invalidation on HEAD/beads change, and CachedCorrelator wrapper with hit/miss stats

title: Lightweight vs full robot-insights modes

Deliverables: --robot-insights mode flag (summary|full) or similar; summary returns top lists + status, full includes metric maps; warn/estimate payload when full on XL graphs; honor size-based config; document behavior.

title: Impact Radar & Drift Alerts
description:
Detect significant changes in graph metrics and alert when structure degrades.

## Background & Motivation
Graph metrics (PageRank, betweenness, cycles) can degrade over time without anyone noticing. A new cycle might be introduced, or a bottleneck might emerge. Proactive detection prevents issues from compounding.

## Value Proposition
- For Humans: Early warning when project health degrades
- For AI Agents: Validate changes before committing - 'did I introduce a cycle?'

## Technical Approach
1. Baseline storage (snapshot of metrics at a point in time)
2. Drift calculator (compare current to baseline)
3. Threshold configuration (when to alert)
4. CLI: --check-drift for CI integration

## Alert Types
- New cycles detected (critical)
- Significant PageRank changes (potential new bottleneck)
- Betweenness spike (new critical path node)
- Density increase (complexity growing)

## Success Criteria
- CI can run --check-drift and fail on regressions
- Clear exit codes: 0=OK, 1=critical, 2=warning
- Human-readable and JSON output available

title: Semantic Vector Search
description:
Add natural language search via vector embeddings, enabling queries like 'memory leak issues' to find relevant results even without exact keyword matches.

## Background & Motivation
Current search is regex-based, requiring exact matches. Users want to search by meaning, not just keywords. AI agents benefit from semantic retrieval for RAG patterns.

## Value Proposition
- For Humans: 'that thing about memory leaks' actually finds it
- For AI Agents: RAG over project history - 'how did we solve similar issues before?'

## Technical Approach
1. Local embeddings (start simple: sentence-transformers via subprocess or pure Go)
2. Vector storage (sqlite-vec extension or simple flat file)
3. Incremental indexing (embed on first access, update when issues change)
4. Semantic search mode in CLI and TUI

## Scope Control
Start minimal:
- Embed title + description only
- Single embedding model
- Top-10 results
- No hybrid search initially

## Dependencies & Risks
- Embedding model choice affects quality and size
- May require external tool (Python) initially
- Could start with Go-native solution later
close_reason: All subtasks (bv-9gf.1-.3) complete: embedding approach + vector index + CLI/TUI integration.

title: Cross-project blocker detection: suffix match with asymmetric evidence (bug chain, not paired work)
description:
## Why

During bt-gkyn Phase 1 triage, 22 orphaned pairs split 12 intentional /
10 collisions. Of the 10 collisions, one (`4yn4`) wasn't actually a
collision вҖ” bt-4yn4 ("CASS session correlator finds no results") and
cass-4yn4 ("Search tokenization fails on hyphenated IDs") are the same
bug from two sides. That's a cross-project **blocker** relationship, not
a symmetric pair.

Today there's no tool surface for detecting these. A smarter follow-up to
`--orphaned` could surface both paired-work candidates AND cross-project
blocker candidates вҖ” the latter using asymmetric cues (e.g., dep type
patterns, issue_type, status chain, title overlap).

## Proposed change

Either:

- Extend `--orphaned` to also emit `blocks_suggestions` for pairs where
  the evidence says "blocker" not "paired work" (e.g. same-suffix in
  closely-coupled projects plus title keyword overlap), OR
- A separate `bt robot cross-deps --suggest` subcommand that scans the
  full corpus for cross-project blocker candidates independent of suffix
  match.

The separate subcommand is probably cleaner вҖ” `--orphaned` has a tight
pairs-only scope.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Design decision: extend --orphaned vs new subcommand
- [ ] Heuristic spec: what signals suggest "blocker" vs "paired work"
      (suffix match + title overlap + same bug keywords? Different enough
      description? A user-tunable threshold?)
- [ ] Reader is suggestion-only; no writes
- [ ] Labeled-corpus evaluation (same rubric as bt-gkyn Phase 5) to
      measure precision
- [ ] Consider upstream beads relationship when a `paired_with` column
      lands вҖ” blocker detection could integrate then

## Priority rationale

P4 (backlog) because:
- Phase 1 of bt-gkyn shipped without this
- Only 1 false-collision observed in the 22-pair triage (5% rate)
- Manual judgment during triage caught it
- Requires design work before implementation

title: History: View Mode Toggle Animation
description:
## Overview
Smooth transition when switching between git-centric and bead-centric views.

## Current
Instant switch with no visual feedback.

## Enhancement

### Transition Effect
- Brief fade or slide animation (subtle, not gimmicky)
- Use lipgloss transitions if available
- Fallback: instant switch with clear visual indicator

### Mode Indicator
- Persistent mode indicator in corner
- Git icon (в—ү) for git-centric
- Bead icon (в—Ҳ) for bead-centric

title: Toggleable Keyboard Shortcuts Sidebar
description:
## Feature Request

Add a toggleable sidebar panel that displays keyboard shortcuts while using the application. Unlike the current help overlay (which is modal and blocks interaction), this would be a persistent panel that can be shown/hidden while continuing to work.

## User Request

From @austinm911 in GitHub Issue #8:
> Not sure if anyone would find this helpful but there are a lot of shortcuts here so I am always using ? to view them (unable to scroll this view on Warp terminal). It would be nice to have a keyboard shortcuts panel that could be toggled on/off so we can see the rest of the panels.

## Why This Matters

bv has 60+ keyboard shortcuts across multiple contexts. Learning these shortcuts improves efficiency, but the current modal help overlay:
1. Blocks the main view while open
2. Cannot be scrolled on some terminals (separate bug: bv-43js)
3. Must be reopened each time you forget a shortcut

## Proposed Approaches

### Option A: Persistent Sidebar (Recommended)

A right-side panel that can be toggled on/off. Toggle with a key like F2 or Shift+?. Shows shortcuts organized by category, scrollable within the sidebar.

### Option B: Context-Sensitive Status Bar

Show relevant shortcuts in the footer based on current view/focus. Less intrusive but shows fewer shortcuts.

### Option C: Compact Floating Panel

A smaller overlay that does not cover the entire screen.

## Design Considerations

1. Width impact: Sidebar reduces main content width
2. Context awareness: Show shortcuts relevant to current view/focus
3. Persistence: Remember toggle state across sessions?
4. Customization: Allow users to choose which shortcuts to show?

## Priority Justification

P4 (Backlog) because:
- Nice-to-have, not critical functionality
- Help overlay serves the basic need (once scroll bug is fixed)
- Requires significant UI work
- Should implement after help overlay scroll (bv-43js)

## Dependencies

Blocked by: bv-43js (Help Overlay Scroll)
- Fix the existing help system first
- Learn from help scroll implementation patterns
- Sidebar might reuse scroll logic

## Test Plan

1. Toggle sidebar on/off with key
2. Layout adjusts correctly with sidebar
3. Shortcuts update based on context
4. Works in all views

## Acceptance Criteria

- Toggleable sidebar or alternative implementation
- Shows relevant keyboard shortcuts
- Does not block main interaction
- Scrollable if content exceeds height
- Adapts to terminal size
- Works across all views

## References

- GitHub Issue #8: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer/issues/8
- Related: bv-43js (help overlay scroll) - should fix first
close_reason: Implemented toggleable keyboard shortcuts sidebar with F2 toggle, context-aware sections, and Ctrl+j/k scrolling

value: Cross-project beads OS ownership model (bd-mh6 epic): beads (bd) = engine (CLI, server lifecycle, data model, gates/human/formulas). bt = dashboard (TUI, global mode, visual orchestration). marketplace (mkt) = OS/teaching layer (plugins, skills, hooks, beads-onboard, PRIME.md, conventions). bt owns the startup fix (bt-zsy8), TUI surfacing (bt-c69c), and is the global orchestration layer. Key paired beads: bd-0il/mkt-0il (feature discovery), bt-zsy8/bd-zsy8 (server lifecycle). See bd show bd-mh6 for full epic.
Refactor worker Monitor: 60s during active edits (breakage expected), 15-20s when errors start dropping (convergence). 30s generated 40+ noise notifications in Phase 1.

From multi-agent research: Anthropic's Agent Teams use shared task lists with dependency tracking (blockedBy) and peer-to-peer messaging between long-running instances. This is architecturally what beads + shared Dolt server already provides. Validates the approach. The 'split by context not by role' principle applies to how bt handles multi-project views.

Cross-platform test patterns discovered (session 15): (1) os.UserHomeDir on Windows ignores HOME env var - use USERPROFILE or configHome override. (2) pflag single-dash args get shorthand-matched: -robot-help becomes -r obot-help if -r shorthand exists. Always use double-dash. (3) Windows file locking: defer m.Stop() and t.Cleanup registration order matters (LIFO) for TempDir cleanup. (4) Golden files need \r\n normalization.

Two cross-project dependency patterns in beads: (1) Bare cross-prefix (bd dep add bt-xxx bd-yyy) for known issue IDs on same server, (2) external: + ship (bd dep add bt-xxx external:beads:capability) for capability contracts across teams. Upstream CLI has a bug where resolveIDWithRouting fails on bare cross-prefix before storage layer - requires external: workaround until fixed. bt graph builder in global mode needs to handle both.
Never use sed/regex for Go struct field renames. Use compiler-driven refactoring: rename field, go build, fix each error. sed overshoot hit FlowMatrixModel/InsightsModel during Phase 1.2.
Monitor should poll go test ./... (full suite) not just the package being refactored. Phase 1 monitor missed potential cmd/bt regressions by only watching pkg/ui.

Same gap as CASS (cass-rjm): bt has TUI and robot JSON but agents pipe through Python to format output. Robot mode should be agent-friendly without post-processing. The harness research finding applies: format for agent consumption, not human readability.

Background worker uses a one-at-a-time subscription chain via WaitForBackgroundWorkerMsgCmd. Each handler MUST re-subscribe or the chain dies silently - all poll loop updates, snapshots, and connection status stop. Worker messages are grouped in model.go Update switch with an invariant comment block. All must follow the return m.handleXxx pattern.

Full test suite (go test ./...) takes ~8.5 minutes as of 2026-04-08. The e2e package alone (tests/e2e) accounts for ~505s. Use 300s+ timeout or run in background. For quick checks, run affected packages directly.

gastownhall org is the ecosystem hub. gastownhall/beads is a fork of steveyegge/beads (steve's is still upstream). gastownhall/gascity replaces gastown as the orchestration SDK. Gas City likely uses port 3307. bt must not conflict with Gas City's Dolt server.

Wasteland visibility in bt is a planned feature - surface wanted items and reputation from wl CLI inside the TUI. Deferred but tracked. Shell out to wl binary, same pattern as using bd for writes. gastownhall/wasteland is the canonical repo.
